[C32183] Basal Body -- **Semantic type:** Cell Component  **Definition:** A centriole-containing organelle that serves as the base and nucleation site for microtubules that form cilia or flagella.  **Synonyms:** - Kinetosome
[C32184] Basal Cerebral Vein of Rosenthal -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A large blood vessel formed by the anterior and deep middle cerebral veins and small branches from the insula and cerebral peduncles which receives tributaries from the temporal horn and the medial temporal lobe and is joined by the inferior striate veins. This vessel may also receive blood from the lateral mesencephalic vein and empties into the vein of Galen.
[C32185] Basal Domain of the Epithelium -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The free part of the epithelium that faces the interior surface of the lumen of an organ or towards the interstitium.
[C32186] Basal Lamina -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A layer of extracellular matrix found beneath epithelial tissues. It is secreted by epithelial cells and comprised of proteoglycans, laminin and type IV collagen.  **Synonyms:** - Lamina Densa
[C32187] Basal Lamina Collagen -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A family of large fibrous proteins contained within the layer of extracellular matrix found beneath epithelial tissues.
[C32188] Basal Lamina of the Vascular Endothelium -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A layer of extracellular matrix found beneath the single pavement layer of cells which line the luminal surface of the entire vascular system.
[C32189] Basal Layer of the Endometrium -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A layer occurring in the endometrial lining of the uterus which is adjacent to the myometrium and below the functional layer.
[C3219] Maxillary Sinus Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the maxillary sinus.  Representative examples of benign neoplasms include Schneiderian papilloma and salivary gland-type adenoma.  Representative examples of malignant neoplasms include carcinoma and lymphoma.  **Synonyms:** - Maxillary Sinus Tumor - Neoplasm of Maxillary Sinus - Neoplasm of the Maxillary Sinus - Tumor of Maxillary Sinus - Tumor of the Maxillary Sinus
[C32190] Basaloid Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cell usually of the epidermis that resembles a basal cell.
[C32191] Base of the Brain -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The inferior surface of the brain as viewed from below.  **Synonyms:** - Base of Brain
[C32192] Base of the Lung -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The bottom concave portion of the lung that is situated on the convexity of the diaphragm.
[C32193] Base of the Pericardium -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The inferior surface of the conical membranous sac in which the heart and roots of the aorta and other large blood vessels are contained. This surface rests on the diaphragm and is fused with the diaphragm's central tendons.
[C32195] Basilar Artery Branch -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** Any artery arising from the basilar artery.
[C32196] Basilar Membrane of the Membranous Canal of the Cochlea -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The wall of the cochlear duct, which separates it from the scala tympani. It vibrates in response to sound, exciting the hair cells and supports the organ of Corti.
[C32197] Basilic Vein -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** One of the moderately large superficial veins of the forearm that transports blood from the hand and the medial aspect of the forearm to the axillary vein.  **Synonyms:** - BASILIC VEIN
[C32198] Basophilic Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cell whose cytoplasm or granules stain readily with basic dyes.
[C32199] Beta Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cell that composes the bulk of the islets of Langerhans and secretes insulin.  **Synonyms:** - Insulin Cell - Insulin Secreting Cell
[C322] Butylated Hydroxytoluene -- **Semantic type:** Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** An organic chemical composed of 4-methylphenol modified with tert-butyl groups at positions 2 and 6. Butylated hydroxytoluene (BHT) inhibits autoxidation of unsaturated organic compounds. BHT is used in food, cosmetics and industrial fluids to prevent oxidation and free radical formation.  **Synonyms:** - 2,6-Bis(1,1-dimethylethyl)-4-methylphenol - 2,6-Di-tert-butyl-1-hydroxy-4-methylbenzene - 2,6-Di-tert-butyl-4-cresol - 2,6-Di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol - BHT - BUTYLATED HYDROXYTOLUENE - Butylated hydroxytoluene - Butylhydroxytoluene - Dibutylhydroxytoluene

[C32201] Bicuspid Tooth -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** One of the two permanent teeth located in front of the molars and behind each cuspid. These teeth have two cusps (points) and are used to tear and grind food.  **Synonyms:** - Bicuspid - Premolar - Premolar Tooth - TOOTH, PREMOLAR
[C32202] Bile Canaliculus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A thin tube within the liver that collect bile secreted by hepatocytes. Many canaliculi come together to form bile ductules that ultimately form the common hepatic duct.
[C32203] Bile Salt -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A bile acid compounded with a cation, often sodium. The most common human bile salts are sodium glycocholate and sodium taurocholate.
[C32204] Birbeck Granule -- **Semantic type:** Cell Component  **Definition:** A rod shaped cytoplasmic organelle found in Langerhans cells of the epidermis.
[C32205] Bladder Mucosa -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The mucous membrane that is the inner lining of the urinary bladder.  **Synonyms:** - Urinary Bladder Mucosa - mucosa of urinary bladder
[C32206] Bladder Muscular Coat -- **Semantic type:** Tissue

[C3221] Mediastinal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the structures of the mediastinum. [C32210] Bladder Urothelial Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** An epithelial cell, found in the bladder, originally thought to represent a transitional form between stratified squamous and columnar epithelial cells.  In the contracted condition the epithelium consists of many cell layers, whereas in the stretched condition usually only two layers of cells can be distinguished.  **Synonyms:** - Bladder Transitional Cell

[C32213] Blood Vessel Tissue -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The tissue component of a blood vessel. Large vessels contain three layers of tissue, the tunica intima, tunica media, and tunica adventitia. Capillary tissue is composed of a single layer of endothelium and connective tissue.
[C32214] B-Microtubule -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** An incomplete or c-shaped microtubule that is part of a microtubule doublet in cilia.
[C32215] Body Cavity -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure
[C32216] Body of the Corpus Callosum -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** An area within the corpus callosum, a white matter structure within the cleft that separates the left and right cerebral hemispheres in the mammalian brain, between the genu (anterior region) and the splenium (posterior region).  **Synonyms:** - CORPUS CALLOSUM, BODY
[C32217] Body of the Epididymis -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The middle part of the epididymis, a crescent-like structure located in the upper and posterior surfaces of the testis, that extends downward from the head to the tail.

[C32219] Body of the Nail -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** Part of the horny plate, which is attached to the nail bed, and is the intervening portion between the cuticle and the free edge of the nail.
[C3222] Medulloblastoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A malignant, invasive embryonal neoplasm arising from the cerebellum or posterior fossa. It occurs predominantly in children and has the tendency to metastasize via the cerebrospinal fluid pathways. Signs and symptoms include truncal ataxia, disturbed gait, lethargy, headache, and vomiting. There are four histologic variants: classic medulloblastoma, large cell/anaplastic medulloblastoma, desmoplastic/nodular medulloblastoma, and medulloblastoma with extensive nodularity.  **Synonyms:** - CNS, medulloblastoma - MEDULLOBLASTOMA, MALIGNANT - Medulloblastomas - medulloblastoma
[C32220] Body of the Spermatozoon -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A part of a sperm cell, also known as the middle piece, between the head and the tail consisting of the neck and connection piece. This area contains a large number of mitochondria that produce energy to power the tail.
[C32221] Body Part -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** Any part of an organism.  **Synonyms:** - Body Structure
[C32222] Bone Matrix -- **Semantic type:** Tissue

[C32224] Bone Surface -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The exterior part of the bone.
[C32225] Bowman Capsule -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** A double-walled, cup-like sac that surrounds the glomerulus. It is located at the beginning of a nephron in the kidney.  **Synonyms:** - Bowman's Capsule - Malpighian Capsule
[C32226] Bowman's Membrane -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** A transparent homogeneous acellular layer, 6 to 9 um thick, lying between the basal lamina of the outer layer of stratified epithelium and the substantia propria of the cornea; it is considered to be a basement membrane.
[C32227] Brachial Artery Branch -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** Any artery arising from the brachial artery.
[C32228] Brain Infundibulum -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A funnel-shaped tube of grey matter and axons that connects the hypothalamus to the posterior pituitary. The infundibulum is open to the third ventricle to allow for the release of antidiuretic hormone and oxytocin.
[C3223] Meigs Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare syndrome affecting females. It is characterized by pleural effusion, ascites and non-malignant ovarian neoplasm. This syndrome usually follows a benign course. Prognosis is favorable following surgical resection of the ovarian mass.  **Synonyms:** - Meigs' Syndrome
[C32230] Bronchial Artery -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Two series of blood vessels that supply the lungs with nutrients. The right side bronchial arteries arise from the first aortic intercostal while the left side arises from thoracic aorta.
[C32231] Bronchial Epithelium -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The epithelium of the bronchus of the lung, which forms a pseudo-stratified ciliated layer composed of secretory and basal cells.

[C32233] Bronchial Tree -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** The left and right bronchi considered together with their branches.

[C32235] Brown Adipose Tissue -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** Brown-colored adipose tissue that is present in higher concentrations in newborns and hibernating animals. It contains numerous small droplets of lipids and high numbers of mitochondria as its primary function is to generate body heat.  **Synonyms:** - ADIPOSE TISSUE, BROWN - BAT - BAT - Brown Fat - Brown Fat
[C32236] Brush Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cell characterized by the presence of a tuft of blunt, squat microvilli (120-140/cell) on the cell surface. The microvilli contain filaments that stretch into the underlying cytoplasm. It has a distinctive pear shape with a wide base and a narrow microvillus apex. This cell is distinct from the unipolar brush cell found in the cerebellar cortex.
[C32237] Buccal Salivary Gland -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** A type of minor salivary gland located in the mucous membrane of the cheek.
[C32239] C1 Vertebra -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** The first of the seven cervical vertebrae.  **Synonyms:** - Atlas of the Vertebra - Bone structure of atlas (body structure) - C1 VERTEBRA
[C3224] Melanoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A malignant, usually aggressive tumor composed of atypical, neoplastic melanocytes. Most often, melanomas arise in the skin (cutaneous melanomas) and include the following histologic subtypes: superficial spreading melanoma, nodular melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.  **Synonyms:** - MELANOMA, MALIGNANT - Malignant Melanoma - Malignant Melanoma - Malignant Melanoma - Melanoma, NOS - melanoma
[C32240] C2 Vertebra -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** The second of the seven cervical vertebrae.  **Synonyms:** - Axis of the Vertebra - Bone structure of axis (body structure) - C2 VERTEBRA

[C32246] CA1 Field of the Cornu Ammonis -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The first of four fields in the cornu ammonis of the hippocampus which receives inputs from the perforant path originating in layer III of the entorhinal cortex. This area consists of pyramidal cells that send axons to layer V of the entorhinal cortex and the subiculum.
[C32247] CA2 Field of the Cornu Ammonis -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The second of four fields in the cornu ammonis of the hippocampus which receives inputs from the perforant path originating in layer II of the entorhinal cortex. This area is small, contains pyramidal cells, and does not contain mossy fiber connections from the dentate gyrus.
[C32248] CA3 Field of the Cornu Ammonis -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The third of four regions in the cornu ammonis of the hippocampus which receives inputs from mossy fibers originating from the granule cells in the dentate gyrus and from inputs along the perforant path originating in layers II and III within the entorhinal cortex. This area contains pyramidal cells that send axons to the CA1 field and acts as a pacemaker, setting the pace for neuronal firing in the hippocampus.
[C32249] CA4 Field of the Cornu Ammonis -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The last of four regions in the cornu ammonis of the hippocampus and is also part of the hilus of the dentate gyrus. This area contains mostly mossy cells that receive inputs from the dentate gyrus and pyramidal cells in the CA3 region and also projects back to the dentate gyrus.
[C3225] Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia Type 1 -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** Multiple endocrine neoplasia caused by inactivation of the tumor suppressor gene MEN-1. Patients may develop hyperparathyroidism and parathyroid gland adenomas, pituitary gland adenomas, and neuroendocrine tumors.  **Synonyms:** - MEA Type 1 - MEA Type I - MEN 1 - MEN Type 1 - MEN Type I - MEN1 syndrome - Multiple Endocrine Adenomatosis - Multiple Endocrine Adenomatosis Type 1 - Multiple Endocrine Adenomatosis Type I - Multiple Endocrine Adenomatosis, Type I - Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia Type I - Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia, Type I - Multiple endocrine neoplasia [MEN] type I - Wermer Syndrome - Wermer Syndrome - Wermer syndrome - Wermer's Syndrome - multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 syndrome

[C32283] Centriole -- **Semantic type:** Cell Component  **Definition:** One of two small cylindrical cell organelles composed of 9 triplet microtubules.
[C32284] Centromedian Nucleus of the Thalamus -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** One of a pair of nuclei of the intralaminar nucleus of the dorsal thalamus, which receives afferent fibers from the cerebral cortex, vestibular nuclei, globus pallidus, superior colliculus, reticular formation, and spinothalamic tract, and sends efferent fibers to the subthalamic nucleus and putamen.

[C3229] Meningeal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the meninges. [C32295] Cervical Curve -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The natural bend in the spine at the neck.
[C32296] Cervical Gland -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** The mucous secreting glands in the cervix.
[C32297] Cervical Gland Secretion -- **Semantic type:** Body Substance  **Definition:** The mucous produced by the glands in the cervix which is composed mainly of water but also contains electrolytes, amino acids, glucose, proteins and other trace elements (e.g. Mn, Fe, Cu, Zn, and Se).  **Synonyms:** - Cervical Mucus
[C32298] Cervical Lymph Node -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** Any of the lymph nodes located in the neck.  **Synonyms:** - CERVICAL LYMPH NODE - Cervical Nodes - Cervical lymph node structure (body structure) - Cervical lymph nodes - LYMPH NODE, CERVICAL - Lymph Node(s) Cervical - cervical lymph node
[C32299] Cervical Nerve -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Any of the eight nerves arising from one of the seven cervical vertebrae.
[C323] Butyrophenone -- **Semantic type:** Chemical Viewed Functionally  **Definition:** Synthetic agents containing the phenyl-1-butanone group, most Butyrophenones are neuroleptic (antipsychotic) drugs, such as haloperidol, droperidol, or azaperone, that control schizophrenic symptoms (hallucinations, delusions, dementia). Most antipsychotic agents interfere with neurotransmitter functions, usually blocking dopamine receptors, and induce behavioral, endocrine, motor-kinetic effects. (NCI04)  **Synonyms:** - 1-phenylbutan-1-one - BUTYROPHENONE
[C3230] Meningioma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A generally slow growing tumor attached to the dura mater. It is composed of neoplastic meningothelial (arachnoidal) cells. It typically occurs in adults, often women and it has a wide range of histopathological appearances. Of the various subtypes, meningothelial, fibrous and transitional meningiomas are the most common. Most meningiomas are WHO grade 1 tumors, and some are WHO grade 2 or 3 tumors. Most subtypes share a common clinical behavior, although some subtypes are more likely to recur and follow a more aggressive clinical course. (Adapted from WHO)  **Synonyms:** - Meningioma, NOS - Meningioma, NOS - Meningiomas - meningioma
[C32301] Chamber of the Eye -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** One of three spaces (anterior, posterior, and vitreous chamber) in the eye.
[C32302] Chemical Synapse -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A synapse in which an action potential causes the exocytosis of neurotransmitter from the presynaptic cell, which diffuses across the synaptic cleft and binds to ligand gated ion channels on the post synaptic cell. These ion channels then affect the resting potential of the post synaptic cell.
[C32303] Chemical Synapse Component -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A functional part or structural element of a chemical synapse.
[C32304] Chiasmatic Cistern -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The cavity surrounding the optic chiasm.
[C32305] Chief Cell of the Stomach -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** An epithelial cell of the stomach that secretes digestive enzymes. Chief cells may be found at any level in the fundic glands, but are most common in the deeper region, toward the muscularis mucosae. They are basophilic.  **Synonyms:** - Gastric Chief Cell - Gastric Chief Cells - Stomach Chief Cell
[C32306] Chondroblast -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cartilage-forming cell derived from a mesenchymal cell. The chondroblast secretes hyaluronidase, chondroitin sulfates, and collagen II to form a collagen matrix. It differentiates into a chondrocyte.
[C32307] Chordae Tendineae -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Tendons that connect the papillary muscles to the tricuspid valve and mitral valve.  **Synonyms:** - CHORDAE TENDINEAE - Chorda Tendinea
[C32308] Choroid Plexus of the Fourth Ventricle -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Two vascular fringes, one on each side of the lower part of the roof of the fourth ventricle where cerebrospinal fluid is produced.
[C32309] Choroid Plexus of the Lateral Ventricle -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A vascular fringe projecting from the choroidal fissure into each lateral ventricle where cerebrospinal fluid is produced.
[C3231] Menopause -- **Semantic type:** Organism Attribute  **Definition:** The permanent cessation of menses, usually defined by 6 to 12 months of amenorrhea in a woman over 45 years of age.  **Synonyms:** - Menopausal - menopause
[C32310] Choroidal Artery -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** One of two arteries (anterior and posterior choroidal artery) that supply blood to the choroid plexus, optic tract, hippocampus, globus pallidus, and other various brain regions.
[C32311] Choroidal Fissure -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A narrow groove along the medial wall of the lateral ventricle from which the choroid plexus attaches.
[C32312] Chromatin Fibril -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Synonyms:** - Chromatin Fiber
[C32313] Chromophobe Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A small, faintly staining cell with scanty cytoplasm and rounded or polygonal contours. It is found in clusters in the anterior lobe of the pituitary gland.
[C32314] Ciliary Epithelium -- **Semantic type:** Tissue
[C32315] Ciliary Muscle -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A ring of smooth muscle consisting of circular and radiating fibers within the middle layer of the eye. The ciliary muscle is innervated by both the parasympathetic and sympathetic nervous systems and acts to control the flow of aqueous humour into Schlemm's canal and changes the shape of the eye lens.
[C32316] Ciliary Process -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The 60 to 80 radiating pigmented ridges that form a frilled circle around the eye lens behind the iris.
[C32317] Ciliated Bronchial Epithelial Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A columnar-shaped cell found in the epithelium of the lobular bronchiole. Each cell contains 200-300 cilia 5-8 mm long. The cilia are part of the mucociliary system, which extends through the tracheobronchial tree and into the respiratory bronchioles. This system protects the respiratory surface from dirt and airborne infection and represents the principal mechanism of defense in the respiratory tract. The cilia beat in unison (about 1,000 strokes per minute) and in a wave-like fashion, thereby propelling mucus and entrapped foreign material toward the oropharynx for expectoration or swallowing.
[C32318] Cilium -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** An organelle that protrudes outward from the cell body. It consists of a specialized arrangement of 9 doublet microtubules in a ring with or without a core of 2 microtubules and is enclosed in a plasma membrane coat. Motile cilia contain the motor protein dynein.
[C32319] Cilium Microtubule -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A microtubule that is part of a cilium.
[C3232] Menstruation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The normal physiologic discharge through the vagina of blood and mucosal tissues from the nonpregnant uterus.  **Synonyms:** - Menses - Menses - Menstrual - menstrual period - menstruation
[C32320] Circular Ligament of the Tooth -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A ligament located in the lamina propria of the marginal gingiva that encircles the tooth.
[C32321] Circular Sulcus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A semicircular groove or fissure separating the insula from the opercula.
[C32322] Circumvallate Papilla -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** One of eight or ten projections from the dorsum of the tongue forming a row anterior to and parallel with the sulcus terminalis; each papilla is surrounded by a circular trench (fossa) having a slightly raised outer wall (vallum); on the sides of the vallate papilla and the opposed margin of the vallum are numerous taste buds.  **Synonyms:** - Vallate Papilla
[C32323] Cis-Golgi Network -- **Semantic type:** Cell Component  **Definition:** A network of membrane components where vesicles from the endoplasmic reticulum fuse with the Golgi apparatus.  **Synonyms:** - CGN
[C32324] Cisterna Magna -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The largest of the three principle cisterns in the subarachnoid space which is located between the cerebellum and the medulla oblongata.  **Synonyms:** - Cerebellomedullary Cistern

[C32338] Collagen Fiber -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** A structural protein and connective tissue component in fiber form composed of numerous fine fibrils, which provides strength and flexibility to tissue.
[C32339] Collagen Fibril -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** Part of the infrastructure of the collagen fiber. Individual collagen polypeptides assemble into trimers that that assemble into a fibril, which then form a fiber.  **Synonyms:** - Collagen Fiber

[C32343] Collecting Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A simple cuboidal epithelial cell lining the collecting duct of the kidney. Its function is dependent upon its location on the collecting duct.
[C32344] Collecting Tube -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The final section of a renal tubule in the nephron between the distal convoluted tubule and the ureter comprising of principal and intercalated cells. The collecting tube acts to reabsorb sodium and water while secreting potassium and maintaining acid-base homeostasis.  **Synonyms:** - Straight Tube - collecting duct - renal collecting tubule

[C32348] Human Colostrum -- **Semantic type:** Body Substance  **Definition:** A thick, protein-rich substance secreted by the breasts during the second half of pregnancy and for a few days after birth before the breast milk comes in. It contains antibodies which serve to give the baby protection from disease and also has a laxative effect on the colon which helps to expel the plug of meconium and facilitate the establishment of normal bowel movements.  **Synonyms:** - COLOSTRUM - HUMAN COLOSTRUM
[C32349] Commissure -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A bundle of nerve fibers crossing from one side to the other side of the brain or spinal cord.

[C32351] Common Canaliculus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A lacrimal duct found in the majority of the population, which is formed by the union of the superior and inferior lacrimal canaliculi, and which connects to the lacrimal sac. In a small percentage of people the common duct is absent, and the superior and inferior lacrimal canaliculi enter the lacrimal sac separately.

Clinical variants include non-secretory myeloma, smoldering myeloma, indolent myeloma, and plasma cell leukemia. (WHO, 2001)  **Synonyms:** - Kahler disease - MYELOMA, PLASMA CELL, MALIGNANT - Multiple Myeloma/Plasma Cell Myeloma - Multiple Myeloma/Plasma Cell Myeloma - Multiple myeloma - Myeloma - Myeloma - Myeloma, NOS - Myeloma, NOS - Myelomatosis - Plasma Cell Myeloma - Plasma Cell Myeloma - Plasma Cell Myeloma - Plasma Cell Myeloma - Plasma Cell Myeloma - Plasma Cell Myeloma - Plasma Cell Myeloma/Multiple Myeloma - Plasma cell myeloma - multiple myeloma - myeloma - myelomatosis - plasma cell myeloma

[C32429] Decidual Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** An endometrial fibroblast that differentiates during pregnancy in response to the implanting embryo by accumulating lipid and glycogen. It is polygonal, possesses a large, vesicular nucleus and is believed to secrete placental prolactin. The decidual cells form a tightly adherent, massive cellular matrix that first surrounds the implanting embryo and later occupies most of the endometrium.

[C325] CA-125 Antigen -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Immunologic Factor|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** An antigen expressed by tissues derived from mullerian epithelium (tubal, endometrial and endocervical) or celomic epithelium (mesothelial cells of the pleura, pericardium and peritoneum).  Although its biochemical nature has not been clearly defined, CA125 has been described as a glycosylated, high-molecular-weight protein that may be released from damaged cells.  Measurable in plasma, CA125 is a sensitive, nonspecific tumor marker for ovarian cancer; as such it is much more useful for monitoring clinical responses to the treatment of ovarian cancer than for the diagnosis of ovarian cancer.  Elevated plasma CA125 may occur in association with other malignancies and non-malignant gynecologic disorders. (NCI04)  **Synonyms:** - CA-125 - CA-125 - CA-125 - CA-125 - CA125 - Cancer Antigen 125 - Carbohydrate Antigen 125 - cancer antigen 125

[C32504] Embryonic Connective Tissue -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** One of two types of loose tissue formed during early embryonic development: mesenchyme and mucoid. [C3253] Myositis Ossificans -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A self-limited benign neoplasm characterized by a zonal architecture. It is composed of spindle cells oriented randomly or in short intersecting fascicles. Peripherally, the spindle cells merge with osteoblasts that rim and populate ill-defined trabeculae and sheets of unmineralized woven bone, which is surrounded by well-formed trabecular and cortical-type bone that remodels into lamellar bone. Treatment is usually simple excision. Prognosis is excellent; recurrence is uncommon. (WHO 2020)
[C32530] Epithelial Cell Junction -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A region of cell-cell contact between adjacent cells in an epithelial layer.

Many complex O-linked oligosaccharides are presumably added by glycosyltransferases.  Expression is increased in the lactating breast and in breast, ovary, lung, and prostate malignancies. [C3273] Neurofibromatosis Type 1 -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** The most common type of neurofibromatosis.  It is characterized clinically by cutaneous and subcutaneous tumors with patches of hyperpigmentation. The hyperpigmented skin areas, are present from birth and found anywhere on the body surface.  They can vary markedly in size and color.  The dark brown areas are called cafe-au-lait spots. The multiple cutaneous and subcutaneous tumors are nerve sheath tumors, called neurofibromas.  They can develop anywhere along the peripheral nerve fibers. Neurofibromas can become quite large, causing a major disfigurement, eroding bone, and compressing various peripheral nerve structures.  Type 1 neurofibromatosis has dominant inheritance, with a gene locus on the proximal long arm of chromosome 17.  **Synonyms:** - NF1 - NF1 - NF1 - Neurofibromatosis - Neurofibromatosis 1 - Neurofibromatosis Type 1 Microdeletion Syndrome - Peripheral Neurofibromatosis - Recklinghausen disease - Von Recklinghausen Disease - Von Recklinghausen Disease - Von Recklinghausen disease - neurofibromatosis type 1

[C32766] Immature B-Lymphocyte -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A B-lymphocyte derived from a Pre B lymphocyte. It develops in bone marrow then exit via the central sinus. It goes through the vascular system to the spleen. It does not proliferate and differentiate in response to antigen, but instead responds to antigen by negative selection resulting in subsequent immune system tolerance to that antigen.
[C32767] Immature Bone -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The earliest tissue to appear in embryonic development (it is the predominant bone in the developing fetus) and post fracture. The collagenous lamellae are randomly oriented and loosely intertwined. It is characterized by coarse fiber bundles approximately 30 um in diameter.  **Synonyms:** - Bundle Bone - Nonlamellar Bone - Woven Bone

[C32770] Incus -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** One of the three bones comprising the middle ear. This anvil-shaped bone is positioned between the malleus and the stapes.  **Synonyms:** - Bone structure of incus (body structure) - INCUS

[C328] Caffeine -- **Semantic type:** Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A methylxanthine alkaloid found in the seeds, nuts, or leaves of a number of plants native to South America and East Asia that is structurally related to adenosine and acts primarily as an adenosine receptor antagonist with psychotropic and anti-inflammatory activities. Upon ingestion, caffeine binds to adenosine receptors in the central nervous system (CNS), which inhibits adenosine binding. This inhibits the adenosine-mediated downregulation of CNS activity; thus, stimulating the activity of the medullary, vagal, vasomotor, and respiratory centers in the brain. This agent also promotes neurotransmitter release that further stimulates the CNS. The anti-inflammatory effects of caffeine are due the nonselective competitive inhibition of phosphodiesterases (PDEs). Inhibition of PDEs raises the intracellular concentration of cyclic AMP (cAMP), activates protein kinase A, and inhibits leukotriene synthesis, which leads to reduced inflammation and innate immunity.  **Synonyms:** - 1,3,7-trimethylxanthine - 3,7-Dihydro-1,3,7-trimethyl-1H-purine-2,6-dione - CAF - CAFFEINE - CAFFEINE - Methyltheobromine - Theine - Trimethylxanthine - caffeine
[C3280] Nodule -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A small lump, swelling or collection of tissue.  **Synonyms:** - NODULE - nodule
[C32800] Infundibular Segment of the Fallopian Tube -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The funnel shaped portion of the fallopian tube proximal to the fimbrial segment and most distal to the uterus.

[C32804] Inner Enamel Epithelium -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The inner most layer of the enamel organ. It is derived from the ectoderm of the dental lamina and differentiates into ameloblasts that secrete the enamel of the root crown.
[C32805] Inner Hair Cell of the Organ of the Corti -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cell situated on the inner most layer of the basilar membrane of the cochlea. Each cell has multiple, sensitive strands called stereocilia. In the resting state the stereocilia are leaning on each other in a conical bundle and touch the tectorial membrane. When the cochlea moves in response to sound, a slight shearing force occurs between the basilar and tectorial membranes, the stereocilia bend and send electrical impulses to the brain via the eighth cranial nerve.  **Synonyms:** - Inner Hair Cell of Organ of Corti

Malignant neoplasms are usually associated with rapid swelling and pain.  **Synonyms:** - Odontogenic Tumor - Odontogenic Tumor - Odontogenic Tumors - Odontogenic tumor, NOS

[C32869] Interphase Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cell in a resting state. Individual chromosomes are not visible. The cell performs all biochemical and physiologic functions and replication of chromatin occurs.  **Synonyms:** - Non-dividing Cell - Resting Cell - resting
[C3287] Odontoma -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A benign, slow growing, and painless hamartomatous tumor occurring in tooth-bearing areas of the jaws. According to the presence or absence of tooth-like structures, it is classified as complex type or compound type. Odontoma of complex type is characterized by the presence of enamel and dentin and the absence of tooth-like structures. It is treated with local excision. If it is incompletely removed, it may recur. Odontoma of compound type is characterized by the presence of tooth-like structures. It is treated by local excision. Recurrences have not been reported.  **Synonyms:** - Fibro-Odontoma - Fibro-Odontoma - Fibroodontoma - Fibroodontoma - ODONTOMA, BENIGN - Odontoma, NOS
[C32870] Interstitial Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** An interstitial cell is a cell that is part of the connective and soft tissues.
[C32871] Interstitial Cell of Cajal -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A specialized cell found throughout the gastrointestinal tract. These cells are essential for normal gastrointestinal motility by functioning as the pacemaker cells in gastrointestinal muscles. They mediate or transduce inputs from enteric motor nerves to the smooth muscle syncytium.
[C32872] Interstitial Cell of the Pineal Gland -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A non-neuronal cell that supports the pineal gland. It resembles an astrocyte and is found around blood vessels and among groups of pinealocytes. The nucleus is small, elongated and darkly stained.

[C32879] Intraparietal Sulcus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A horizontal groove on the surface of the parietal lobe extending backward from the postcentral sulcus forming the ascending, superior and inferior parietal convolutions.
[C3288] Oligodendroglioma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A well-differentiated (WHO grade 2), diffusely infiltrating neuroglial tumor, typically located in the cerebral hemispheres.  It is composed predominantly of cells which morphologically resemble oligodendroglia. The neoplastic cells have rounded homogeneous nuclei and, on paraffin sections, a swollen, clear cytoplasm ('honeycomb' appearance). (Adapted from WHO)  **Synonyms:** - Oligodendroglioma, NOS - WHO Grade 2 Oligodendroglial Neoplasm - WHO Grade 2 Oligodendroglial Tumor - WHO Grade II Oligodendroglial Neoplasm - WHO Grade II Oligodendroglial Tumor - Well Differentiated Oligodendroglial Tumor - Well Differentiated Oligodendroglioma - oligodendroglioma

[C32882] Irregular Dense Connective Tissue -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** Tissue characterized by a thick, random arrangement of collagen and elastin fibers with very few cells. The majority of the cells are fibroblasts, but mast cells and macrophages may also be seen. It is found in the dermis, periosteum, perichondrium, capsules of organs and sheaths of nerves and muscles.

[C32925] Large Intestinal Glandular Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** Intestinal enterocytes are low columnar to cuboidal cells comprising the majority of the epithelial tissue in the intestine and colon. They have both absorptive and secretory functions, absorbing water, sodium and short-chain fatty acids while secreting bicarbonate and potassium. Colonocytes originate from stem cells located at the base of the crypts. Enterocyte production, differentiation, and turnover occurs in topographically distinct regions of crypts. The transformation of colonic epithelial cells are frequently involved in colorectal cancers.
[C32926] Large Intestinal Mucosa -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** Mucosa consisting of the epithelium, basement membrane, lamina propria mucosae and lamina muscularis mucosae of the distal portion of the digestive tract from the small intestine to the anus.

[C32928] Large Intestinal Muscularis Mucosa -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The thin layer of smooth muscle fibers usually found as a part of the tunica mucosa deep to the lamina propria mucosae of the large intestine.
[C32929] Large Intestinal Serosal Surface -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The membrane lining the external walls of the distal portion of the digestive tract from the small intestine to the anus. It consists of mesothelium on a connective tissue layer and it secretes a watery exudate.
[C3293] Osteoarthritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A noninflammatory degenerative joint disease occurring chiefly in older persons, characterised by degeneration of the articular cartilage, hypertrophy of bone at the margins and changes in the synovial membrane. It is accompanied by pain and stiffness, particularly after prolonged activity.  **Synonyms:** - DEGENERATIVE JOINT DISEASE
[C32930] Large Intestinal Submucosa -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The layer of areolar tissue situated beneath the mucous membrane in the distal portion of the digestive tract from the small intestine to the anus.
[C32931] Large Intestinal Wall Tissue -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The tissue of the large intestine. It is composed of four layers - mucosa, submucosa, smooth muscle with inner circular and outer longitudinal layers, and serosa. The mucosa has a large number of goblet cells but does not have any villi. The longitudinal muscle layer is incomplete. The longitudinal muscle is limited to three distinct bands, called teniae coli that run the entire length of the colon. Contraction of the teniae coli exerts pressure on the wall and creates a series of pouches, called haustra, along the colon. Epiploic appendages, pieces of fat-filled connective tissue, are attached to the outer surface of the colon.
[C32932] Centroblast -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** An activated B-lymphocyte found in the germinal centers.  It has a large nucleus without clefts, finely dispersed chromatin, and a rim of basophilic cytoplasm.  **Synonyms:** - Large Non-Cleaved Follicle Center Cell

[C3294] Osteoblastoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A rare benign bone-forming neoplasm usually arising from the spine. It is a well-circumscribed lytic tumor that varies in size. The tumor is composed of woven bone trabeculae and shares similar histologic characteristics with the osteoid osteoma. Surgical curettage is the treatment of choice. The prognosis is excellent.  **Synonyms:** - Giant Osteoid Osteoma - Giant Osteoid Osteoma - Giant osteoid osteoma - OSTEOBLASTOMA, BENIGN - Ossifying Giant Cell Tumor - Ossifying Giant Cell Tumor - Osteoblastoma, NOS

[C3295] Osteochondroma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A common, benign cartiliginous neoplasm arising from the metaphysis of bone. The tumor grows on the surface of the bone; it may be pedunculated or sessile. It is characterized by the presence of chondrocytes, a cartilage cap, and a fibrous perichondrium that extends to the periosteum of the bone. In some cases, there is deletion of 8q24.1 chromosome locus.  **Synonyms:** - OSTEOCHONDROMA, BENIGN - Osteocartilaginous Exostosis - Osteocartilaginous exostosis

[C3297] Osteoid Osteoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A small, benign bone-forming neoplasm that can arise from any bone but more frequently affects the long bones. The central portion of the neoplasm (nidus) contains differentiated osteoblasts that produce osteoid and sometimes bone. The lesion is usually surrounded by hypervascular sclerotic bone and has limited growth potential. Clinical signs and symptoms include pain and localized tenderness, at the site of the lesion. The pain may be intense but in the majority of cases it is completely alleviated by non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs. Prognosis is excellent and recurrences are rare.  **Synonyms:** - Osteoid osteoma, NOS
[C32970] Left Portion of the Diaphragm -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The portion of the diaphragm in the left side of the body.

[C3298] Osteoporosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition of reduced bone mass, with decreased cortical thickness and a decrease in the number and size of the trabeculae of cancellous bone (but normal chemical composition), resulting in increased fracture incidence. Osteoporosis is classified as primary (Type 1, postmenopausal osteoporosis; Type 2, age-associated osteoporosis; and idiopathic, which can affect juveniles, premenopausal women, and middle-aged men) and secondary osteoporosis (which results from an identifiable cause of bone mass loss).  **Synonyms:** - osteoporosis

[C32984] Levator Ani -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A broad, thin muscle originating form the posterior side of the pubis body, spine of the ischium, and pelvic fascia between the pubis and ischium on either side of the pelvis. [C32989] Lipid Inclusion -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A detectable aggregation of lipids or membrane fragments in the cytoplasm.  **Synonyms:** - Fat Droplet - Lipid Droplet
[C3299] Infectious Otitis Externa -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the anatomical structures of the outer ear and ear canal secondary to an infectious process. Bacterial etiology is most common, but fungal infection is also possible. Symptoms include erythema, edema, and pain.  **Synonyms:** - External Ear Infection - Otitis externa - Outer Ear Infection - Swimmer's Ear
[C32990] Lipoblast -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A connective tissue cell that develops into a fat cell.
[C32991] Lipocyte -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A fat storing cell.
[C32992] Liver Acinus -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The smallest functional unit of the liver, comprising all of the liver parenchyma supplied by a terminal branch of the portal vein and hepatic artery; typically involves segments of two lobules lying between two terminal hepatic venules.
[C32993] Liver Acinus Zone 1 -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** An area of hepatocytes that are closest to an arteriole. Because of their close proximity to oxygenated blood, the cells in this zone carry out oxidative processes such as beta-oxidation, cholesterol synthesis and gluconeogenesis.  **Synonyms:** - Periportal Zone - Zone 1 - Zone 1 of Liver Acinus - Zone 1 of the Liver Acinus
[C32994] Liver Acinus Zone 2 -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** An area of hepatocytes between zone 1 and 3 in which the cells display properties intermediate to both neighboring zones.  **Synonyms:** - Transition Zone - Transition Zone - Zone 2 - Zone 2 of Liver Acinus - Zone 2 of the Liver Acinus
[C32995] Liver Acinus Zone 3 -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** An area of hepatocytes that are closest to a central vein. These cells receive the least amount of oxygenated blood and are thus more prone to ischemia and store fat.  **Synonyms:** - Centrilobular Zone - Zone 3 - Zone 3 of Liver Acinus - Zone 3 of the Liver Acinus
[C32996] Liver Fissure -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Any of the clefts or grooves on the surface of the liver.  **Synonyms:** - LIVER FISSURE

[C330] Calcitriol -- **Semantic type:** Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A synthetic physiologically-active analog of vitamin D, specifically the vitamin D3 form.  Calcitriol regulates calcium in vivo by promoting absorption in the intestine, reabsorption in the kidneys, and, along with parathyroid hormone, regulation of bone growth.  A calcitriol receptor-binding protein appears to exist in the mucosa of human intestine. Calcitriol also induces cell cycle arrest at G0/G1 phase of the cell cycle, cell differentiation, and apoptosis, resulting in inhibition of proliferation of some tumor cell types.  This agent may be chemopreventive for colon and prostate cancers. (NCI04)  **Synonyms:** - (1alpha,3beta,5Z,7E)-9,10-Secocholesta-5,7,10(19)-triene-1,3,25-triol - 1,25(OH)2-D3 - 1,25-DHCC - 1,25-Dihydroxycholecalciferol - 1,25-Dihydroxycholecaliferol - CALCITRIOL - Calcijex - Rocaltrol - calcitriol

Symptoms include pelvic and abdominal pain and irregular periods.  **Synonyms:** - Cyst of Ovary - Cyst of the Ovary

[C33024] Lung Tissue -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** Tissue consisting of an external serous coat, subserous areolar tissue and lung parenchyma. The parenchyma is made up of lobules wound together by connective tissue. A primary lobule consists of a terminal bronchiole, respiratory bronchioles, and alveolar ducts, which communicate with many alveoli, each alveolus being surrounded by a network of capillary blood vessels.  **Synonyms:** - Lung Parenchyma

[C33029] Lymph Node Cortex -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The portion of the lymph node that contains nodules with germinal centers where B lymphocyte mature into plasma cells after activation. The nodules are separated by diffuse lymphatic tissue, lymph sinuses and trabeculae.

[C33032] Lymph Node Medullary Portion -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The central portion of the lymph node. It is divided into sections by tracts of lymphatic tissue called medullary cords. Between the medullary cords are the medullary sinuses, which consist mainly of macrophages that engulf microorganisms as part of the lymph node's filtering function. Lymphocytes and plasma cells are also found in the medullary portion.
[C33033] Lymph Node Sinus -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** An irregular tortuous space within lymph nodes through which a continuous stream of lymph passes entering into the efferent lymphatic vessels.  **Synonyms:** - Lymph Sinus - Lymphatic Sinus
[C33034] Lymph Node Tissue -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** Tissue composed of specialized cells that are organized as a definite organ, situated along the course of lymphatic vessels, and consisting of an outer cortical and an inner medullary part. The lymph nodes are the main source of lymphocytes of the peripheral blood and serve as a defense mechanism by removing noxious agents, such as bacteria and toxins, and probably play a role in antibody production.  **Synonyms:** - LYMPH NODE TISSUE
[C33035] Lymph Node Trabecula -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The supporting and anchoring strand of connective tissue radiating out from the capsule through the interior of the lymph node.  **Synonyms:** - Lymph Node Trabeculae
[C33037] Lymphatic Capillary -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Tiny vessels with porous walls that join to form the lymphatic vessels.

[C33040] Lymphoid Follicle -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** Nodular structures composed of lymphocytes. In the absence of immune stimulation, they ae called primary follicles and are composed of small B-lymphocytes. With antigenic stimulation the B-lymphocytes replicate and differentiate, to give rise to secondary follicles which are composed of germinal centers and are surrounded by mantle zones.

[C33059] Mature Bone -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** A type of bone tissue. There are two subtypes of mature bone - compact and spongy. Compact bone is very dense and hard on the outside. Compact bone forms the thick-walled tube of the shaft of long bones and covers the epiphyses of long bones. Spongy bone is lightweight and consists of trabeculae, which branch and intersect to form a sponge like network, and red bone marrow for hematopoiesis. Short bones and the epiphyses of long bones consist mainly of spongy bone.  **Synonyms:** - Lamellar Bone

[C33061] Mature T-Lymphocyte -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A lymphocyte derived from a pre T-lymphocyte in the thymus and stored in secondary lymphoid organs, bone marrow, and lymph nodes. It circulates in the bloodstream and the lymphatic system, where it searches for and attacks particular foreign or abnormal cells. A mature T lymphocyte has T cell receptors and other surface proteins on its cell surface.  **Synonyms:** - Mature T-Cell

[C3308] Paraganglioma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm arising from paraganglia located along the sympathetic or parasympathetic nerves.  Infrequently, it may arise outside the usual distribution of the sympathetic and parasympathetic paraganglia.  Tumors arising from the adrenal gland medulla are called pheochromocytomas.  Morphologically, paragangliomas usually display a nesting (Zellballen) growth pattern.  There are no reliable morphologic criteria to distinguish between benign and malignant paragangliomas.  The only definitive indicator of malignancy is the presence of regional or distant metastases.  **Synonyms:** - Neoplasm of Paraganglion - Neoplasm of the Paraganglion - Paraganglioma, NOS - Paraganglion Neoplasm - Paraganglion Tumor - Paraganglionic Neoplasm - Paraganglionic Tumor - Tumor of Paraganglion - Tumor of the Paraganglion - paraganglioma

[C33107] Metatarsal Artery -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Any of the four dorsal or four plantar arteries that arise from the dorsalis pedis artery that extend over the metatarsal bones.

[C33109] Microcirculatory Bed -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A collection of the smallest blood vessels of the vascular system, which are involved in the exchange of gases, water, nutrients, and waste products, and which includes blood vessels from the first-order arteriole to the first-order venule.  **Synonyms:** - Microvascular Bed
[C3311] Paraneoplastic Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A classification for rare disorders of diverse organ systems (endocrine, neuromuscular, gastrointestinal, renal, dermatologic, rheumatologic, hematologic) that are affected by substances secreted by a distant neoplasm but not by the action of the neoplasm itself metastasizing to that organ or tissue. Less than 1 % of neoplasms are associated with these syndromes. An immune-mediated response to neoplasm-elaborated proteins may be the cause of these syndromes. Additionally, their manifestation may signal the presence of an occult neoplasm, potentially at an earlier stage of disease thereby leading to a better clinical outcome. Constitutional signs may include fever, night sweats, anorexia and cachexia. Clinical course is usually progressive. Prognosis is variable depending on the effective treatment of the underlying neoplasm.  **Synonyms:** - paraneoplastic syndrome
[C33110] Microtrabecular Strand -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure
[C33111] Microtubule Triplet -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A structure made of three cylindrical tubulin polymers.
[C33112] Microvillus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A finger-like projection of the plasma membrane found in epithelial cells that increases the surface area of the apical domain of the epithelium. It is comprised of plasma membrane that is supported by longitudinal actin filaments that are crosslinked by fimbrin and villin.
[C33113] Midcortical Nephron -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Nephrons located superior to the juxtamedullary region but inferior to the superficial nephrons in the middle region of the renal cortex.  **Synonyms:** - Intermediate Nephron
[C33114] Middle Cerebral Artery Branch -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Any artery arising from the middle cerebral artery.
[C33115] Middle Constrictor Muscle -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A pharyngeal muscle located in the neck between the superior and inferior pharyngeal constrictor muscles, which originates on the hyoid bone, inserts on the pharyngeal raphe, and assists in the act of swallowing.
[C33116] Middle Cuneiform Bone of the Foot -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The second wedge-shaped bone between the internal and external cuneiforms bones in the distal row of the tarsus.
[C33117] Middle Cranial Fossa -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A depression of the skull base lying posterior and inferior to the anterior cranial fossa and separated from the posterior cranial fossa by the clivus and the petrous crest. It supports the temporal lobes of the cerebrum and the pituitary gland.
[C33118] Middle Frontal Gyrus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A relatively large region on the lateral surface of the frontal lobe and part of the prefrontal cortex, which lies between the superior and the inferior frontal sulci and rostral to the precentral gyrus.  **Synonyms:** - MIDDLE FRONTAL GYRUS - Middle Frontal Convolution
[C33119] Middle Frontal Gyrus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure
[C3312] Parapsoriasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inflammatory skin disorder of unknown etiology characterized by papules and plaques or scaly patches resembling psoriasis.

[C33121] Middle Inferior Frontal Convolution -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A region of the brain, located on the inferior frontal gyrus, that contains part of Broca's area, which is an important neuroanatomical region used in speech and language production.  **Synonyms:** - Pars Triangularis

[C33130] Minus End of the Actin Filament -- **Semantic type:** Cell Component  **Definition:** The end of an actin filament that is polymerizing at a slower rate.  **Synonyms:** - Minus End of Actin Filament - Pointed End of Actin Filament - Pointed End of the Actin Filament

[C33138] Monocytoid B-Lymphocyte -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A lymphocyte derived most often from a pre-germinal center B cell, less often from a post-germinal center B cell. It is characterized by an abundant pale cytoplasm, indented nuclei with inconspicuous nucleoli, and open chromatin. It is found in the marginal zones of lymph nodes adjacent to the subcapsular and intermediary sinuses.  **Synonyms:** - Monocytoid B-Cell
[C33139] Mons Pubis -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A rounded prominence of adipose tissue overlying the pubic symphysis and adjacent pubic bone.
[C3314] Parotid Gland Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the parotid gland.  Representative examples of benign neoplasms include Warthin tumor, monomorphic adenoma, and pleomorphic adenoma.  Representative examples of malignant neoplasms include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma.  **Synonyms:** - Neoplasm of Parotid - Neoplasm of Parotid Gland - Neoplasm of the Parotid - Neoplasm of the Parotid Gland - Parotid Gland Tumor - Parotid Neoplasm - Parotid Tumor - Tumor of Parotid - Tumor of Parotid Gland - Tumor of the Parotid - Tumor of the Parotid Gland
[C33140] Motor Nucleus of the Trigeminal Nerve -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** An aggregation of CN V cell bodies located in the mid-pons at the level of attachment of the trigeminal nerve to the brainstem; it is the origin of special visceral efferent (motor) fibers that innervate the muscles of mastication.

[C33151] Myoblast -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A muscle cell precursor. It is essential for muscle repair.
[C33152] Myoepithelial Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A contractile cell found between the secretory cells and basement membrane of exocrine glands. Each myoepithelial cell has long cytoplasmic processes that wrap around a secretory unit. The contraction of the myoepithelial processes forces the secretory product from the secretory unit into its duct.  **Synonyms:** - Basket Cell
[C33153] Myofibroblast -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A spindle-shaped cell that exhibits characteristics of both fibroblasts and smooth muscle cells. It has an elongated nucleus and does not have basal lamina. The cytoplasm can be distinguished from the surrounding matrix because of actin filaments, myosin and other muscle proteins arranged to suggest a contractile ability.
[C33154] Myofilament -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Fibrillar protein polymers found in the contractile apparatus of muscle cells.
[C33155] Myoid Portion of the Inner Segment of the Photoreceptor Cell -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The portion of the inner segment of a photoreceptor cell between the ellipsoid portion and the nucleus that is rich in ribonucleic acids (RNA) and is a major site for protein synthesis.

[C332] Calcium Carbonate -- **Semantic type:** Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** The carbonic salt of calcium (CaCO3). (NCI04)  **Synonyms:** - CALCIUM CARBONATE - calcium carbonate

[C33203] Olfactory Epithelium -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** An epithelium located in the roof, superior conchae, and septum of the nasal cavity. It is formed by ciliated olfactory receptor cells, supporting cells and basal cells. Olfactory glands in the epithelium produce and secrete mucus which forms a layer over the epithelium. The cilia of the receptor cells protrude into the mucous layer where molecular reception with odors occurs and sensory transduction begins.
[C33204] Olfactory Lobe -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A small, rounded neural structure involved in olfaction, which is located at the anterior end of the olfactory sulcus on the orbital surface of the frontal lobe, and which is attached to the rest of the brain by the olfactory tract.
[C33205] Olfactory Mucosa -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The part of the nasal mucosa composed of neuroepithelial tissue and mucus-producing Bowman's glands. The mucus moistens the epithelium and helps dissolve odor-containing gases.  **Synonyms:** - OLFACTORY MUCOSA
[C33206] Olfactory Sulcus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A fissure or narrow groove running rostrocaudally on the anterior surface of each frontal lobe of the brain separating the gyrus rectus from the medial orbital gyrus and containing the olfactory bulb and tract.
[C33207] Olfactory Tract -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A white band comprising axons originating in the olfactory bulb and running caudally along the orbital surface of each frontal lobe that terminate in the primary olfactory cortex at the base of the cerebral hemisphere.
[C33209] Omentum -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A fold of peritoneum originating at the stomach and supporting the viscera.  **Synonyms:** - OMENTUM - Omental Fat - omentum - omentum

[C33215] Ophthalmic Nerve -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The first and smallest of three divisions of the trigeminal nerve, which carries only sensory fibers supplying innervation to the forehead, scalp, lacrimal gland, eye, and side of the nose.
[C33216] Ophthalmic Artery -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** An artery arising from the internal carotid artery that branches into two groups of vessels; the orbital group that supplies the orbit and surrounding parts and the ocular group that supplies the globe and muscles of the eye.  **Synonyms:** - OPHTHALMIC ARTERY
[C33217] Optic Commissure -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The bundle of optic nerve fibers that cross to the opposite side of the brain at the optic chiasm.
[C33218] Optic Tract -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A continuation of the optic nerves just dorsal to the optic chiasm, formed by temporal fibers of the ipsilateral optic nerve and nasal fibers of the contralateral optic nerve, that carries axons to nuclei within the thalamus and relays visual information to the primary visual cortex of the occipital lobe.  **Synonyms:** - optic tract - optic tract
[C33219] Orbiculus Ciliaris -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** An annular zone of the ciliary body continuous with the retina at the ora serrata and covered with ciliary plicae.
[C3322] Peritoneal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the peritoneum. Representative examples of malignant neoplasms include primary peritoneal carcinoma, metastatic carcinoma to the peritoneum, and malignant mesothelioma.
[C33220] Orbit Disc -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure
[C33221] Orbital Part of the Inferior Frontal Gyrus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The most rostral and prominent of three divisions of the inferior frontal gyrus, which merges anteriorly with the lateral orbital gyri, superiorly with the anterior parts of the middle frontal gyrus, and posteriorly with the triangular part the inferior frontal gyrus.
[C33222] Orbital Sulcus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** An H shaped groove on the inferior surface of the frontal lobe dividing it into four orbital gyri.
[C33223] Organ of Corti -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The principal sensory structure of the auditory system, which is located on the basilar membrane in the cochlea.  **Synonyms:** - Spiral Organ of Corti
[C33224] Organ of Special Sense -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Anatomic structures in the head responsible for special sensations rather than general sensations.  This category includes the eyes, ears, nose, and tongue.  **Synonyms:** - Special Sense Organ
[C33225] Orifice of the Eustachian Tube -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The opening of the eustachian tube at its pharyngeal end.  **Synonyms:** - Orifice of Auditory Tube - Orifice of Eustachian Tube - Orifice of the Auditory Tube
[C33226] Orthodox Configuration of the Mitochondrion -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A basic architectural plan of a mitochondrion that exhibits a prominent matrix and narrow cristae, with the inner and outer compartment-intracristal space volumetrically decreased, and which is linked to inactive oxidase phosphorylation.
[C33227] Osseous Labyrinth -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A part of the inner ear located in the petrous part of the temporal bone consisting of a series of bony cavities that contains the membranous labyrinth, which is structurally and functionally divided into the vestibule, the semicircular canals, and the cochlea.  **Synonyms:** - Bony Labyrinth
[C33228] Osteoid -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** A protein mixture secreted by osteoblasts. When the protein mixture mineralizes, it becomes bone. Osteoid is primarily composed of type 1 collagen.
[C33229] Other Body Part -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** Any component associated with an organ or tissue that is different from the one(s) previously specified or mentioned.
[C3323] Petechia -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Purple or red hemorrhagic pinpoint spots in the skin or mucous membranes.  **Synonyms:** - Petechiae - Petechiae - petechiae
[C33230] Otolith -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Crystals of calcium carbonate embedded in the otolithic membrane in the maculae of the saccule and utricle in the inner ear, which comprise the otolith organs containing receptors that respond to gravity and head tilt.
[C33231] Otolymph -- **Semantic type:** Body Substance
[C33232] Outer Enamel Epithelium -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** Epithelium derived from the ectoderm of the dental lamina. It is found on the upper part of the bell or cap of the enamel organ of the developing tooth and is composed of cuboidal cells.
[C33233] Outer Hair Cell of the Organ of the Corti -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cell situated on one of three of the most outer layers of the basilar membrane of the cochlea. Each cell has multiple, sensitive strands called stereocilia. In the resting state the stereocilia are leaning on each other in a conical bundle and are embedded in the tectorial membrane. When the cochlea moves in response to sound, a slight shearing force occurs between the basilar and tectorial membranes, the stereocilia bend and send electrical impulses to the brain via the eighth cranial nerve.  **Synonyms:** - Outer Hair Cell of Organ of Corti
[C33234] Outer Leaflet of the Lipid Bilayer -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The extracellular facing lipid layer of the plasma membrane.
[C33235] Outer Limiting Membrane -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** A thin fenestrated layer of the pars nervosa retinae adjacent to the outer nuclear layer and through which extend the visual rods and cones.
[C33236] Outer Nuclear Layer -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Synonyms:** - Outer Nuclear Layer of Retina - Outer Nuclear Layer of the Retina
[C33237] Outer Plexiform Layer -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Synonyms:** - Outer Plexiform Layer of the Retina
[C33238] Outer Rod of the Corti -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The cells forming the outer spiraled tunnel walls in the organ of Corti within the inner ear.  **Synonyms:** - Outer Rod of Corti
[C33239] Outer Segment of the Photoreceptor Cell -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The distal portion of the photoreceptor cells made up of densely packed disks containing opsin proteins that absorb light.
[C3324] Peutz-Jeghers Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inherited condition characterized by generalized hamartomatous multiple polyposis of the intestinal tract. Transmitted in an autosomal dominant fashion, Peutz-Jeghers syndrome consistently involves the jejunum and is associated with melanin spots of the lips, buccal mucosa, and fingers. This syndrome is associated with abnormalities of chromosome 19. Also known as Jeghers-Peutz syndrome and Peutz's syndrome.  **Synonyms:** - Jeghers-Peutz Syndrome - PJS - PJS - Peutz's Syndrome - Peutz-Jeghers Disease - Peutz-Jeghers syndrome - Peutz-Jeghers syndrome
[C33240] Outer Supporting Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cell forming a cup for the outer hair cell of the Organ of Corti. The supporting cell sends out a narrow filament that angles towards the base of the cochlea. The structure is such that the supporting cell touches the outer hair cell only at the top and bottom.  **Synonyms:** - Outer Phalangeal Cell
[C33241] Outer Wall of the Vestibule -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The wall facing the outside of the vestibule of the inner ear.  **Synonyms:** - Outer Wall of Vestibule - Tympanic Wall of Vestibule - Tympanic Wall of the Vestibule
[C33242] Ovarian Artery -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Either of two arteries (right or left) that arise from the aorta to supply the corresponding ovary, ovarian ligament, uterine tube and ureter.
[C33243] Ovarian Cortex -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The outer layer of the ovary, composed of cellular connective tissue which contains the ovarian follicles.
[C33244] Ovarian Follicle -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A spherical aggregation of cells found in the ovaries that contains a single oocyte.  **Synonyms:** - Follicle - OVARIAN FOLLICLE
[C33245] Ovarian Medulla -- **Semantic type:** Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component  **Definition:** The central portion of the ovary, composed of loose connective tissue that contains blood vessels, lymph vessels and nerves.
[C33246] Ovarian Tissue -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** Tissue comprised of specialized cells and intercellular matrix, aggregated according to genetically determined spatial relationships in order to carry out the functions of production and ovulation of oocytes and the production and secretion of hormones.  **Synonyms:** - Human Ovarian Tissue
[C33247] Palatal Muscle -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Any of the small muscles having an origin or insertion on the soft palate or palatine aponeurosis, including the levator veli palatini, musculus uvulae, palatoglossus, palatopharyngeus, and tensor veli palatini muscle.
[C33248] Palate Bone -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A horizontal bony plate located in the roof of the mouth created by the palatine processes of the two maxillae and the palatine bone.
[C33249] Palatine Salivary Gland -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** One of the many racemose mucous glands within the back half of the hard palate submucosa.
[C3325] Pharyngeal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the nasopharynx, oropharynx, or hypopharynx.  **Synonyms:** - Neoplasm of Pharynx - Neoplasm of the Pharynx - Pharyngeal Neoplasms - Pharyngeal Tumor - Pharynx Neoplasm - Pharynx Tumor - Tumor of Pharynx - Tumor of the Pharynx

[C33251] Palmar Fascia -- **Semantic type:** Tissue
[C33252] Palmar Region -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The undersurface of the hand.  **Synonyms:** - PALM
[C33254] Pancreatic Centroacinar Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cell with a centrally placed nucleus found in the proximal portion of pancreatic intercalated duct that protrudes into the acinus. It secretes bicarbonate made in the cell by the dissociation of carbonic acid in the presence of carbonic anhydrase II. These cells are thought to act as signal transducers, influencing the degranulation of the acinar cells.
[C33255] Pancreatic Endocrine Secretion -- **Semantic type:** Body Substance  **Synonyms:** - Endocrine Pancreas Secretion

[C33258] Papilla of the Tongue -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Numerous, tiny, vertical projections on the anterior two thirds of the dorsal side of the tongue that consist of four types: filiform, fungiform, foliate, and circumvallate.
[C33259] Papillary Muscle -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Any one of the group of small muscles located in the ventricles of the heart that insert onto the ventricular wall and are continuous at the opposite end with the chordae tendineae, which insert onto the free edge of the atrioventricular valves, functioning to keep the valves closed during ventricular systole.  **Synonyms:** - MUSCLE, PAPILLARY - PAPILLARY MUSCLE
[C3326] Adrenal Gland Pheochromocytoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neuroendocrine neoplasm of the sympathetic nervous system that arises from the chromaffin cells of the adrenal medulla and secretes catecholamines. Clinical presentation includes headaches, palpitations, chest and abdominal pain, hypertension, fever, and tremor.  Microscopically, a characteristic nesting (zellballen) growth pattern is usually seen.  Other growth patterns including trabecular pattern may also be present.  **Synonyms:** - Adrenal Gland Chromaffin Paraganglioma - Adrenal Gland Chromaffinoma - Adrenal Gland Paraganglioma - Adrenal Medullary Paraganglioma - Adrenal Medullary Pheochromocytoma - Adrenal Pheochromocytoma - Adrenal medullary paraganglioma - Chromaffin Paraganglioma of the Adrenal Gland - Intraadrenal Paraganglioma - PCC - Pheochromocytoma - Pheochromocytoma - Pheochromocytoma (adrenal) - pheochromocytoma
[C33260] Parafascicular Nucleus of the Thalamus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** Part of the posterior (caudal) intralaminar group of thalamic nuclei, located medial to the centromedian nucleus and ventral to the dorsomedial nuclei, that is associated with the limbic and associative striatum.
[C33261] C-Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A neuroendocrine cell found in the thyroid gland interspersed among the follicular cells or in clusters between the follicles. [C33262] Parahippocampal Gyrus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A cortical region or convolution on the dorsal surface of each cerebral hemisphere overlying the hippocampus between the hippocampal and collateral fissures, which includes the entorhinal cortex, the temporal cortical fields, and is considered part of the limbic system.
[C33263] Paranasal Sinus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure

[C33265] Parathyroid Gland Capsule -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The thin fibrous lining that divides the parathyroid gland into lobules.
[C33266] Parathyroid Gland Chief Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** The primary cell of the parathyroid gland. Its contains secretory granules, large Golgi complexes, and moderate numbers of mitochondria. It produces parathyroid hormone. The cells occur in sheets interspersed with areas of fatty tissue. Occasionally the cells are arranged in follicles.
[C33267] Parathyroid Gland Clear Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A large cell of the parathyroid gland. It has abundant clear cytoplasm packed with glycogen and a basal nucleus.
[C33268] Parathyroid Gland Follicle -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** A follicle believed to be formed by degeneration of suppressed chief cells. Secretion of hormonal and/or other substances into intercellular spaces progressively forms follicular cavities.
[C33269] Parathyroid Gland Oxyphil Cell -- **Semantic type:** Cell  **Definition:** A cell of the parathyroid gland that has condensed chromatin in a small round nucleus. Its cytoplasm is composed of tightly packed mitochondria and glycogen granules, with rare secretory granules, stains pink, and the margin is usually observed.  **Synonyms:** - Parathyroid Gland Oxyphilic Cell
[C3327] Skin Discoloration -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Abnormal skin coloration.  **Synonyms:** - Skin Dyschromia
[C33270] Parathyroid Gland Tissue -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** Parathyroid gland tissue consists of three primary cell types - chief cells, oxyphil cells and clear cells. These glands are usually located in close proximity to the thyroid gland.  **Synonyms:** - Human Parathyroid
[C33271] Parietal Layer of Bowman Capsule -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** The outermost layer of the renal capsule, which is composed of a single layer of simple squamous epithelium.  **Synonyms:** - Parietal Layer of Bowman's Capsule

[C33273] Parietal Pleura -- **Semantic type:** Tissue  **Definition:** The outermost of the two pleural membranes.
