[C34195] Intraembryonic Coelom -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A cavity located in the lateral plate mesoderm in the conceptus, which appears during the third week of development, and which eventually forms the pleuropericardial-peritoneal cavity.
[C34196] Jugular Lymph Sac -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Two of six primary lymph sacs found during the embryonic period that are the first precursors to lymph node development, and which are located near the junction of the subclavian veins and the anterior cardinals.
[C34197] Lanugo -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Fine downy hair that covers the body of a human fetus beginning in the fifth month of gestation; it is usually shed by the ninth month of gestation.
[C34198] Laryngotracheal Tube -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A developmental feature of the early embryonic foregut on the ventral wall of the pharynx, generally forming during gestational week four, which is the primordium of the larynx, trachea, bronchi, and lungs.
[C34199] Lateral Mesoderm -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A mesodermal sheet located in the middle layer of the trilaminar embryo, lateral to the intermediate mesoderm.
[C342] Carboquone -- **Semantic type:** Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** An aziridinylbenzoquinone-based alkylating agent with potential antineoplastic activity. The alkylating group in carboquone becomes activated upon reduction of quinone to the hydroquinone form. This eventually results in the alkylation and crosslinking of DNA, thereby inhibiting DNA replication followed by an induction of apoptosis. In addition, reactive oxygen species may form during redox cycling which may contribute to this agent's cytotoxic activity.  **Synonyms:** - CARBOQUONE
[C3420] Chromosomal Translocation -- **Semantic type:** Cell or Molecular Dysfunction  **Definition:** A genetic exchange where a piece of one chromosome is transferred to another chromosome.  **Synonyms:** - Chromosome Translocation - Translocation - Translocation - Translocation - Translocation - Translocation - Translocation NOS - chromosomal translocation - chromosomal_translocation - t
[C34200] Left Brachiocephalic Vein -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure
[C34201] Lens Pit -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A depression in the surface ectoderm in the head region of the early embryo formed by invagination of the lens placodes, which houses the lens precursor cells.
[C34202] Lens Placodes -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A pair of thickened surface ectodermal patches in the head region of the early embryo that will ultimately give rise to the lenses of the eyes.
[C34203] Limb Bud -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Outgrowths of ectoderm and mesoderm from the embryonic trunk, generally forming during gestational week four, that will eventually form the paired upper and lower limbs.
[C34204] Mandibular Prominence -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An embryonic structure developing from the lower part of the first pharyngeal arch, which gives rise to the mandible.
[C34205] Maxillary Artery -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Structure  **Definition:** A terminal branch of the external carotid artery running from the ramus of the jaw to the pterygopalatine fossa with numerous branches supplying the deep structures of the face.
[C34206] Maxillary Prominence -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An embryonic structure developing from the upper part of the first pharyngeal arch, which gives rise to the upper jaw and palatal shelves.
[C34207] Metanephric Diverticulum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An outgrowth arising from each mesonephric duct during the 5th week of embryonic development, which gives rise to the renal collecting ducts, calyces, pelvis and ureters, and, together with the metanephric blastema, will give rise to the permanent kidney.  **Synonyms:** - Ureteric Bud
[C34208] Metanephric Mass of the Intermediate Mesoderm -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A mass of cells located on the caudal part of the intermediate mesoderm that will develop into the nephrons of the kidney, and, together with the ureteric bud, will give rise to the permanent kidney.  **Synonyms:** - Metanephrogenic Blastema
[C34209] Metanephros -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The third and final stage of mammalian kidney (pro-, meso-, meta-) development, which takes place within the intermediate mesoderm.  **Synonyms:** - Primordium of Permanent Kidney
[C3421] Trisomy -- **Semantic type:** Cell or Molecular Dysfunction  **Definition:** A chromosomal abnormality consisting of the presence of one chromosome in addition to the normal diploid number.  **Synonyms:** - trisomy
[C34210] Midgut -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The middle region (foregut, midgut and hindgut) of the early formation of the digestive tract in the developing embryo, which gives rise to the distal duodenum, jejunum, ileum, cecum, appendix, and colon from the cecum to the distal transverse colon.
[C34211] Midgut Loop -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The portion of the midgut in the developing embryo that bends around the superior mesenteric artery to form the alimentary canal from the duodenum through the proximal two-thirds of the transverse colon.
[C34212] Morula -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An early stage in embryonic development, that occurs between the zygote and the blastocyst stage during the first week post fertilization, and is characterized by the formation of a solid mass of cells (blastomeres) with a "mulberry" appearance.
[C34213] Mucous Connective Tissue -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A gelatinous type of connective tissue that is found in the umbilical cord.  **Synonyms:** - Wharton's Jelly
[C34214] Myotome Region -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The region of the dermomyotome that will form myoblasts (primordial muscle cells).
[C34215] Nail Fold -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Folds of epidermis surrounding the embryonic nail fields, with the proximal fold eventually keratinizing to form the nail plate.
[C34216] Nail Plate -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The hard, outer surface of the nail composed of keratinized squamous cells, which arise from the proximal nail fold.
[C34217] Nasal Prominence -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Lateral and medial horseshoe-shaped mesenchymal elevations surrounding the embryonic nasal placodes that arise from a division of the frontonasal prominence and contribute to the formation of the nose.
[C34218] Nasolacrimal Groove -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A cleft located between the lateral nasal process and the adjacent maxillary process, which is seen during early embryonic face development, and which gives rise to the nasolacrimal duct.
[C34219] Nephrogenic Cord -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** One of a pair of laterally positioned longitudinal masses of nephrogenic mesoderm on the urogenital ridge that is found in the early embryo, and which gives rise to the urinary system.  **Synonyms:** - Nephrogenic Ridge
[C3422] Trophoblastic Tumor -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A gestational or non-gestational neoplasm characterized by excessive proliferation of trophoblastic cells.  **Synonyms:** - Trophoblastic Neoplasm - Trophoblastic Neoplasms - Trophoblastic neoplasms
[C34220] Nerve of the Pharyngeal Arch -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Any of the four cranial nerves or their branches innervating the pharyngeal arches, including the Trigeminal nerve (CN V), the Facial nerve (CN VII), the Glossopharyngeal nerve (CN IX) and the Vagus nerve (CN X).  **Synonyms:** - Nerve of Pharyngeal Arch
[C34221] Neural Canal -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The central lumen of the embryonic neural tube, which gives rise to the ventricular system of the brain and central canal of the spinal cord.
[C34222] Neural Crest -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A cell region originating at the margins of the dorsal part of the neural tube during the early stages of embryonic development that migrates to multiple locations within the embryo, and are multipotent, giving rise to a vast number of differentiated cell and tissue types.
[C34223] Neural Fold -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Two longitudinal elevations derived from the embryonic neural plate that fuse to form the neural tube.
[C34224] Neural Groove -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A narrow, elongated depression on the embryonic neural plate, located between the neural folds, that, when the neural folds unite at the midline, will deepen to form the neural canal.
[C34225] Neural Plate -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A thickened area of ectoderm on the posterior aspect of the trilaminar embryo that will give rise to the nervous system.
[C34226] Neural Tube -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A cylindrical structure formed by the longitudinal closure of the neural folds in the early embryo that will later differentiate into the brain and spinal cord.
[C34227] Neurenteric Canal -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A small, temporary passage between the amniotic cavity and yolk sac arising from the proximal part of the notochordal canal in the early embryo, which obliterates at the completion of the notochord.
[C34228] Neuroectoderm -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The dorsal surface of the early vertebrate embryo that gives rise to the nervous system.  **Synonyms:** - Ectomesenchyme
[C34229] Neurula -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An stage in embryonic development during which neurulation takes place, and which follows the gastrula stage.
[C3423] Tuberculosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A chronic, recurrent infection caused by the bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Tuberculosis (TB) may affect almost any tissue or organ of the body with the lungs being the most common site of infection. The clinical stages of TB are primary or initial infection, latent or dormant infection, and recrudescent or adult-type TB. Ninety to 95% of primary TB infections may go unrecognized. Histopathologically, tissue lesions consist of granulomas which usually undergo central caseation necrosis. Local symptoms of TB vary according to the part affected; acute symptoms include hectic fever, sweats, and emaciation; serious complications include granulomatous erosion of pulmonary bronchi associated with hemoptysis. If untreated, progressive TB may be associated with a high degree of mortality. This infection is frequently observed in immunocompromised individuals with AIDS or a history of illicit IV drug use.  **Synonyms:** - Active Tuberculosis - TB - TB - TB - Tuberculosis Disease - tuberculosis
[C34230] Notochordal Canal -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The central lumen of the embryonic notochordal process that is contiguous with the amnion through the primitive pit, and which has a proximal portion that temporarily persists as the neurenteric canal.
[C34231] Notochordal Plate -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A thickened epithelial layer of cells, which develops from the notochordal process, that is contiguous with the embryonic endoderm forming a roof to the secondary yolk sac, and giving rise to the notochord.
[C34232] Notochordal Process -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An embryonic tube-like structure of mesodermal origin that is located between the endoderm and neuroectoderm, extending cranially in the midline from the primitive node, and is the rudiment of the notochord.
[C34233] Optic Cup -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A double walled invagination of the optical vesicles in the early embryo, which forms the neural retina from its inner wall, the retinal pigment epithelium from its outer wall, and the ciliary body and iris from its rim.
[C34234] Optic Groove -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A pair of sulci appearing in the embryonic cranial neural folds and the first indication of eye development, which will evaginate to form the optic vesicles.
[C34235] Optic Stalk -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A paired narrow neural tube extension connecting the optic vesicles with the forebrain during early neural development of the embryo, and which is the primordium of the optic nerve.
[C34236] Optic Vesicle -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** One of two small outpouchings formed from the evagination of the optic groove during early eye formation in the developing embryo.
[C34237] Other Embryologic Structure -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure
[C34238] Otic Pit -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An invagination of the otic placode during early embryonic development that will give rise to the otic vesicle and contribute to the formation the inner ear.
[C34239] Otic Placodes -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A patch of thickened ectoderm alongside the hindbrain in the early embryo that will give rise to the otic pit.
[C3424] Tuberous Sclerosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Hereditary disease characterized by seizures, mental retardation, developmental delay, and skin and ocular lesions. First signs usually occur during infancy or childhood but in rare cases may not occur until 2nd or 3rd decade.  **Synonyms:** - Bourneville's Disease - TSC - Tuberous Sclerosis Complex - Tuberous Sclerosis Syndrome - Tuberous sclerosis - tuberous sclerosis
[C34240] Otic Vesicle -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A simple epithelial sac-like structure formed by the deepening and closure of the otic pit during early embryonic development, which will eventually form the membranous labyrinth of the inner ear.
[C34241] Foramen Ovale of the Fetal Heart -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An opening between the right and left atria in the fetal heart that allows blood to bypass the lungs and flow directly into the systemic circulation.  **Synonyms:** - HEART, FORAMEN OVALE
[C34242] Pancreatic Bud -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Two outpouchings (a ventral and dorsal) arising from the caudal part of the foregut during early embryonic development that will merge to form the pancreas.
[C34243] Parachordal Cartilage -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The basal plate that forms around the cranial end of the notochord during early embryonic development, and which will eventually fuse across the midline to form the base of the occipital bone.
[C34244] Paraxial Mesoderm -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A thick, longitudinal column of intraembryonic mesoderm, located at either side of the notochord, that eventually forms paired somites.
[C34245] Pectinate Line -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An anatomical line of demarcation that separates the upper and lower part of the anal canal, both in structure and neurovascular supply, reflecting the different embryologic origin; superior to the line the epithelium is a mucous membrane like the rest of the gastrointestinal tract and inferiorly the epithelium is considered cutaneous, and is composed of stratified squamous keratinized with hair and sebaceous glands.  **Synonyms:** - Dentate Line
[C34246] Pericardioperitoneal Canal -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Two thin canals, located on either side of the foregut and dorsal to the septum transversum, that connect the thoracic and abdominal portions of the intraembryonic coelom, which subsequently forms the pleural cavities.
[C34247] Periderm -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The outermost and transient layer of the epidermis of the early embryo and fetus, existing into approximately the six month of gestation, that is composed of simple squamous epithelium.
[C34248] Pharyngeal Apparatus -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The collection of pharyngeal arches, pouches, clefts, and membranes present during early embryonic development that contribute to the formation of the face and neck.
[C34249] Pharyngeal Arch -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** One of a series of paired mesodermal tissue bands located on either side of the embryonic pharyngeal foregut, composed of an ectodermal covering, and endodermal lining, and a mesenchymal core, that contains an arch artery, a cranial nerve, a cartilaginous rod and a muscular component. Each arch differentiates to form different components of the head and neck.
[C3425] Tumor Lysis Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition of metabolic abnormalities that result from a spontaneous or therapy-related cytolysis of tumor cells. Tumor lysis syndrome typically occurs in aggressive, rapidly proliferating lymphoproliferative disorders. Burkitt lymphoma and T cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia are commonly associated with this syndrome. Metabolic abnormalities include hyperuricemia, lactic acidosis, hyperkalemia, hyperphosphatemia and hypocalcemia and may result in renal failure, multiple organ failure, and death.  **Synonyms:** - Tumor lysis syndrome - tumor lysis syndrome
[C34250] Pharyngeal Groove -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** One of a series of paired ectodermal clefts, located on either side of the embryonic pharyngeal foregut, that separate the pharyngeal arches. The first pharyngeal groove forms the external auditory meatus, while all other pharyngeal clefts are obliterated during development.
[C34251] Pharyngeal Membrane -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A double-layered, thin, membranous sheet, composed of ectoderm and endoderm, that separates the pharyngeal pouch from the pharyngeal groove in the developing embryo.
[C34252] Pharyngeal Pouch -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** One of a series of paired endodermal evaginations, located on either side of the embryonic pharyngeal foregut between the pharyngeal arches, that each differentiate to form different components of the head and neck.
[C34253] Placental Membrane -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A very thin layer of fetal connective tissue covering the placenta that functions to separate maternal blood from fetal blood.
[C34254] Placental Septum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A wedge-shaped area of decidual tissue that projects toward the chorionic plate and divides the fetal part of the placenta into cotyledons.  **Synonyms:** - Desidual Septum
[C34255] Pleuropericardial Membrane -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A membranous fold in the developing embryo separating the pleural and peritoneal cavities, which contains the cardinal vein and phrenic nerve.
[C34256] Pleuroperitoneal Membrane -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A membranous fold that forms inferiorly at the septum transversum in the developing embryo to separate the peritoneal cavity from the pleural cavity.
[C34257] Posterior Cardinal Vein -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Either of two veins occurring during embryonic development on both sides of the abdomen that drain the lower portion of the fetus into the common cardinal vein.
[C34258] Primordial Uteroplacental Circulation -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Blood flow from lacunar networks that establishes the maternal-placental circulation approximately at the end of the third week of gestation.
[C34259] Primary Brain Vesicle -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Any one of three expansions arising at the rostral end of the developing neural tube in the early embryo, which subsequently form the prosencephalon, mesencephalon and rhombencephalon.  **Synonyms:** - Primary Cerebral Vesicle
[C3426] Ulcer -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A circumscribed inflammatory and often suppurating lesion on the skin or an internal mucous surface resulting in necrosis of tissue.  **Synonyms:** - ULCER - Ulcerated - Ulceration - Ulceration - Ulcerative - Ulcers - ulcer
[C34260] Primary Bronchial Bud -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A left and right outpouching at the caudal end of the laryngotracheal diverticulum in the early embryo that subsequently differentiates into the bronchi and their branches in the mature lungs.
[C34261] Primary Chorionic Villus -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The initial stage of small, non-vascular projectile growth from the chorion that generally occurs between days thirteen to fifteen of gestation, and involves only cytotrophoblast.
[C34262] Primary Lymph Sac -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Any of six primary lymph sacs (two jugular lymph sacs, two iliac lymph sacs, one retroperitoneal lymph sac and one cisterna chyli) found during the embryonic period that are the first precursors of lymph node and lymph vessel development.
[C34263] Primary Ossification Center -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The central part of a developing bone in the fetus, and the first area of the bone that starts ossifying.
[C34264] Primary Palate -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An embryonic structure formed by the fusion of the maxillary processes with the nasal processes during early face formation, which, when coupled with the secondary palate, will form the definitive palate.
[C34265] Primitive Groove -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A narrow, longitudinal, midline fissure on the surface of the primitive streak, ending at the primitive pit, that results from the active involution of cells forming the primitive streak.
[C34266] Primitive Node -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The small circular, localized expansion of the cranial end of the primitive streak, which is centered around the primitive pit, and is where gastrulation takes place.
[C34267] Primitive Pit -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A small depression in the center of the primitive node, located at the cranial end of the primitive groove, and the site of cranial epiblast extension, forming the initial axial process.
[C34268] Primordial Gut -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Intraembryonic endoderm from the head, tail, and lateral folds of the early embryo that incorporate the dorsal part of the yolk sac, forming a tube-like structure extending from the buccopharyngeal membrane to the cloacal membrane, which will eventually differentiate into the digestive tract.
[C34269] Primordial Interventricular Septum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A median muscular ridge between the right and left ventricular masses found in the early embryo, which will form to the muscular interventricular septum.
[C3427] Ureter Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the ureter.  **Synonyms:** - Neoplasm of Ureter - Neoplasm of the Ureter - Neoplasms of Ureter - Neoplasms of the Ureter - Tumor of Ureter - Tumor of the Ureter - Ureter Tumor - Ureteral Neoplasm - Ureteral Tumor
[C34270] Primordial Laryngeal Inlet -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An opening between the laryngotracheal tube and the pharynx found in the early embryo, which will form to the definitive laryngeal inlet.
[C34271] Primordial Mediastinum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A mass of mesenchyme found in the early embryo that extends from the sternum to the primitive vertebral column, separating the developing lungs, and forming the definitive mediastinum.
[C34272] Primordial Myocardium -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A mass of splanchnic mesoderm enclosing the embryonic heart tube, which will become the muscular wall of the mature heart.
[C34273] Primordial Optic Nerve -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The optic stalk, which is the constricted, proximal portion of the optic vesicles found in the embryo, and in which the lumen gradually obliterates as axons of ganglion cells accumulate in the inner layer and form the optic nerve.
[C34274] Primordial Phallus -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The elongated genital tubercle that develops during the fourth week of gestation on the ventral side of the cloacal membrane, and which will give rise to either the glans penis or the clitoris.
[C34275] Primordium -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The earliest recognizable stage of development of an anatomical structure, tissue, organ or cell.
[C34276] Primordium of the Heart -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Splanchnic mesoderm located in the cardiogenic region of the trilaminar embryo that subsequently forms the primordial heart tube, and will ultimately give rise to the heart.  **Synonyms:** - Primordium of Heart
[C34277] Primordium of the Liver -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The cephalic portion of the hepatic diverticulum that invades the septum transversum during the third week of embryonic development, and which forms the parenchyma of the liver, while the hemopoietic cells, the Kupffer cells, and the connective tissue of the liver are derived from mesenchyme in the septum transversum.  **Synonyms:** - Primordium of Liver
[C34278] Proctodeum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The ectodermal lined depression at the caudal end of the alimentary canal of the developing embryo, which gives rise to the inferior third of the anal canal.  **Synonyms:** - Anal Pit
[C34279] Pronephric Duct -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An epithelial streak that arises in the cervical region of the developing embryo; is a precursor to the Wolffian duct, which connects the pronephric kidney to the urogenital sinus.
[C3428] Urethra Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the urethra.  Urethral carcinoma is a representative example.  **Synonyms:** - Neoplasm of Urethra - Neoplasm of the Urethra - Tumor of Urethra - Tumor of the Urethra - Urethra Tumor - Urethral Neoplasm - Urethral Tumor
[C34280] Pronephros -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The primordial kidney, which is the earliest stage in kidney development in the human embryo; it is composed of three structures: the pronephros duct, the pronephros tubules and the coelom, and is located in the cranial part of the nephrogenic cord.
[C34281] Rathke Pouch -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A tubular outgrowth of ectoderm from the stomodeum of the embryo; it grows dorsad toward the infundibular process of the diencephalon, around which it forms a cup-like mass, giving rise to the pars distalis and pars juxtaneuralis of the hypophysis.  **Synonyms:** - Craniopharyngeal Canal - Hypophyseal Diverticulum - Hypophyseal Pouch - Pituitary Diverticulum - Rathke's Diverticulum - Rathke's Pocket - Rathke's Pouch
[C34282] Respiratory Diverticulum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An endodermal evagination of the ventral wall of the foregut that will give rise to the trachea and the lung buds.
[C34283] Respiratory Primordium -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The laryngotracheal groove that extends lengthwise in the floor of the foregut just caudal to the pharyngeal pouches, and that appears during approximately the fourth week of gestation.
[C34284] Retinal Fissure -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A small groove in the ventral margin of the optic cup that temporarily occurs during development.  **Synonyms:** - Optic Fissure
[C34285] Retroperitoneal Lymph Sac -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Any one of six primary lymph sacs located at the root of the intestinal mesentery on the posterior abdominal wall in the developing fetus.
[C34286] Scalp Vascular Plexus -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A characteristic band with vascular features that is visible around the head of a developing fetus.
[C34287] Sclerotome -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The ventromedial part of a somite that differentiates to form the axial skeleton.
[C34288] Second Pharyngeal Arch -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The second of the six pharyngeal arch pairs, which are formed of mesodermal outpouchings and located on either side of the developing pharynx; structures formed from the second arch include the stapes, the temporal styloid process, the stylohyoid ligament, the lesser cornu of the hyoid bone, and multiple muscle groups, including the muscles of facial expression.  **Synonyms:** - Hyoid Arch
[C34289] Second Pharyngeal Pouch -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A paired evagination of embryonic pharyngeal endoderm, which is located between pharyngeal arches two and three, and which contributes to the formation of the middle ear and palatine tonsils.
[C3429] Incontinence -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Involuntary passage of stool or urine from the body.  **Synonyms:** - Urinary/Bowel incontinence - incontinence
[C34290] Secondary Brain Vesicle -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Subdivisions of the developing embryonic neural tube, including the telencephalon, diencephalon, mesencephalon, metencephalon and the myelencephalon, that are formed by the partition of the three primary brain vesicles.
[C34291] Secondary Chorionic Villus -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The second stage of chorionic villi development, which generally occurs during the third week of embryonic development, and is characterized by the appearance of a mesenchymal core within an expanding villus.
[C34292] Secondary Palate -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The portion of the embryonic palate that extends from the incisive foramen to the uvula, and is the primordium of the hard and soft divisions of the palate.
[C34293] Seminiferous Cord -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An embryonic structure derived from the gonadal cords, which is the primordia of the seminiferous tubules.
[C34294] Septum Primum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A wall of cardiac tissue that forms the initial, incomplete separation of the right and left atria during early heart development in the embryo.
[C34295] Septum Secundum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A thick growth on the atrial wall, located to the right of the initial septum primum in the early embryo; it forms an incomplete partition between the atria, leaving only an area at the posterosuperior part of the interatrial septum: the fossa ovalis.
[C34296] Septum Transversum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A ventro-dorsal plate of mesoderm, which arises from the coelomic wall of the caudal portion of the pericardial cavity, and which is located caudal to the pericardial cavity, extending from the ventral and lateral regions of the body wall to the foregut in the early embryo; it contributes to the formation of the liver, and it is the primordium of the central tendon of the diaphragm.
[C34297] Sinovaginal Bulb -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A pair of endodermal tissue swellings of the sinusal tubercle of the posterior urogenital sinus of the early embryo, which will ultimately form the lower third of the vagina.
[C34298] Sinus Tubercle -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A proliferation of endoderm on the dorsal wall of the urogenital sinus that is formed by the fusion of the paramesonephric ducts during embryonic development, and which will ultimately form the colliculus seminalis in the male and the hymen in the female.  **Synonyms:** - Muller Tubercle - Müller Tubercle
[C34299] Sinus Venarum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A smooth-walled, systemic venous sinus, formed by a portion of the sinus venosus, located in the internal wall of the right atrium in the embryonic heart.
[C343] Carbenicillin -- **Semantic type:** Antibiotic|Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A broad-spectrum, semi-synthetic penicillin antibiotic with bactericidal and beta-lactamase resistant activity. Carbenicillin acylates the penicillin-sensitive transpeptidase C-terminal domain by opening the lactam ring. This inactivation prevents the cross-linkage of peptidoglycan strands, thereby inhibiting the third and last stage of bacterial cell wall synthesis. This leads to incomplete bacterial cell wall synthesis and eventually causes cell lysis.  **Synonyms:** - CARBENICILLIN - CARBENICILLIN - Geopen - Geopen - Pyopen - Pyopen
[C3430] Urinary System Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder affecting the urinary system.  **Synonyms:** - Disorder of Urinary System - Urologic Disorder - urinary tract disorder
[C34300] Sinus Venosus -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A cavity occurring during embryonic development adjacent to the embryonic heart where major embryonic veins drain. The sinus venosus becomes part of the right atrium and vena cava in the adult.
[C34301] Somatopleure -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A tissue layer of the early embryo comprising ectoderm and somatic mesoderm that fuse to form the amnion, the chorion, and part of the body wall.
[C34302] Somite -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A developmentally transient structure comprising paired cuboidal bodies formed from paraxial mesoderm on either side of the developing neural tube, with subsequent pairs forming in a craniocaudal sequence; these structures ultimately differentiate into muscle, bone, and connective tissues of the body.
[C34303] Splanchnopleure -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A tissue layer of the early embryo comprised by the union of endoderm with splanchnic mesoderm, which ultimately forms the connective tissue and muscles of the digestive tube.
[C34304] Stapedial Artery -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An artery occurring during embryonic development that runs through the ring of the stapes then branches to form the supraorbital, infraorbital, and mandibular arteries.
[C34305] Stomach Primordium -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A fusiform enlargement, oriented along the median plane, of the caudal portion of the foregut in the early embryo, which will ultimately form the stomach.
[C34306] Stomodeum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The primordial mouth in the early embryo, which initially appears as a slight depression of the surface ectoderm, between the maxillary and mandibular components of the first pharyngeal arch.  **Synonyms:** - Primordial Mouth
[C34307] Subcardinal Vein -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The paired ventromedial blood vessels occurring in the abdomen during embryonic development.
[C34308] Supracardinal Vein -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The paired dorsomedial blood vessels occurring in the abdomen during embryonic development.
[C34309] Surface Ectoderm -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The outermost part of the ectoderm, making it the most superficial of three primary germ layers in the early embryo. It differentiates to form the epidermis and its appendages, the stratified epithelia, the cells that form the lens and cornea of the eye, and the dental enamel.
[C3431] Urinary System Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant, primary or metastatic neoplasm involving the urinary system (kidney, ureter, bladder, and urethra).  **Synonyms:** - Neoplasm of Urinary System - Neoplasm of the Urinary System - Tumor of Urinary System - Tumor of the Urinary System - Urinary System Tumor
[C34310] Tertiary Chorionic Villus -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A stage in the development of the chorionic villi that occurs at approximately 21 days gestation, and which is characterized by the vascularization of the chorionic villi.
[C34311] Third Pharyngeal Pouch -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A paired evagination of embryonic pharyngeal endoderm located between pharyngeal arches three and four, which contributes to the formation of both the inferior parathyroid gland and the thymus gland.
[C34312] Thyroid Primordium -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The precursor to the thyroid gland, originating as an endodermal proliferation at the tip of the foramen cecum of the developing tongue at approximately week four of embryonic development.
[C34313] Tooth Bud -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The primordial structure of a tooth, which first appears as a center of cell proliferation on the dental lamina of the developing embryo.
[C34314] Tracheal Bud -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The primordium of the respiratory system, which begins as an outgrowth at the distal end of the laryngotracheal diverticulum, located ventral to the caudal part of the foregut, and which is ultimately formed by the evagination of the laryngotracheal groove in the early embryo.
[C34315] Tracheoesophageal Fold -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Two longitudinal folds in the laryngotracheal diverticulum of the early embryo, which approach each other and fuse to form the tracheoesophageal septum.
[C34316] Tracheoesophageal Septum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A partition in the laryngotracheal diverticulum formed by the union of the two tracheoesophageal folds, which divides the cranial part of the foregut of the early embryo into a ventral and a dorsal part.
[C34317] Trancus Arteriosus -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A tubular outflow structure that forms at the cranial end of the single heart tube and connects it to the aortic arch arteries in the developing embryo; it is the primordium of the ascending aorta and pulmonary trunk.
[C34318] Tubotympanic Recess -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A small cavity, formed by the elongated expansion of the embryonic first endodermal pharyngeal pouch, which will give rise to the pharyngotympanic tube (from the proximal part) and the tympanic cavity (from the distal part).
[C34319] Umbilical Blood -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Blood that is retained in the umbilical cord after the delivery of a baby; it is rich in hematopoietic stem cells, and can be used for bone marrow reconstitution.
[C3432] Urticaria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A transient, itchy skin eruption characterized by wheals with pale interiors and red margins.  **Synonyms:** - Hives - Hives - hives - urticaria
[C34320] Umbilical Cord -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** Extraembryonic structure that connects the fetus to the placenta. It contains two arteries and one vein.  **Synonyms:** - UMBILICAL CORD - chorda umbilicalis - funiculus umbilicalis - umbilical cord
[C34321] Urogenital Ridge -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** An elongated, mesenchymal structure that lies on both sides of the dorsal mesentery of the early embryo; it will ultimately for parts of both the urinary and genital systems.
[C34322] Urogenital Sinus -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The ventral compartment of the cloaca following its division from the urogenital septum; it will ultimately form the majority of the bladder, the entire urethra in females, part of the urethra in males, and the vestibule of the vagina in females.
[C34323] Urorectal Septum -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A wedge of mesenchymal tissue, found in the early embryo, that acts as a partition to divide the cloaca into the primitive urogenital sinus (ventrally) and the anorectal canal of the hindgut (dorsally).
[C34324] Uterovaginal Primordium -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The fused segment of the Y-shaped ductal structure that is formed by the union of the paramesonephric ducts during early embryonic development; it will form both the uterus and the superior part of the vagina.
[C34325] Vaginal Plate -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A solid core of tissue that is formed by the fusion of the sinovaginal bulbs in the early embryo, and which is the precursor to the inferior portion of the vagina.
[C34326] Valve of the Oval Foramen -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A small flap of tissue, which is a remnant of the septum primum, that covers the foramen ovale in the developing fetus.  **Synonyms:** - Valve of Oval Foramen
[C34327] Ventral Mesogastrium -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The portion of the embryonic mesentery that is attached to the ventral abdominal wall, extending from the caudal foregut to the upper part of the duodenum. The liver develops within it, and it will give rise to the lesser omentum and falciform ligament.
[C34328] Vernix Caseosa -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** A white cheese-like protective material that covers the skin of a fetus.  **Synonyms:** - Vernix
[C34329] Vitelline Vein -- **Semantic type:** Embryonic Structure  **Definition:** The veins occurring during embryonic development that drain the yolk sack.
[C3433] Urticaria Pigmentosa/Maculopapular Cutaneous Mastocytosis -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** The most frequent form of cutaneous mastocytosis.  In children, the lesions tend to be papular, and are characterized by aggregates of elongated or spindle-shaped mast cells which fill the papillary dermis and extend into the reticular dermis.  In adults, the lesions tend to have fewer mast cells compared to those in children.  The lesions are located most commonly on the trunk, but they can be seen on the extremities, head and neck. (WHO, 2001)  **Synonyms:** - UP/MPCM - Urticaria Pigmentosa - Urticaria pigmentosa
[C34330] Inactive Tyrosine-Protein Kinase 7 -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein  **Definition:** Inactive tyrosine-protein kinase 7 (1070 aa, ~118 kDa) is encoded by the human PTK7 gene. This protein is involved in both signal transduction and cellular morphology.  **Synonyms:** - CCK-4 - CCK4 - Colon Carcinoma Kinase 4 - Colon Carcinoma Kinase-4 - PTK7 - PTK7 Protein Tyrosine Kinase 7 - Protein-Tyrosine Kinase PTK7 - Pseudo Tyrosine Kinase Receptor 7 - Tyrosine-Protein Kinase-Like 7
[C34331] Abdominal Cramp -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** An involuntary muscular contraction involving a muscle of the abdomen or of a hollow organ within the abdomen.  **Synonyms:** - Abdominal Cramps
[C34332] Abdominal Injury -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Trauma to the abdomen.
[C34333] Abdominal Mass -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An abnormal growth in the abdomen.
[C34334] Abnormal Hard Tissue Formation in Pulp -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Development of hard tissue in the inner substance of the tooth where blood vessels, lymphatic, and nerve tissue is normally found.
[C34336] Spontaneous Abortion -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Fetal loss at less than 20 weeks of gestation.  **Synonyms:** - Miscarriage - Miscarriage - Miscarriage
[C34337] Illegal Abortion -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Unlawfully induced expulsion of all products of conception.  **Synonyms:** - Criminal Abortion
[C34338] Therapeutic Abortion Finding -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An abortion performed on the individual due to a harmful medical condition caused by pregnancy.  **Synonyms:** - Therapeutic Abortion
[C34339] Legally Induced Abortion -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Lawfully induced expulsion of all products of conception.
[C3434] Uterine Corpus Leiomyoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign smooth muscle neoplasm arising from the body of the uterus. It is characterized by the presence of spindle cells with cigar-shaped nuclei, interlacing fascicles, and a whorled pattern.  **Synonyms:** - Body of Uterus Fibroid - Body of Uterus Leiomyoma - Corpus Uteri Fibroid - Corpus Uteri Leiomyoma - Fibroid of Body of Uterus - Fibroid of Corpus Uteri - Fibroid of Uterine Body - Fibroid of Uterine Corpus - Fibroid of the Body of Uterus - Fibroid of the Corpus Uteri - Fibroid of the Uterine Body - Fibroid of the Uterine Corpus - Fibroid uterus - Fibromyoma - Leiomyofibroma - Leiomyoma of Body of Uterus - Leiomyoma of Corpus Uteri - Leiomyoma of Uterine Body - Leiomyoma of Uterine Corpus - Leiomyoma of the Body of Uterus - Leiomyoma of the Corpus Uteri - Leiomyoma of the Uterine Body - Leiomyoma of the Uterine Corpus - Uterine Body Fibroid - Uterine Body Leiomyoma - Uterine Corpus Fibroid - Uterine Corpus Leiomyomata - Uterine Fibroid - Uterine Fibroids
[C34340] Accident -- **Semantic type:** Phenomenon or Process  **Definition:** An unforeseen and unplanned event or circumstance frequently causing loss or injury.  **Synonyms:** - Accidental
[C34341] Aircraft Accident -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An accident taking place aboard, or involving, any type of aircraft.
[C34342] Domestic Accident -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An accident taking place within a home.
[C34343] Industrial Accident -- **Semantic type:** Injury or Poisoning  **Definition:** An accident taking place on the site of a commercial enterprise.
[C34344] Automobile Accident -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An accident occurring while aboard, or involving, an automobile.  **Synonyms:** - Motor Vehicle Accident
[C34345] Achondroplasia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An autosomal dominant disorder caused by mutation(s) in the FGFR3 gene, encoding fibroblast growth factor receptor 3. The condition is characterized by inappropriate cartilage growth plate differentiation and deficient endochondral growth, manifest clinically with severe rhizomelic short stature, short limbs, characteristic facies with frontal bossing and midface hypoplasia.
[C34346] Acne Keloid -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A chronic eruption of fibrous papules that develop and fuse to form a thick sclerotic, hypertrophic band at a site of deep folliculitis, usually along the posterior hairline of the scalp. It is most commonly seen in men of African descent.  **Synonyms:** - Keloidal Folliculitis
[C34347] Acquired Coagulation Factor Deficiency -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Deficiency of a coagulation factor that is not caused by genetic alterations. Causes include vitamin K deficiency, amyloidosis, and severe liver disease.  **Synonyms:** - Acquired coagulation factor deficiency
[C34348] Acrocephalosyndactyly -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A genetic disorder characterized by craniosynostosis and fusion of the fingers and toes.
[C34349] Actinomycetoma -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A chronic granulomatous inflammation involving the deep dermis and the subcutaneous tissues. It is caused by Actinomycetes bacteria.
[C3435] Uterine Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the uterine corpus or the cervix. Representative examples of benign neoplasms include leiomyoma, adenomyoma, and endocervical polyp. Representative examples of malignant neoplasms include endometrial carcinoma, carcinosarcoma, and cervical carcinoma.  **Synonyms:** - Neoplasm of Uterus - Neoplasm of the Uterus - Tumor of Uterus - Tumor of the Uterus - Uterine Neoplasms - Uterine Tumor
[C34350] Actinomycosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An infectious process caused by bacteria of the Actinomyces species. It is characterized by the formation of purulent and painful abscesses in the mouth, lungs and gastrointestinal tract.
[C34351] Cervicofacial Actinomycotic Infection -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Actinomycotic infection of the face and hands.
[C34352] Acute Alcoholic Hepatitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Acute hepatitis resulting from ingestion of alcohol.
[C34353] Acute Atopic Conjunctivitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Atopic conjunctivitis that is of relatively short duration and that has a rapid onset.
[C34354] Acute Periodontitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An acute inflammatory process that affects the tissues that surround and support the teeth.
[C34355] Acute Pharyngitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An acute and painful inflammatory process that affects the pharynx.  It is usually caused by viruses and less often bacteria. Signs and symptoms include discomfort on swallowing, low-grade fever, headache, and earache.
[C34356] Acute Intestinal Ischemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Ischemia of the intestine that is rapid in onset.
[C34357] Holmes-Adie Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare syndrome characterized by an abnormally dilated pupil, hypoflexia, and diaphoresis. The syndrome is usually caused by a viral or bacterial infection. The abnormally dilated pupil is caused by damage to postganglionic parasympathetic fibers innervating the eye.  **Synonyms:** - Adie Pupil - Adie Syndrome - Tonic Pupil
[C34358] Adjustment Reaction with Brief Depressive Reaction -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** Temporary maladaptive depressive signs and symptoms associated with a life event.
[C34359] Adnexitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the appendages of the uterus.  **Synonyms:** - Uterine Adnexitis
[C3436] Uveal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the uvea. Representative examples are nevi and melanomas.  **Synonyms:** - Neoplasm of Uvea - Neoplasm of the Uvea - Tumor of Uvea - Tumor of the Uvea - Uvea Neoplasm - Uvea Tumor - Uveal Tract Neoplasm - Uveal Tumor
[C34360] Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A genetic disorder characterized by defects in the synthesis of cortisol and/or aldosterone, resulting in hyperplasia of the adrenal cortical cells.  **Synonyms:** - Adrenal Hyperplasia - Adrenal Hyperplasia, Congenital - Adrenogenital Disorder - Adrenogenital Syndrome - Congenital Adrenal Gland Hyperplasia
[C34361] Affective Psychosis -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** The manifestation of psychotic symptoms, such as hallucinations or delusions, in the presence of a mood disorder.
[C34362] Agoraphobia -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** An anxiety disorder characterized by an intense, irrational fear of venturing out into open places or situations in which help (or escape) might not be available should excessive anxiety or panic symptoms develop.
[C34363] Agoraphobia without a History of Panic Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** An anxiety disorder characterized by agoraphobia in the absence of a history of panic attacks; the individual fears incapacitation or humiliation in open, public places or situations due to panic-like symptoms rather than a full-blown panic attack.
[C34364] Air Sickness -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** A sensation of discomfort associated with air travel that may include nausea, vomiting, dizziness, or sweating.
[C34365] Alastrim -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A orthopoxvirus that causes a milder clinical syndrome than smallpox.
[C34366] Alcohol-Induced Wernicke-Korsakoff's Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A syndrome which occurs in individuals with a history of alcohol abuse. It is caused by prolonged alcohol-induced neurological damage and malnutrition including vitamin and electrolyte deficiencies. Clinical signs include altered mental status, visual impairment and decreased muscle coordination. The clinical course varies and is, in part, dependent upon the severity of symptoms at presentation. The prognosis is poor and worsens if alcohol abuse continues.  **Synonyms:** - Alcohol-Induced Amnestic Syndrome - Alcoholic Korsakoff's Psychosis
[C34367] Alcohol Intoxication -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Disturbances in psychophysiological functions and responses as a result of  consumption of a beverage containing ethanol.
[C34368] Alpha Thalassemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A genetic hematologic disorder characterized by partial or complete absence of the alpha globin chains of the heme molecule.
[C34369] Extrinsic Allergic Alveolitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inflammatory interstitial lung disease caused by hypersensitivity reaction to inhalation or ingestion of antigens.  The antigens are usually related to the patient's occupation.  It can present as an acute illness with flu-like symptoms, subacute with repeated episodes of pneumonia, or chronic with dyspnea and productive cough.  The majority of patients recover following the cessation of the exposure to the antigen that causes the disease.  Chronic exposure may eventually progress to interstitial lung fibrosis.  **Synonyms:** - Allergic Pneumonitis - Hypersensitivity Pneumonitis
[C3437] Vaginal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm affecting the vagina. Representative examples of benign neoplasms include squamous papilloma and melanocytic nevus. Representative examples of malignant neoplasms include carcinoma, melanoma, and sarcoma.  **Synonyms:** - Neoplasm of Vagina - Neoplasm of the Vagina - Tumor of Vagina - Tumor of the Vagina - Vagina Neoplasm - Vagina Tumor - Vaginal Tumor
[C34370] Amelia -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A congenital malformation characterized by the complete absence of all limbs.
[C34371] Ametropia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An optical condition in the eye where there is an error of refraction of light rays on the retina.
[C34372] Retrograde Amnesia -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** The loss of access to memories that were previously encoded; this disorder is most commonly preceded by trauma, including physical brain injury, stroke, or seizure, but may also be psychogenic in origin. Memory loss may be temporary or permanent, but the ability to encode new memories or skills is not generally affected.
[C34373] Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A neurodegenerative disorder characterized by progressive degeneration of the motor neurons of the central nervous system. It results in weakness and atrophy of the muscles which leads to an inability to initiate and control voluntary movements.  **Synonyms:** - ALS - Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis - Lou Gehrig Disease
[C34374] Ancylostomiasis due to Ancylostoma Duodenale -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Ancylostomiasis that is caused by infection by Ancylostoma Duodenale.
[C34375] Beta Thalassemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An autosomal recessive disorder that affects the production of beta polypeptide chains which are necessary for the hemoglobin synthesis. Anemia and failure to thrive are characteristic features.  **Synonyms:** - Thalassemia Major
[C34376] Hemolytic Anemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Anemia resulting from the premature destruction of the peripheral blood red cells. It may be congenital or it may be caused by infections, medications, or malignancies.
[C34377] Acquired Hemolytic Anemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Hemolytic anemia, the cause of which is not present at birth.
[C34378] Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An acquired anemia caused by destruction of the red blood cells by autoantibodies. Causes include autoimmune disorders, lymphoproliferative disorders, and infections.  **Synonyms:** - autoimmune hemolytic anemia
[C34379] Hereditary Hemolytic Anemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A congenital hemolytic anemia caused by defects of the erythrocyte membrane, enzyme deficiencies, or hemoglobinopathies.  **Synonyms:** - Congenital Hemolytic Anemia
[C3438] Vascular Hamartoma -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A benign focal growth composed of vascular tissue.
[C34380] Hypochromic Anemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Anemia caused by the reduction of hemoglobin in relation to the red cell volume. As a result, the red cells have an area of central pallor which is increased in size. The leading cause is iron deficiency.
[C34381] Macrocytic Anemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Anemia that is characterized by increased red blood cell volume.
[C34382] Megaloblastic Anemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Anemia characterized by the presence of unusually large erythroblasts in the bone marrow called megaloblasts. It is usually caused by vitamin B12 or folic acid deficiency. Other causes include toxins and drugs.
[C34383] Sickle Cell Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A blood disorder characterized by the appearance of sickle-shaped red blood cells and anemia.  **Synonyms:** - Sickle Cell Anemia - Sickle Cell Anemia - Sickle-cell Disorders - Sickling Disorder due to Hemoglobin S - sickle cell anemia - sickle cell disease
[C34384] Anemia due to Disorder of Glutathione Metabolism -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Anemia resulting from an abnormality of glutathione deficiency.
[C34385] Anhidrosis -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** Lack of sweating or the ability to sweat when provoked by the appropriate stimulus.  **Synonyms:** - Adiaphoresis
[C34386] Anomia -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Impaired ability to retrieve words; in particular, an inability to recall the names of objects and people.  **Synonyms:** - Nominal Aphasia
[C34387] Anorexia Nervosa -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A disorder most often seen in adolescent females characterized by a refusal to maintain a minimally normal body weight, an intense fear of gaining weight, a disturbance in body image, and, in postmenarcheal females, the development of amenorrhea.  **Synonyms:** - Anorexia nervosa - anorexia nervosa
[C34388] Anovulation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The absence of ovulation.
[C34389] Anthracosilicosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Fibrosis of the lung parenchyma caused by inhalation of carbon and silica dust.  It manifests as shortness of breath.
[C3439] Viral Infection -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Any disease caused by a virus.  **Synonyms:** - Other viral infection - Viral Disease - Viral Disease - Viral Disorder
[C34390] Anthracosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A chronic lung disorder characterized by deposition of coal dust in the lung parenchyma leading to the formation of black nodules and emphysema. It occurs in coal miners.  **Synonyms:** - Coal Miner's Pneumoconiosis - Coalworker's pneumoconiosis
[C34391] Aortic Arch Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A syndrome resulting from structural defects of the arteries that arise from the aortic arch. Signs and symptoms include weakness, dizziness, arm numbness, blurred vision and transient ischemic attacks.
[C34392] Aphakia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** The absence of the crystalline lens of the eye.
[C34393] Aphasia -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Impairment of the expression or comprehension of verbal and written language.
[C34394] Broca's Aphasia -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Aphasia characterized by impairment of expressive language due to injury to the motor association cortex in the frontal lobe.  **Synonyms:** - Motor Aphasia
[C34395] Arthropod-Borne Hemorrhagic Fever -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A group of viral illnesses that are vectored by arthropods, and that are characterized by increased susceptibility to bleeding diatheses.
[C34396] Arthropod-Borne Viral Infection -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A viral infection that is transmitted by an arthropod.  **Synonyms:** - Arbovirus Infection
[C34397] Arm Pain -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** A sensation of discomfort emanating from the humerus or its supporting structures.
[C34398] Arteriosclerosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A vascular disorder characterized by thickening and hardening of the walls of the arteries.  **Synonyms:** - Arterial Sclerosis - Arteriosclerosis/ Atherosclerosis - Vascular Sclerosis
[C34399] Arteritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inflammatory process affecting an artery.  **Synonyms:** - Arteritis, unspecified
[C344] Carbohydrate -- **Semantic type:** Chemical Viewed Structurally|Organic Chemical  **Definition:** A class of hydrocarbons with an empirical formula of Cm(H20)n (where m and n may be equal) and includes monomers, polymers and derivatives of aldoses, ketoses, saccharides, and alditols.  **Synonyms:** - Carbohydrates - carbohydrate
[C3440] Vocal Cord Polyp -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic polypoid swelling of the vocal cord mucosa.  It is usually unilateral and caused by excessive use of the voice.  **Synonyms:** - Polyp of Vocal Cord - Polyp of the Vocal Cord
[C34400] Arthus Reaction -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A localized vasculitis resulting from deposition of antibody-antigen complexes.  **Synonyms:** - Arthus Phenomenon
[C34401] Aschoff Body -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A small inflammatory nodule found in cardiac muscle.
[C34402] Peritoneal Effusion -- **Semantic type:** Finding
[C34403] Arteriosclerotic Cardiovascular Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Cardiovascular disease resulting from arteriosclerosis.
[C34404] Aseptic Necrosis of Head and Neck of Femur -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Aseptic necrosis involving the head and neck of the femur.
[C34405] Sports Injury -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An injury sustained while practicing or competing in a sport.
[C34406] Avitaminosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome
[C34407] Back Injury -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Trauma to the posterior trunk.
[C34408] Background Diabetic Retinopathy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome
[C34409] Bagassosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An occupational lung disorder caused by inhalation of bagasse dust.  In the acute phase, it manifests as cough, dyspnea, fever, chills, and weakness.  Chronic exposure may lead to interstitial lung fibrosis.
[C3441] Voice Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A pathologic process in the larynx that affects the production of speech.  Causes include vocal cord paresis, vocal cord nodule, vocal cord polyp, and laryngitis.
[C34410] Baritosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare type of pneumoconiosis caused by long standing exposure to barium dust.  It is characterized by the formation of fine dense lesions in the lung parenchyma.  The lesions do not affect the lung function and disappear without treatment after the exposure to barium dust stops.
[C34411] Cervicocranial Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A neurologic syndrome following injury of the spinal sympathetic nerves of the neck. The injury usually results from arthritis or pinching by the adjacent vertebrae. Symptoms include facial pain, chronic allergies, dizziness, neck pain, ear pain and vertigo.  **Synonyms:** - Barre-Lieou Syndrome - Posterior Cervical Sympathetic Syndrome
[C34412] Bartter Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare inherited syndrome characterized by juxtaglomerular cell hyperplasia, hyperaldosteronism, hypokalemia, and alkalosis. Patients have high levels of plasma renin concentration which is not associated with hypertension.  **Synonyms:** - Bartter's Syndrome - Hypokalemic Alkalosis
[C34413] Basilar Artery Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A syndrome which occurs as a result of the occlusion of the basilar artery. It may be caused by atherosclerosis, embolism or hemorrhage. Clinical signs include dizziness, headache, vomiting, hemiparesis or hemiplegia, dysarthria, dysphagia, blurred vision and loss of consciousness. The clinical course is variable and is dependent upon the extent of the occlusion and the location of the clot along the basilar artery which determines the resultant neurologic impairment. Prognosis is dismal in cases where a complete occlusion occurs with rapid deterioration of neurological function.
[C34414] Basophilia -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** Abnormally high level of basophils in the blood.  **Synonyms:** - BASOPHILIA
[C34415] Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A genetic syndrome caused by abnormalities in chromosome 11. It is characterized by large birth weight, macroglossia, umbilical hernia, ear abnormalities, and hypoglycemia. Patients with this syndrome have an increased risk of developing embryonal tumors (gonadoblastoma, hepatoblastoma, Wilms tumor, rhabdomyosarcoma) and adrenal cortex carcinomas.  **Synonyms:** - BWS - Beckwith-Wiedemann - Beckwith-Wiedemann Spectrum - Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome/Isolated Hemihyperplasia - Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome
[C34416] Behcet Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare chronic inflammatory disorder of unknown etiology. It is characterized by the development of ulcers in the mouth and genital region and uveitis. Other signs and symptoms include arthritis, deep vein thrombosis and superficial thrombophlebitis.  **Synonyms:** - Behcet Disease - Behcet's Disease - Behcet's Syndrome - Behcet's Syndrome - Behcet's disease - Behçet Disease - Behçet Syndrome - Behçet's Syndrome - Behçet-Adamantiades Syndrome - Morbus Behçet's Syndrome - Silk Road Disease
[C34417] Benign Mandible Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A non-metastasizing neoplasm that arises from the mandible.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Neoplasm of Lower Jaw Bone - Benign Neoplasm of Lower Jaw Bone - Benign Neoplasm of Mandible

[C34419] Berry Aneurysm -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A small cerebral artery aneurysm, characterized by a saccular appearance, that typically forms at the junction of vessels in the circle of Willis.  **Synonyms:** - Saccular Aneurysm
[C3442] Vomiting -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Forceful ejection of the contents of the stomach through the mouth.  **Synonyms:** - Emesis - Emesis - emesis - vomit
[C34420] Extrahepatic Cholestasis -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Impairment of the bile flow caused by an obstruction in the portion of the bile duct system located outside of the liver.  **Synonyms:** - Extrahepatic Bile Duct Obstruction - Extrahepatic Biliary Stasis
[C34421] Biliary Atresia -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A congenital disorder characterized by blockage or absence of the intrahepatic or extrahepatic bile ducts.  **Synonyms:** - Congenital Biliary Atresia - Congenital Biliary Atresia - Congenital absence of bile duct
[C34422] Binocular Vision Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Any inability to efficiently utilize and/or sustain binocular vision.
[C34423] Bipolar Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A disorder of the brain that causes unusual shifts in mood, energy, activity levels and the ability to carry out day-to-day tasks. Often these moods range and shift from periods of elation and energized behavior to those of hopelessness and depression.  **Synonyms:** - Bipolar disorder, unspecified - Manic Depressive Disorder - Manic-Depression
[C34424] Bipolar Depression -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** The depressive stage of bipolar disorder.
[C34425] Bird Fancier's Lung -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Hypersensitivity granulomatous pneumonitis caused by the inhalation of avian antigens that are present in the dust of the droppings and feathers of many species of birds.  In the acute phase it manifests as fever, chills, dyspnea, cough, and chest tightness.  Chronic exposure may lead to interstitial lung fibrosis.  **Synonyms:** - Avian Hypersensitivity Pneumonitis - Bird fancier's lung - Bird-Breeder's Lung - Pigeon-Breeder's Lung
[C34426] Blackwater Fever -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A complication of malaria resulting from hemolysis.  **Synonyms:** - Malarial Hemoglobinuria
[C34427] Blast Injury -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The result of physical trauma sustained in an explosion.
[C34428] Blastomycosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A fungal infection caused by inhalation of spores of Blastomyces dermatitidis. It presents with flu-like symptoms including fever, chills, cough, pleuritic chest pain and myalgias. It may lead to a chronic granulomatous pulmonary infection and disseminate to other anatomic sites including skin, nervous system and bones.
[C34429] North American Blastomycosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A pulmonary disease resulting from infection with Blastomyces dermatitidis, which is prevalent in North America.  **Synonyms:** - Blastomyces Dermatitidis Infection
[C3443] Vulvar Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign, precancerous, or malignant neoplasm that affects the vulva. Representative examples include Bartholin gland adenoma, vulvar nodular hidradenoma, vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia, vulvar carcinoma, and vulvar melanoma.  **Synonyms:** - Neoplasm of Vulva - Neoplasm of the Vulva - Tumor of Vulva - Tumor of the Vulva - Vulva Neoplasm - Vulva Tumor - Vulvar Tumor
[C34430] Blepharoconjunctivitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of both the eyelids and the conjunctiva.
[C34431] Blind Loop Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder affecting the small intestine. It is caused by the stasis of food and subsequent overgrowth of bacteria in a portion of the small intestine that is unintentionally bypassed as a complication of abdominal surgery or as a sequela of gastrointestinal disorders which impede effective motility. Clinical signs include bloating, abdominal pain, diarrhea and weight loss. If untreated, the clinical course progresses to malabsorption of fats, vitamin B12 and calcium, the latter which predisposes to nephrolithiasis and osteoporosis.  **Synonyms:** - Stasis (Blind Loop) Syndrome - Stasis Syndrome
[C34432] Bone Development Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Any disorder of development of the bone.
[C34433] Bone Marrow Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the bone marrow.  **Synonyms:** - Bone Marrow Disease
[C34434] Bonnevie-Ullrich Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A genetic syndrome which occurs in females. It is caused by the inheritance of only one complete X chromosome (45, X). Clinical signs of the symmetrical form are identical to those of Turner syndrome and include bilateral webbing of the neck and edema of the extremities. Clinical characteristics include decreased stature and under-developed sexual organs. Patients usually have a normal life expectancy.

[C34440] Bulimia Nervosa -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A disorder characterized by recurrent episodes of binge-eating over which the individual feels a lack of control; these episodes of binge-eating are followed by recurrent compensatory behavior to prevent weight gain, usually self-induced vomiting. In addition, self-evaluation is unduly influenced by body image.  **Synonyms:** - Bulimia - Bulimia
[C34441] Burn -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A traumatic injury involving interruption of tissue cohesiveness that results from exposure to caustic chemicals, extreme heat, extreme cold or excessive radiation.  **Synonyms:** - Burn(s)

[C34445] Cannabis Dependence -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** Physical and psychological dependence on the drug cannabis.
[C34446] Capgras Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare neuropsychiatric disorder whose primary feature is the delusion that relatives or close acquaintances are not the persons that they are known to be. Visual recognition appears intact but familiar persons are thought be imposters, that is, they appear similar or identical to known individuals but are not. Most cases are seen in the context of a psychotic state. However, if manifested post-traumatically, the cause is most likely due to neurologic impairment. This disorder should be contrasted with prosopagnosia, in which an individual may not recognize a familiar person at all.
[C34447] Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A squamous cell carcinoma that arises from any of the following anatomic sites: lip and oral cavity, nasal cavity, paranasal sinuses, pharynx, larynx, and salivary glands.  **Synonyms:** - HNSCC - Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma, NOS - SCCHN - Squamous Cell Carcinoma of Head and Neck - Squamous Cell Carcinoma of the Head and Neck - squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck
[C34448] Carcinosarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A malignant tumor composed of a mixture of carcinomatous and sarcomatous elements.  **Synonyms:** - CARCINOSARCOMA, MALIGNANT - Carcinosarcoma, NOS - carcinosarcoma
[C34449] Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition in which the myocardium is hypertrophied without an obvious cause. The hypertrophy is generally asymmetric and may be associated with obstruction of the ventricular outflow tract.
[C3445] Weight Loss -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A reduction in total body weight.  **Synonyms:** - WEIGHT_LOSS - Weight Decrease - Weight loss - Weight loss
[C34450] Carpal Tunnel Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Entrapment of the median nerve in the wrist that is characterized by numbness, tingling and painful movement.

[C34452] Catalepsy -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A nervous system disorder characterized by diminished responsiveness and consciousness, and rigidity of the body.
[C34453] Cauda Equina Syndrome with Neurogenic Bladder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare neurologic disorder caused by impingement of the nerve roots of the cauda equina secondary to disc herniation, spinal stenosis, vertebral fracture, neoplasm or infection. Clinical signs may include bladder or bowel dysfunction, paresthesia and weakness of the lower extremities. The development of neurogenic bladder implies that surgical decompression was either ineffective, delayed or not attempted.
[C34454] Cellulitis of Hand -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Cellulitis of the hand.
[C34455] Central Opacity of Cornea -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Clouding of the middle area of the cornea, usually as a result of scarring due to Infection, injury, or inflammation.  **Synonyms:** - Central Corneal Clouding

[C3446] Primary Immune Thrombocytopenia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Acquired thrombocytopenia of unknown cause, characterized by immune-mediated destruction of normal platelets. It affects both children and adults. It manifests with petechiae, purpura, and overt bleeding. Based upon the duration of the disease, it is classified as newly diagnosed (from diagnosis until 3 months), persistent (3-12 months), and chronic (lasting for more than 12 months).  **Synonyms:** - ITP - ITP - ITP - ITP - Idiopathic Thrombocytopenia - Idiopathic Thrombocytopenia Purpura - Idiopathic Thrombocytopenic Purpura - Idiopathic Thrombocytopenic Purpura - Idiopathic Thrombocytopenic Purpura - Idiopathic Thrombocytopenic Purpura - Immune Thrombocytopenia - Immune Thrombocytopenic Purpura - Immune thrombocytopenic purpura - idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura - immune thrombocytopenic purpura

[C34467] Synovial Chondromatosis -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An uncommon, benign cartilaginous neoplasm usually occurring in adults. [C34468] Choroid Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the choroid.  Representative examples include choroiditis, hemangioma, and melanoma.  **Synonyms:** - Choroid Disease
