[C3447] Werner Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare, autosomal recessive syndrome caused by mutations in the WRN gene. It is characterized by the appearance of accelerated aging following puberty. It is associated with the development of diabetes mellitus, atherosclerosis, cataracts, and cancer.  **Synonyms:** - Adult Progeria - WS - WS - Werner syndrome - Werner's Syndrome - adult progeria
[C34470] Chromosome Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder that results from a chromosomal abnormality.  **Synonyms:** - Chromosome Abnormality Disorder
[C34471] Chronic Eosinophilic Pneumonia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Chronic inflammatory disorder of the lungs characterized by the presence of eosinophils in the interalveolar septa and alveolar spaces and peripheral blood eosinophilia.  Chest x-rays reveal peripheral infiltrates.  Approximately half of the patients have history of asthma or atopic disease.  Signs and symptoms include fever, dyspnea, cough, and weight loss.  Following treatment with corticosteroids, the eosinophilic infiltrates in the lungs disappear, resulting in dramatic clinical improvement.
[C34472] Chronic Ethmoidal Sinusitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the ethmoid sinus that typically lasts beyond eight weeks. It is caused by infections, allergies, and the presence of sinus polyps or a deviated septum. Signs and symptoms include headache, nasal discharge, swelling in the face, dizziness, and breathing difficulties.

[C34476] Chronic Iridocyclitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Uveitis that is usually insidious in onset, bilateral, and can be asymptomatic, most often affecting the anterior uveal tract, including the iris and ciliary body, and can result in long-term complications. Signs and symptoms include headache, nasal discharge, swelling in the face, dizziness, and breathing difficulties.
[C34478] Cor Pulmonale -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Heart disease which occurs as a result of a primary pulmonary disease. [C34479] Chronic Rhinitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Chronic inflammation of the nasal cavity mucosa.  It may lead to post-nasal drip resulting in chronic sore throat and chronic cough.
[C3448] Wiskott-Aldrich Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare, X-linked immunodeficiency syndrome characterized by eczema, thrombocytopenia, and recurrent pyogenic infection. It is seen exclusively in young boys. Typically, IgM levels are low and IgA and IgE levels are elevated. Lymphoreticular malignancies are common.  **Synonyms:** - Aldrich syndrome - Wiskott Aldrich Syndrome - Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome
[C34480] Chronic Sphenoidal Sinusitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the sphenoid sinus that typically lasts beyond eight weeks. [C34482] Chylous Peritoneal Effusion -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Accumulation of chyle (milky fluid) in the peritoneal cavity. It results from the disruption of the lymphatic vessels in the abdominal cavity.  **Synonyms:** - Chylous Ascites

[C3451] Juvenile Xanthogranuloma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign histiocytic tumor that usually arises from the skin and occurs during childhood; it is distinct from Langerhans cell histiocytosis. It is characterized by the presence of lipid-laden, foamy histiocytes and Touton-type giant cells in the dermis. The lesions usually develop during infancy. While the majority of cases arise from the skin, it can rarely also arise from extracutaneous sites including the eye, ocular adnexa, oral cavity, paranasal sinuses, and ear. It is sometimes associated with deep soft tissues nodules.  **Synonyms:** - JXG - JXG - Juvenile Solitary Xanthogranuloma
[C34510] Conn Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An endocrine disorder characterized by excessive production of aldosterone by the adrenal glands. Causes include adrenal gland adenoma and adrenal gland hyperplasia. The overproduction of aldosterone results in sodium and water retention and hypokalemia. Patients present with high blood pressure, muscle weakness, and headache.  **Synonyms:** - Conn's Syndrome - Primary Hyperaldosteronism
[C34511] Coprophagia -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** The deliberate ingestion of feces. It is considered a form of pica.
[C34512] Hereditary Corneal Dystrophy -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A family of inherited disorders characterized by the gradual, progressive accumulation of extraneous material on one or both corneas, which, without proper treatment, can impair vision.
[C34513] Corneal Dystrophy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An eye disease that affects the structure and function of the cornea.  **Synonyms:** - DYSTROPHY, CORNEA
[C34514] Corn -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A thickening of the skin due to friction or pressure which results in the formation of a small, painful, raised bump associated with a conical mass pointing down into the dermis.

[C34518] Cri du Chat Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A genetic syndrome resulting from a partial deletion on the short arm of chromosome 5. It is characterized by a cat-like cry in infancy, microcephaly, mental retardation, growth failure, round face, hypertelorism, and cardiac failure.  **Synonyms:** - 5p Deletion Syndrome - 5p Partial Monosomy Syndrome - Cat-Cry Syndrome - Cri-du-Chat Syndrome
[C34519] Cystic Endometrial Hyperplasia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Benign endometrial hyperplasia characterized by the presence of cystically dilated glands.
[C3452] Xeroderma Pigmentosum -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inherited skin disorder characterized by photosensitivity with severe sunburn in infancy, the development of numerous pigmented spots resembling freckles, larger atrophic lesions associated with telangiectasis, and multiple solar keratoses. Transmitted in an autosomal recessive manner, xeroderma pigmentosa involves a defect in nucleotide excision repair (NER), leading to deficient repair of DNA damaged by UV radiation and chromosome breakage. Individuals with this disease develop multiple malignant cutaneous neoplasms at an early age and may suffer from severe ophthalmic and neurologic abnormalities.  **Synonyms:** - xeroderma pigmentosum
[C34520] Cysticercosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A parasitic infection caused by the larval form of Taenia solium. It is a disseminated infection affecting the central nervous system, subcutaneous tissues, lungs, heart and liver. The most serious complications result from infection of the brain parenchyma. Patients may develop seizures, hydrocephalus, encephalopathy and meningoencephalitis.
[C34521] Dacryocystitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the lacrimal sac.
[C34522] Multi-Infarct Dementia -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A common form of dementia caused by multiple cortical or subcortical cerebral infarctions.  **Synonyms:** - Multi Infarct Dementia
[C34523] Pre-Senile Dementia -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** The presence of dementia in an individual younger than age sixty five.  **Synonyms:** - Presenile Dementia
[C34524] Senile Dementia -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** Loss of intellectual abilities in an elderly person, interfering with this person's activities.
[C34525] Vascular Dementia -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A degenerative vascular disorder affecting the brain. It is caused by the blockage of the blood supply to the brain. It is manifested with decline of memory and cognitive functions.
[C34526] Demyelinating Disorder of Central Nervous System -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Any condition in which there is degeneration of the myelin sheath that covers the nerves of the central nervous system.  **Synonyms:** - Demyelinating Disease of Central Nervous System - Demyelinating disease of central nervous system, unspecified
[C34527] Demyelinating Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A broad group of disorders that affect the myelin sheaths that cover the neurons. Myelin sheathes cover neuronal axons in the central and peripheral nervous system and function to increase travelling impulse speeds. Disruption of this sheath impairs neuronal transmission and can result in disorders such as multiple sclerosis and Guillain-Barre syndrome, among others.  **Synonyms:** - Demyelinating Disease
[C34528] Dengue Fever -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An acute infection caused by Dengue virus. Signs and symptoms include sudden onset of fever, headache, rash, arthralgia, and myalgia. It is transmitted through mosquitoes.  **Synonyms:** - Break Bone Fever - Dengue - Dengue - Dengue
[C34529] Enamel Hypoplasia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Deficiency in the enamel tissue that results in the formation of grooves, pits, or dents on the surface of the affected teeth.
[C3453] Zollinger Ellison Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A syndrome characterized by the presence of a gastrin-secreting tumor, usually in the pancreas or duodenum, resulting in increased gastric acidity and formation of gastric ulcers. Signs and symptoms include abdominal pain and diarrhea. It may be sporadic or a manifestation of multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1.  **Synonyms:** - Zollinger-Ellison Syndrome - Zollinger-Ellison syndrome
[C34530] Dental Pulp Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder that affects the dental pulp tissue.  Examples include inflammation, calcification, and dental pulp exposure.
[C34531] Amyloid Deposition -- **Semantic type:** Finding
[C34532] Endogenous Depression -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** Depression which is considered strictly biological.
[C34533] Reactive Depression -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** Depression precipitated by events in a person's life.
[C34534] Dermatitis due to Food taken Internally -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Dermatitis caused by an allergic reaction to ingested food.
[C34535] Dermatophytosis of Groin and Perianal Area -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Dermatophytosis involving the stratum corneum of the skin of the groin and perianal area.
[C34536] Dermatophytosis of Scalp and Beard -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Dermatophytosis involving the stratum corneum of the skin of the scalp and beard area.
[C34537] Lipoatrophic Diabetes Mellitus -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare syndrome characterized by almost complete absence of body fat, accentuated muscularity, insulin-resistant diabetes, hyperlipidemia, hepatomegaly, and hypermetabolism.
[C34538] Diabetic Retinopathy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A chronic, pathological complication associated with diabetes mellitus, where retinal damages are incurred due to microaneurysms in the vasculature of the retina, progressively leading to abnormal blood vessel growth, and swelling and leaking of fluid from blood vessels, resulting in vision loss or blindness.
[C34539] Digitalis Toxicity -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Acute CNS, visual, GI, and cardiac disturbances caused by an overdose of, or intolerance to, the cardiac glycoside, digitalis.
[C34540] Dipetalonemiasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A parasitic infection caused by genus of filarial worms called Dipetalonema. It produces microfilariae in the blood and body fluids.  **Synonyms:** - Dipetalonema Infection - Dipetalonema Infections
[C34541] Diphtheria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A Gram-positive bacterial infection caused by Corynebacterium diphtheriae. It usually involves the oral cavity, pharynx, and nasal cavity. Patients develop pseudomembranes in the affected areas and manifest signs and symptoms of an upper respiratory infection. The diphtheria toxin may cause myocarditis, polyneuritis, and other systemic effects.
[C34542] Anterior Nasal Diphtheria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Infection of the anterior nasal structures by Corynebacterium diphtheriae.
[C34543] Pseudomembranous Diphtheritic Conjunctivitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Pseudomembranous colitis resulting from infection by Corynebacterium diphtheriae.
[C34544] Cutaneous Diphtheria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A usually mild form of diphtheria characterized by infection of the skin by corynebacterium diphtheria and the resulting formation of a chronic, shallow ulcer that is sometimes bordered or followed by a bulla.
[C34545] Faucial Diphtheria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Infection of the fauces by Corynebacterium diphtheriae.
[C34546] Laryngeal Diphtheria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Infection of the larynx by Corynebacterium diphtheriae.
[C34547] Nasopharyngeal Diphtheria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Infection of the nasopharynx by Corynebacterium diphtheriae.
[C34548] Fluid Discharge -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The pouring forth of a fluid.  **Synonyms:** - Discharge - discharge
[C34549] Disturbance of Skin Sensation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Disruption of the normal functioning of the sensory nerves in the skin.  **Synonyms:** - Skin Sensation Disturbance
[C3455] Arachnoid Cyst -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An intracranial or spinal cyst which contains fluid and is composed of arachnoid cells. Intracranial arachnoid cysts may present with headaches, seizures and hydrocephalus. Spinal arachnoid cysts may present with symptoms of radiculopathy.  **Synonyms:** - Leptomeningeal Cyst
[C34550] Colonic Diverticulum -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A pouch or sac-like protrusion in the colonic wall.  **Synonyms:** - Colon Diverticulum
[C34551] Colonic Diverticulosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Diverticulosis of the colon.
[C34553] Dry Eye Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A syndrome characterized by dryness of the cornea and conjunctiva. It is usually caused by a deficiency in tear production. Symptoms include a feeling of burning eyes and a possible foreign body presence in the eye.  **Synonyms:** - Dry Eye - Dry Eye(s) - Dry eye - Keratoconjunctivitis Sicca
[C34554] Dwarfism -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Short stature that usually results from genetic mutations. Other causes include hormonal deficiencies and poor nutrition.
[C34555] Pituitary Dwarfism -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Proportionately decreased bodily growth due to failure of the pituitary gland to produce an adequate supply of growth hormone.
[C34556] Dyschondroplasia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome
[C34557] Skin Pigmentation Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder of the skin characterized by loss or reduction of the skin color. It is caused by loss of melanocytes or abnormalities in melanin production.  **Synonyms:** - Pigmentation Disorders
[C34558] Amebic Colitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Colitis resulting from amebic infection.
[C34559] Dysmenorrhea -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Abnormally painful abdominal cramps during menstruation.
[C3456] Inclusion Body Fibromatosis -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign myofibroblastic neoplasm that affects young children and is characterized by the presence of neoplastic spindle cells and intracytoplasmic eosinophilic spherical inclusions. It arises from the soft tissues of the digits or extradigital areas including the extremities, breasts, and tongue.  **Synonyms:** - Digital Fibrous Tumor of Reye - Infantile Digital Fibroma/Fibromatosis - Infantile Digital Fibromatosis - Reye Tumor - Reye's Tumor
[C34560] Dysostosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A defect in ossification of bone.
[C34561] Paroxysmal Nocturnal Dyspnea -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** Sudden onset of shortness of breath that causes one to awaken coughing or wheezing; it may be associated with congestive heart failure or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.  **Synonyms:** - Cardiac Asthma - PND - Paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea
[C34562] Dysthymic Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A chronic mood disorder in which the symptoms are similar to, though milder than, those diagnosed in depression.  **Synonyms:** - Dysthymia - Dysthymic disorder - PDD - Persistent Depressive Disorder
[C34563] Dystonia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A movement disorder characterized by sustained or intermittent muscle contractions, resulting in abnormal movements and/or postures.
[C34564] Idiopathic Torsion Dystonia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Torsion dystonia for which no underlying cause has been identified.
[C34565] Vulvar Dystrophy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic lesion that affects the vulva and is characterized by thinning or thickening of the skin and dryness.
[C34566] Congenital Ectopic Lens -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Displacement of the lens of the eye secondary to defective zonule formation that is present at the time of birth.
[C34567] Ectromelia -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A congenital defect characterized by the absence or hypoplasia of one or more extremities.
[C34568] Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inherited connective tissue disorder characterized by loose and fragile skin and joint hypermobility.  **Synonyms:** - Ehlers-Danlos syndrome
[C34569] Elephantiasis -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Enlargement of an area of the body due to obstruction within the lymphatic system and the resulting accumulation of lymph.  **Synonyms:** - lymphostatic elephantiasis - stage III lymphedema
[C3457] B-Cell Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** The most common type of non-Hodgkin lymphoma.  It includes the most frequently seen morphologic variants which are: diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, follicular lymphoma, small lymphocytic lymphoma and marginal zone B-cell lymphoma.  **Synonyms:** - B-Cell Non Hodgkin's Lymphoma - B-Cell Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma - B-cell lymphoma - Lymphomas Non-Hodgkin's B-Cell - Non-Hodgkin's B-Cell Lymphoma - Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma B-Cell - Unspecified B-cell lymphoma, unspecified site
[C34570] Emaciation -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An extreme loss of muscle and subcutaneous fat that is caused by malnutrition, and which results in a severely thin (emaciated) appearance.
[C34571] Pulmonary Interstitial Emphysema -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Pathologic accumulation of air in the interstitium of the lungs, which is caused by the rupture of alveoli and terminal bronchioles, and is most often seen in premature infants that need mechanical ventilation for respiratory distress syndrome.  **Synonyms:** - Interstitial Emphysema of Lung - PIE
[C34572] Empyema -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An accumulation of pus in the pleural space.  **Synonyms:** - Pleural Empyema
[C34573] Empyema with Fistula -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An empyema with a fistula.
[C34574] Pulmonary Empyema -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome
[C34575] Tuberculous Empyema -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An empyema resulting from infection by Mycobacterium tuberculosis.
[C34576] Epidemic Encephalitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A term that may be used to describe encephalitis characterized by sleepiness, which is likely due to a viral cause.
[C34577] Japanese B Encephalitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Viral encephalitis caused by the mosquito-born Japanese encephalitis virus. Signs and symptoms include an initial period of fever, headaches, and malaise, followed by neck rigidity, hemiparesis, and convulsions. It may lead to coma.  **Synonyms:** - Japanese Encephalitis
[C34578] Acute Disseminated Encephalomyelitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inflammatory process affecting the central nervous system. It is characterized by perivascular demyelination. Symptoms usually follow an acute viral infection or immunization and include fever, confusion, somnolence and involuntary movements. It may lead to coma and death.  **Synonyms:** - ADEM - ADEM
[C34579] Tick-Borne Viral Encephalitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Viral encephalitis that is transmitted by ticks.
[C3458] High Grade Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A term that refers to B-cell or T-cell non-Hodgkin lymphomas with high-grade histopathological features.  **Synonyms:** - high-grade lymphoma
[C34580] Encephalomyelitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the brain and the spinal cord.
[C34581] Endarteritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the arterial intima.
[C34582] Endocarditis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the endocardium.  **Synonyms:** - endocarditis
[C34583] Subacute Bacterial Endocarditis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Subacute inflammation of the endocardium. Streptococcus viridans is the usual etiologic agent of subacute bacterial endocarditis. The distinction between "acute" and "subacute" endocarditis has traditionally been made based on the pathogenic organism and clinical presentation.  **Synonyms:** - SBE
[C34584] Endocervical Polyp -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A polyp that arises from the endocervix. It is characterized by the presence of endocervical glands and a fibrovascular stroma.  **Synonyms:** - Benign endocervical polyp
[C34585] Endomyocardial Fibrosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disease characterized by fibrotic thickening of the endocardium, particularly the right and/or left inflow tracts. The disease often involves the atrioventricular valves, leading to valvular regurgitaion.  It most commonly occurs in children living within 15 degrees of the equator.
[C34586] Endophthalmitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An infectious process affecting the internal structures of the eye.
[C34587] Parasitic Endophthalmitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Infection of the interior of the eye, especially the aqueous and/or vitreous humor, by a parasite.
[C34588] Enuresis -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** An elimination disorder characterized by urinary incontinence, whether involuntary or intentional, which is not due to a medical condition and which occurs at or beyond an age at which continence is expected (usually 5 years).
[C34589] Epicondylitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the epicondyles.
[C3459] Intermediate Grade Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An antiquated term that refers to a non-Hodgkin lymphoma with intermediate grade histologic features.  **Synonyms:** - intermediate-grade lymphoma
[C34590] Epidemic Keratoconjunctivitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Keratoconjunctivitis resulting from infection by adenoviruses.
[C34591] Erythematosquamous Dermatosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A skin condition that primarily affects the scalp and face and presents as scaly inflammation. Examples include itchy, dry skin and dandruff.
[C34592] Erythroid Hyperplasia -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** A laboratory test result indicating an abnormally high quantity of immature red blood cells.
[C34593] Erythromelalgia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare disorder characterized by periodic inflammation and blockage of the vessels of the extremities, resulting in skin redness, swelling, and burning pain in the affected sites. It may manifest as a primary disorder caused by mutations of the SCN9A gene or as a secondary disorder due to hematologic disorders or medication side effects.
[C34594] Escherichia coli Infection -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Infection with the organism Escherichia Coli.  **Synonyms:** - E coli Infection - Escherichia coli
[C34595] Esophageal Stricture -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Narrowing of the lumen of the esophagus.  **Synonyms:** - Esophageal Stenosis - Esophageal stenosis - Stenosis of the esophagus
[C34596] Internal Strabismus -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A form of strabismus in which one or both eyes are deviated medially.  **Synonyms:** - Esotropia - Esotropia
[C34597] Ethmoidal Sinusitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An acute or chronic inflammatory process affecting the mucous membrane of the ethmoid sinus.
[C34598] Euphoria -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** An exaggerated feeling of well-being which is disproportionate to events and stimuli. It may result from psychological causes, organic brain syndromes, or toxic states.  **Synonyms:** - euphoria
[C34599] Excoriation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Self-inflicted tearing or wearing off of skin.  **Synonyms:** - Excoriation of Skin
[C346] Glucarpidase -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A zinc-dependent enzyme isolated from a strain of the bacterium Pseudomonas. Because glucarpidase rapidly hydrolyzes methotrexate into inactive metabolites, it may be useful as a rescue agent for methotrexate-induced nephrotoxicity. In antibody-directed enzyme prodrug therapy (ADEPT), this agent is conjugated with an antibody that binds to a specific tumor cell type, allowing for glucarpidase-catalyzed activation of a co-administered prodrug at the site of the tumor.  **Synonyms:** - Acetylaspartylglutamate Dipeptidase - CPDG2 - CPG2 - Carboxypeptidase G2 - GLUCARPIDASE - Megludase - Poly(gamma-glutamic Acid) Endohydrolase - Pteroylpolygammaglutamyl Hydrolase - Voraxaze - carboxypeptidase-G2
[C3460] Grade 3 Follicular Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A follicular lymphoma which contains more than 15 centroblasts per 40X high-power microscopic field.  **Synonyms:** - Follicular Large Cell Lymphoma - Follicular Lymphoma Grade 3 - Follicular lymphoma grade III, unspecified, unspecified site - Follicular lymphoma, grade 3 - Grade 3 - Grade III Follicular Large Cell Lymphoma - Grade III Follicular Lymphoma - Malignant Lymphoma Centroblastic, Follicular - Malignant Lymphoma Follicular, Large Non-Cleaved Cell Type - Nodular Histiocytic Lymphoma - WHO Follicular Lymphoma Histologic Grade 3 - follicular large cell lymphoma - grade 3 follicular lymphoma
[C34600] Exercise-Induced Bronchospasm -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Sudden contraction of the smooth muscles of the bronchial wall that occurs during or following exercise.  **Synonyms:** - Exercise induced bronchospasm
[C34601] Divergent Strabismus -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A form of strabismus in which the eyes are deviated laterally.  **Synonyms:** - Exotropia
[C34602] Ocular Injury -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Damage to the eye.  **Synonyms:** - Eye Injury - Injury of Eye Region
[C34603] Face Injury -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Trauma to the face.
[C34605] Farmer's Lung -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Hypersensitivity pneumonitis caused by the repeated exposure and inhalation of biological dust (such as hay dust, mold spores, or other agricultural products).  It is considered a type II hypersensitivity inflammatory reaction.  In the acute phase, signs and symptoms include fever, chills, cough, dyspnea, headache, and chest tightness.  The subacute phase manifests as chronic cough, dyspnea, anorexia, and weight loss.  The chronic phase results from the prolonged exposure to the antigen and is characterized by severe dyspnea and irreversible damage to the lungs.  **Synonyms:** - Farmer's lung
[C34606] Fasciculation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The involuntary trembling of one or more muscles.  **Synonyms:** - Muscle Twitching
[C34607] Favism -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition associated with glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency, which is characterized by hemolysis.
[C34608] Fever of Unknown Origin -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** A febrile process with an etiology that is not apparent or yet determined.
[C34609] Fibrous Dysplasia -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign medullary neoplasm characterized by the presence of poorly organized and inadequately mineralized bone and intervening fibrous tissue, in varying proportions. It can be monostotic or polyostotic. The skull, femur, tibia, and humerus are the most frequently affected bones. It manifests with pain, deformities, and fractures. GNAS activating mutations are detected in the majority of cases.  **Synonyms:** - Fibrous Dysplasia of Bone
[C3461] Immunoblastic Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A diffuse large B-cell lymphoma characterized by the presence of immunoblasts with uniformly round-to-oval nuclei, a prominent nucleolus, and abundant cytoplasm.  **Synonyms:** - LYMPHOMA, IMMUNOBLASTIC, MALIGNANT
[C34610] Polyostotic Fibrous Dysplasia -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** Fibrous dysplasia affecting more than one bone. When it is associated with café-au-lait skin pigmentation and endocrine disorders, it is known as McCune-Albright syndrome.  **Synonyms:** - Polyostotic Fibrous Dysplasia of Bone - Polyostotic Fibrous Dysplasia of Bone
[C34611] Filariasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Infection by filarial worms.

[C34614] Intestinal Fistula excluding Rectum and Anus -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An abnormal connection between the intestine and another epithelial-lined structure, but that does not include the rectum or anus.
[C34615] Flank Pain -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** Painful sensation in the region below the ribs and above the hip.  **Synonyms:** - Flank pain
[C34616] Flat Foot -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An anatomic deformity in which the arch of the foot collapses, resulting in the entire sole of the foot coming into complete or near-complete contact with the ground.  **Synonyms:** - Fallen Arch
[C34617] Follicle Cyst of Ovary -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A benign ovarian cyst that develops from a persistent, unruptured Graafian follicle, which grows larger than three centimeters.  **Synonyms:** - Follicular Cyst of Ovary
[C34618] Foot Pain -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** A sensation of discomfort emanating from the foot, its supporting structures, or its articulation with the ankle.
[C34619] Patent Foramen Ovale -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A persistent opening in the atrial septum after birth. While a normal part of fetal circulation, the foramen ovale should close once the newborn begins breathing and the pressure in the left atrium exceeds that of the right atrium. While a PFO is generally asymptomatic, it can lead to the passage of clots from the venous circulation into the artierial circulation, resulting in paradoxical emboli, and possible strokes.
[C3462] Low Grade Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An antiquated term that refers to a non-Hodgkin lymphoma with low grade histologic features.  **Synonyms:** - low-grade lymphoma
[C34620] Foreign Body -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Extraneous material within the body.  **Synonyms:** - FOREIGN MATERIAL - Foreign Body In Patient

[C34622] Foreign Body in Gastrointestinal Tract -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Extraneous material located within the gastrointestinal tract.
[C34623] Closed Fracture -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A traumatic break to the bone in which the continuity of the bone has not broken through the skin.
[C34624] Open Fracture -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A traumatic break to the bone in which the continuity of the bone has broken through the skin.  **Synonyms:** - Compound Fracture
[C34625] Frohlich Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare acquired endocrine disorder more commonly affecting males. It is caused by lesions in the hypothalamus. Clinical signs include obesity, short stature, pubertal delay, hypogonadism, visual impairment, polydipsia and polyuria.  **Synonyms:** - Adiposogenital Dystrophy - Babinski-Froelich Syndrome - Dystrophia Adiposogenitalis - Froehlich's Syndrome - Froelich's Syndrome - Frohlich's Syndrome - Frolich's Syndrome - Fröhlich Syndrome - Hypothalamic Infantilism-Obesity - Launois-Cleret Syndrome - Sexual Infantilism
[C34626] Frontal Sinusitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An acute or chronic inflammatory process affecting the mucous membrane of the frontal sinus.
[C34627] Frostbite -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An injury to the skin and/or its underlying tissues that results from exposure of the affected area to extreme cold.
[C34628] Nonorganic Enuresis -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** Involuntary urine release that cannot be attributed to an anatomic or physiologic etiology.
[C34629] Furunculosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A skin condition that is characterized by recurring furuncles, lesions primarily caused by staphylococcus aureus infection of the hair follicles and surrounding skin. Furunculosis occurs more frequently in persons with immune systems disorders than in the general population.
[C3463] Diffuse Mixed Cell Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An antiquated term referring to non-Hodgkin lymphomas with a mixed cellular composition. This term applies to both B- and T- cell non-Hodgkin lymphomas.  **Synonyms:** - Diffuse Mixed Large and Small Cell Lymphoma
[C34630] Gagging -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The sudden, coordinated, involuntary contraction of pharyngeal muscles in an attempt to prevent any matter from passing into the posterior pharynx; it is stimulated by touching the posterior palate, tongue, or pharynx, or by psychic stimuli.
[C34631] Gallbladder Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the gallbladder. Representative examples of non-neoplastic disorders include acute and chronic cholecystitis, often associated with the presence of gallstones. Representative examples of neoplastic disorders include adenoma, carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma.  **Synonyms:** - Gall Bladder Disease - Gall Bladder Disorder - Gallbladder Disease - Gallbladder Disease
[C34632] Gastroenteritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inflammatory disorder that affects the upper and lower gastrointestinal tract. Most commonly, this is attributed to viruses; however bacteria, parasites or adverse reactions can also be the culprit. Symptoms include acute diarrhea and vomiting.  **Synonyms:** - gastroenteritis
[C34633] Gastrointestinal Malfunction Arising from Mental Factor -- **Semantic type:** Pathologic Function  **Definition:** A dysfunction in gastrointestinal function that is due to a psychological process.
[C34634] Congenital Leukocyte Abnormality -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An abnormality of leukocytes, the cause of which is present from birth.  **Synonyms:** - Congenital Leucocyte Abnormality - Congenital Leucocyte Anomaly - Congenital Leukocyte Anomaly - Genetic Anomaly of Leucocyte - Genetic Anomaly of Leukocyte
[C34635] Gingival Pocket -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An abnormal deepening of a gingival sulcus not associated with destruction of the supporting periodontal tissue.
[C34636] Gingivitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder involving inflammation of the gums; may affect surrounding and supporting structures of the teeth.
[C34637] Necrotizing Ulcerative Gingivitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A bacterial infectious process affecting the gums. It is characterized by the development of necrotic, ulcerated, and painful lesions with creation of pseudomembranes extending along the gingival margins.  **Synonyms:** - Vincent Angina - Vincent's Angina
[C34638] Glanders -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition resulting from infection by Burkholderia mallei, which mainly affects horses.
[C34639] Angle Closure Glaucoma -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** The sudden increase of intraocular pressure, resulting in pain and an abrupt decrease in visual acuity.  **Synonyms:** - Closed Angle Glaucoma - Closed Angle Glaucoma - Narrow Angle Glaucoma - Primary Angle Closure Glaucoma

[C347] Carcinogen -- **Semantic type:** Classification  **Definition:** Any chemical, biological or physical agents that increase the risk of neoplasms in humans or animals.  Carcinogens include natural or synthesized compounds, certain viruses, and various sources of radiation.  A carcinogen may directly alter the genetic material of cells (genotoxic), thereby initiating or promoting the process of malignant transformation; it also may induce cancers by mechanisms that do not involve a direct alteration of cellular genetic material (non-genotoxic). (NCI04)  **Synonyms:** - Cancer Causing Agents - carcinogen

[C3471] AIDS-Related Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An aggressive lymphoma that develops in HIV-positive patients.  There is a significant increase in the incidence of lymphomas associated with the AIDS epidemic.  These lymphomas are usually extranodal, most often express a B-cell phenotype and morphologically are classified as Burkitt, diffuse large B-cell, and Hodgkin lymphomas.  The prognosis is closely related to the severity of immunodeficiency.  **Synonyms:** - AIDS Lymphoma - AIDS Related Lymphoma - HIV Associated Lymphoma - Lymphoma AIDS Related - Lymphoma, AIDS-Related

[C34730] Insulin Coma -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A hypoglycemia-induced comatose state resulting from a large dose of exogenous insulin.
[C34731] Intestinal Disaccharidase Deficiency and Disaccharide Malabsorption -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A classification of inherited or acquired disorders of sugar metabolism. Deficiencies of lactase, maltase or sucrase-isomaltase usually occur irreversibly and independent of one another. Congenital deficiencies are rare whereas acquired deficiencies are more common and may be seen following intestinal mucosal brush-border injury. Clinical signs include abdominal cramping, bloating, flatulence and diarrhea following dietary intake of lactose, maltose or sucrose. The clinical course leads to malabsorption of disaccharides which has implications for normal growth and development if manifested at an early age.

[C34739] Jacksonian Seizure -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A partial motor seizure that begins in a single area of the body and progresses to include other areas on the same side of the body.
[C3474] Granular Cell Tumor -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An unusual benign or malignant neoplasm characterized by the presence of neoplastic large polygonal cells with granular, eosinophilic cytoplasm which contains abundant lysosomes. It was originally thought to be a tumor originating from muscle cells and was named granular cell myoblastoma. Subsequent studies have suggested a derivation from Schwann cells. It affects females more often than males and it usually presents as a solitary mass. A minority of patients have multiple tumors. It can arise from many anatomic sites including the posterior pituitary gland, skin, oral cavity, esophagus, stomach, heart, mediastinum, and breast.  **Synonyms:** - Abrikossoff Tumor - Abrikossoff's Tumor - Granular Cell Myoblastoma - Granular Cell Nerve Sheath Tumor - Granular Cell Schwannoma - Granular cell tumor, NOS

[C34742] Obstructive Jaundice -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A finding indicating increased bilirubin levels in the blood and urine, due to intrahepatic or extrahepatic obstruction of the biliary system.  **Synonyms:** - Cholestatic Jaundice

[C3475] Bone Remodeling -- **Semantic type:** Organ or Tissue Function  **Definition:** A concomitant process in which old bone is resorbed and new bone is formed. Bone remodeling occurs primarily in bone and involves two types of cells, osteoblasts and osteoclasts. Osteoblasts synthesize bone matrix and form new bone while osteoclasts digest mineral matrix and old bone.
[C34750] Cystic Kidney Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A congenital or acquired kidney disorder characterized by the presence of renal cysts.
[C34751] Medullary Sponge Kidney -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A developmental disorder of the kidney characterized by cystic dilatation of the medullary collecting ducts, resulting in a spongy gross appearance of the kidney. It may be asymptomatic or complicated by hematuria, infections, or renal stones.
[C34752] Klinefelter Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A sex chromosome disorder caused by the presence of an extra X chromosome in the male karyotype. Affected individuals are infertile and have a small penis and testes. They tend to have tall stature and long legs and may have difficulties with speech and language development. Gynecomastia may be present.  **Synonyms:** - 47,XXY Syndrome - Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism - Klinefelter syndrome - Klinefelter's Syndrome - Klinefelter's Syndrome, XXY - XXY Syndrome - XXY Syndrome (Klinefelter Syndrome)
