[C34757] Lacrimal Duct Obstruction -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Blockage of the tear duct.
[C34758] Visceral Larva Migrans -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A parasitic infection caused by worms found in domestic animals. In humans nematode larvae enter the portal system from the small intestine and disseminate in visceral organs causing inflammatory reactions. Signs and symptoms include eosinophilia, hepatomegaly, splenomegaly, and lung infections.
[C34759] Latent Schizophrenia -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** Schizophrenia that develops in middle age.
[C3476] Li-Fraumeni Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An autosomal dominant cancer predisposition syndrome caused by germline mutations of the TP53 gene. It is associated with breast carcinoma, choroid plexus carcinoma, adrenal cortex carcinoma, astrocytic tumors, medulloblastoma, soft tissue sarcoma, osteosarcoma, and leukemia.  **Synonyms:** - Li-Fraumeni - Li-Fraumeni Familial Cancer Susceptibility Syndrome - Li-Fraumeni Syndrome, TP53-Associated - Li-Fraumeni syndrome - TP53-Associated Li-Fraumeni Syndrome
[C34760] Laurence-Moon Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare genetic syndrome with an autosomal recessive pattern of inheritance. Mutations in the BBS5 and MKKS genes of chromosome 11 have been observed in some cases which has lead to comparisons with Bardet-Biedl syndrome and McKusick-Kaufman syndrome. These syndromes have similar etiologies and presentations but are considered separate entities. Clinical signs of Laurence-Moon syndrome include ataxia, intellectual delay, retinitis pigmentosa and hypogonadism. The clinical course follows a progression to growth retardation, spastic paraplegia and optic atrophy with eventual vision loss.
[C34761] Lead Poisoning -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Chronic toxicity to the body caused by the absorption of lead, which is characterized by fatigue, gastrointestinal symptoms, anemia, a dark line along the gums, and muscular paralysis or weakness of limbs.
[C34762] Leg Cramp -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** An involuntary contraction of a muscle supporting the femur.
[C34763] Leg Injury -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Trauma to the lower extremity, which is confined to the area between the hip and ankle joints.
[C34764] Leg Pain -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** A sensation of discomfort emanating from the femur or its supporting structures.
[C34765] Lower Extremity Ulcer -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A circumscribed, inflammatory, and often suppurating lesion that causes necrosis of tissue on the skin of the limb comprising the hip, thigh, leg and foot.
[C34766] Perthes Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A very rare disorder of unknown etiology affecting children. It is characterized by avascular necrosis of the femoral head.  **Synonyms:** - Legg-Calve-Perthes Disease
[C34767] Leishmaniasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A parasitic infection caused by protozoa of the genus Leishmania. It is transmitted to humans via the bite of sandflies. There are three main forms of the disease: cutaneous, mucocutaneous, and visceral leishmaniasis. Cutaneous leishmaniasis causes skin ulcers; mucocutaneous leishmaniasis causes destructive lesions of the mucous membranes of the nose, mouth, and throat; visceral leishmaniasis is the most severe form of the disease and is manifested with anemia, weight loss, hepatomegaly and splenomegaly.
[C34768] Cutaneous Leishmaniasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Leishmaniasis affecting the skin. It is the most common form of leishmaniasis. It presents with erythematous macules and papules, and nodules which may eventually ulcerate. The lesions appear in the bite site in the exposed skin areas.
[C34769] American Cutaneous Leishmaniasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** The most common form of leishmaniasis that is transmitted through the bite of female phlebotomine sand flies or after exposure to leishmania parasites. It is characterized by skin lesions at the site of insect bite which typically develop within weeks or months after exposure. The lesions typically progress from small papules to open sores with raised borders and central ulcers which can be covered with scales or crust.
[C3477] Bacillary Angiomatosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition that is caused by infection with Bartonella, and which is characterized by vascular proliferation, usually in immunocompromised individuals.  **Synonyms:** - Bacillary Epithelioid Angiomatosis - Epithelioid Angiomatosis
[C34770] Asian Desert Cutaneous Leishmaniasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An acute necrotizing form of cutaneous leishmaniasis caused by Infection with leishmania tropica major.
[C34771] Kala-Azar -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A chronic parasitic infection affecting the viscera and caused by Leishmania donovani. Signs and symptoms include fever, anorexia, fatigue, lymphadenopathy, anemia, and hepatosplenomegaly. If left untreated it may lead to death.  **Synonyms:** - Visceral Leishmaniasis
[C34772] Lens Subluxation -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A partial dislocation of the lens of the eye.
[C34773] Leriche Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An atherosclerotic disorder of the peripheral vascular system affecting mostly males in their later decades. It is caused by thrombotic occlusion of the abdominal aorta just above the level of the bifurcation. Clinical signs include impotence, intermittent claudication, diminished femoral pulses and cold, pallid lower extremities. Prognosis is favorable with surgical or endovascular intervention.
[C34774] Chronic Monocytic Leukemia -- **Concept status:** Retired_Concept
[C34775] Leukorrhea -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Whitish or yellowish mucosal vaginal discharge.
[C34776] Lice -- **Semantic type:** Eukaryote  **Definition:** A taxonomic order of wingless insects that are obligate ectoparasites of birds and mammals. Lice attach tightly to hair, fur, and feathers of the host organism and feed on blood, skin, and sebaceous secretions.  **Synonyms:** - Louse - Sucking Louse
[C34777] Atrophic Lichen Planus -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare form of lichen planus that is characterized by well-demarcated, white-bluish papules or plaques with central atrophy.
[C34778] Bullous Lichen Planus -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare form of lichen planus that is characterized by the formation of vesico-bullous lesions on top of preexisting lichen planus papules or on normal skin.
[C34779] Hypertrophic Lichen Planus -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A form of lichen planus that is characterized by plaques of markedly thickened skin that is often extremely pruritic and localized to the lower legs. It can result in permanent pigmentation and scarring.
[C3478] Essential Hypertension -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Hypertension that presents without an identifiable cause.  **Synonyms:** - Essential (primary) hypertension - Primary Hypertension
[C34780] Lichenoid Drug Eruption -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A red or violaceous flat-topped, papular eruption that is induced by exposure to a variety of medications or environmental agents.  **Synonyms:** - Drug-induced Lichen Planus - Drug-induced Lichen Planus
[C34781] Spastic Diplegia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A type of cerebral palsy characterized by spasticity and hypertonia of the lower extremities bilaterally, particularly the legs, hips, and pelvis; this is the most common (70%) form of cerebral palsy.  **Synonyms:** - Little's Disease - Spastic diplegic cerebral palsy
[C34782] Alcoholic Cirrhosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder of the liver characterized by the presence of fibrotic scar tissue instead of healthy liver tissue. This condition is attributed to excessive consumption of alcoholic beverages.  **Synonyms:** - Cirrhosis of the Liver Secondary to Alcohol Use
[C34783] Alcoholic Liver Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder caused by damage to the liver parenchyma due to alcohol consumption. It may present with an acute onset or follow a chronic course, leading to cirrhosis.  **Synonyms:** - Alcoholic liver disease, unspecified
[C34784] Loiasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Infection by the African eye worm, Loa loa.
[C34785] Loneliness -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** Sadness resulting from being isolated or alone.
[C34786] Long QT Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A ventricular arrhythmia characterized by a long QT interval, and accompanied by syncopal episodes sometimes leading to sudden death due to paroxysmal ventricular arrhythmia. This arrhythmia is associated with a prolongation of repolarization following depolarization of the cardiac ventricles. The prolongation of the Q-T interval combined with torsades de pointes manifests as several different forms; some may be acquired or congenital; some may lead to serious arrhythmia and sudden cardiac death.  **Synonyms:** - LONG QT SYNDROME - Ventricular arrhythmia associated with long QT syndrome
[C34787] Lordosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Abnormally increased curvature of the cervical or lumbar portion of the spinal column.  **Synonyms:** - LORDOSIS
[C34788] Lower Back Pain -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** A sensation of discomfort emanating from the lower back.
[C34789] Lupus Glomerulonephritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Glomerulonephritis in the context of systemic lupus erythematosus.  **Synonyms:** - Lupus Nephritis - SLE Nephritis
[C3479] Addiction -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** Physical and/or psychological dependence to any substance.  **Synonyms:** - addiction
[C34790] Lymphangitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the lymphatic vessels.
[C34791] Lymphocytosis -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A finding indicating the presence of a non-neoplastic or neoplastic proliferation of lymphocytes in the peripheral blood, effusions, or bone marrow.  **Synonyms:** - Lymphocyte count increased
[C34792] Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare, but potentially life-threatening disorder, characterized by the proliferation of histiocytes and macrophages, and phagocytosis of red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets. It may be inherited or secondary due to infections, autoimmune disorders, or underlying malignancies. Signs and symptoms include fever, lymphadenopathy, hepatomegaly, splenomegaly, and pancytopenia.  **Synonyms:** - HLH - Hemophagocytic Disorder - hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis
[C34793] Macular Corneal Dystrophy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A stromal corneal dystrophy, with autosomal recessive inheritance, that is caused by lack of or abnormal keratan sulfate.
[C34794] Cystoid Macular Edema -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An accumulation of fluid in the outer plexiform layer, secondary to an increase in permeability of perifoveal retinal capillaries.
[C34795] Macular Hole -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A hole in the macula of the retina.
[C34796] Single Episode of Major Depressive Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An episode of depression lasting two or more weeks without an intervening episode of mania.  **Synonyms:** - Major depressive disorder, single episode, unspecified
[C34797] Malaria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A protozoan infection caused by the genus Plasmodium. There are four species of Plasmodium that can infect humans: Plasmodium falciparum, vivax, ovale, and malariae. It is transmitted to humans by infected mosquitoes. Signs and symptoms include paroxysmal high fever, sweating, chills, and anemia.
[C34798] Falciparum Malaria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Malaria resulting from infection by Plasmodium falciparum.
[C34799] Quartan Malaria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Malaria resulting from infection by Plasmodium malariae.
[C348] Environmental Carcinogen -- **Semantic type:** Classification  **Definition:** A substance that can cause cancer in animals and/or humans and is found in the environment either as a contaminant or as a naturally occurring substance.
[C3480] Lobular Capillary Hemangioma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A friable, benign vascular neoplasm with lobular capillary architecture that presents as a raised red skin growth.  **Synonyms:** - Granulation Tissue-Type Hemangioma - Granuloma Pyogenicum - Granuloma Telangiecticum - Granulomata Pyogenicum - Hemangiomatous Granulation Tissue - Lobular Hemangioma - Pyogenic Granuloma - Pyogenic Granuloma
[C34800] Vivax Malaria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Malaria resulting from infection by Plasmodium vivax.
[C34801] Male Erectile Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder characterized by the persistent or recurrent inability to achieve or to maintain an erection during sexual activity.  **Synonyms:** - Erectile Dysfunction
[C34802] Malignant Essential Hypertension -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Essential hypertension with rapid progression to severe high blood pressure, papilledema, and renal failure.
[C34803] Primary Malignant Liver Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An epithelial or non-epithelial malignant neoplasm that arises from the liver. Representative examples include hepatocellular carcinoma, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma.  **Synonyms:** - Malignant neoplasm of liver, primary, unspecified as to type - Primary Malignant Neoplasm of Liver - Primary Malignant Neoplasm of Liver
[C34804] Malposition of Heart and Cardiac Apex -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Any anatomical misplacement of the heart and cardiac apex such that the organ occupies an unusual location within the body.
[C34805] Manic Bipolar Affective Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** The manic phase of bipolar disorder.
[C34806] Maple Syrup Urine Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An autosomal recessive inherited disorder caused by mutations in the BCKDHA, BCKDHB, DBT, and DLD genes. It is characterized by a deficiency of branched-chain alpha-keto acid dehydrogenase complex, leading to accumulation of metabolites in the body fluids. The name of the disease derives from the sweet odor of the urine in infants, reminiscent of maple syrup. Signs and symptoms usually appear in infancy and include lethargy and developmental delays. If untreated, it may lead to seizures, coma, and death.
[C34807] Marfan Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A genetic syndrome inherited as an autosomal dominant trait. It is caused by mutations in the FBN1 gene. It is characterized by tall stature, elongated extremities, mitral valve prolapse, aortic dilatation, aortic dissection, and subluxation of the lens.  **Synonyms:** - Marfan syndrome - Marfan's Syndrome - Marfan's Syndrome
[C34808] Mass -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A benign or malignant pathologic structure in any part of the body, resulting from a neoplastic accumulation of cells, inflammatory cells, or cystic changes.  **Synonyms:** - MASS - mass
[C34809] Maxillary Sinusitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An acute or chronic inflammatory process affecting the mucous membrane of the maxillary sinus.
[C3481] Nevus Araneus -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A type of telangiectasia comprising a central red spot with red, radiating extensions.  **Synonyms:** - Arterial Spider - Spider Angioma - Spider Nevus - Spider Telangiectasia - Spider Telangiectasis - Stellate Telangiectasis - Telangiectatic Nevus - Vascular Spider
[C34810] Megacolon -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An abnormal dilation of the colon not due to obstruction.
[C34811] Megaesophagus -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An abnormal dilation of the esophagus not due to obstruction.
[C34812] Melancholic Depression -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A subtype of depression characterized by the inability to find pleasure in positive things combined with physical agitation, insomnia, or decreased appetite.  **Synonyms:** - Depression with Melancholic Features - Melancholia
[C34813] Meningoencephalitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the meninges and brain, generally secondary to an infectious cause. Pathogens may be bacterial, viral, fungal, or protozoan.
[C34814] Menopausal Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A grouping of variable physical, vasomotor and psychological symptoms in climacteric females. Physical symptoms include: cessation of menses, headaches, fatigue, weight gain and vaginal dryness. Vasomotor symptoms typically include: palpitations, hot flashes and night sweats. Psychological symptoms may include: decrease in libido, emotional lability, difficulty concentrating and insomnia.
[C34815] Menstrual Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A category of conditions related to menses.  **Synonyms:** - Menstrual Cycle dysfunction - Menstrual Problem - Menstrual Problem
[C34816] Congenital Metabolic Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A group of disorders present at birth that involve genetic defects leading to disturbances in carbohydrate, lipid, lysosomal storage or amino acid metabolism in the body.  **Synonyms:** - Congenital Metabolism Disorder - Inborn Error of Metabolism - Inborn Errors of Metabolism
[C34817] Methemoglobinemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inherited or acquired condition characterized by abnormally increased levels of methemoglobin in the blood.  **Synonyms:** - methemoglobinemia
[C34818] Metrorrhagia -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Uterine bleeding that occurs during a time not associated with the normal menstrual period.  **Synonyms:** - Intermenstrual Bleeding
[C34819] Mikulicz Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare, persistent and bilateral enlargement of the lacrimal and salivary glands associated with lymphocytic infiltration of the affected glands. It is not associated with other systemic diseases.
[C3482] Metastatic Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A carcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to other anatomic sites.  **Synonyms:** - Carcinoma, metastatic, NOS
[C34820] Miliaria -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A small (one mm or less) vesicular, papular or pustular monomorphous rash, which is associated with heat, fever or occlusion of sweat glands.  **Synonyms:** - Heat Rash - Heat Rash - Prickly Heat - Prickly Heat
[C34821] Mixed Hyperlipidemia -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** A dyslipidemia characterized by elevated serum levels of total and low-density cholesterol, and triglycerides.
[C34822] Closed Monteggia's Fracture -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Proximal radial head dislocation in association with a proximal ulnar fracture that does not involve a break in the adjacent skin.
[C34823] Mosquito-Borne Viral Encephalitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Viral encephalitis that is transmitted by mosquitos.
[C34824] Motion Sickness -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** A sensation of discomfort that results from a discordant relationship between visualized movement and any movement sensed by the vestibular system, which is characterized by dizziness, nausea, and vomiting.  **Synonyms:** - Travel Sickness
[C34825] Kawasaki Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A vasculitis characterized by inflammation of the arteries, particularly the coronary arteries. The vasculitis may lead to aneurysm formation and possibly, heart attacks. It affects young children who usually present with persistent high fever, redness of the mucous membranes of the mouth, redness of the palms and soles, skin rashes, lymphadenitis, and joint pain and swelling.  **Synonyms:** - Infantile Polyarteritis Nodosa - Mucocutaneous Lymph Node Syndrome - Mucocutaneous Lymph Node Syndrome - Mucocutaneous lymph node syndrome [Kawasaki]
[C34826] Mucous Polyp of Cervix -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A polypoid lesion that arises from the cervix and contains mucus.
[C34827] Muscle Cramp -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** A sustained, sudden and involuntary contraction of a muscle or group of muscles.  **Synonyms:** - Cramp - Cramp(s) /Muscle Spasm(s) - Muscle Cramping - Spasm - cramp
[C34828] Flaccidity -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** Lack of normal muscle tone.
[C34829] Myocardial Degeneration -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Degeneration of myocardial tissue.
[C3483] Chronic Leukemia -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A slowly progressing leukemia characterized by a clonal (malignant) proliferation of maturing and mature myeloid cells or mature lymphocytes.  When the clonal cellular population is composed of myeloid cells, the process is called chronic myelogenous leukemia.  When the clonal cellular population is composed of lymphocytes, it is classified as chronic lymphocytic leukemia, hairy cell leukemia, or T-cell large granular lymphocyte leukemia.  **Synonyms:** - Chronic leukemia, NOS - chronic leukemia
[C34830] Cardiomyopathy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disease of the heart muscle or myocardium proper. Cardiomyopathies may be classified as either primary or secondary, on the basis of etiology, or on the pathophysiology of the lesion: hypertrophic, dilated, or restrictive.  **Synonyms:** - CARDIOMYOPATHY - Cardiomyopathy, NOS
[C34831] Myocarditis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the muscle tissue of the heart.
[C34832] Myoclonic Jerk -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A sudden, involuntary contraction of a muscle or group of muscles; these movements may develop as a symptom of a number of neurological diseases, including epilepsy, Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's disease, or Creutzfeldt-Jacob disease.  **Synonyms:** - myoclonic jerk
[C34833] Myotonic Cataract -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A cataract occurring as a sequela of myotonic dystrophy.
[C34834] Myxedema -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition characterized by severe hypothyroidism that is caused by autoimmune thyroid gland disorders, surgical reduction of thyroid tissue, radiation exposure, and viral infections.  Signs and symptoms include generalized fatigue, lethargy, increased body weight, pale, edematous and thickened skin, low blood pressure, constipation and cold intolerance.
[C34835] Nabothian Cyst -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A benign, mucus-filled cervical cyst that occurs when mucus-secreting columnar epithelial cells are covered with squamous epithelium.
[C34836] Ingrown Toenail -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Abnormality  **Definition:** A condition in which the nail grows into the nailbed secondary to downward pressure, generally caused by ill-fitting shoes. Symptoms include pain, erythema, and edema, and may result in infection if it remains untreated.
[C34837] Nasopharyngitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inflammatory process that affects the nasopharynx.
[C34838] Necatoriasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder caused by an infection with hookworms of the genus Necator, which settle in the host's small intestine, and cause abdominal pain, diarrhea, weight loss, and anemia.
[C34839] Neck Sprain -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Stretching or tearing of ligaments in the neck.
[C3484] Breast Adenosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic disorder characterized by epithelial and/or myoepithelial tissue growth in the breast lobules.  It may be associated with apocrine changes or sclerosis.  **Synonyms:** - Adenosis of Breast - Adenosis of the Breast - adenosis
[C34840] Necrobiosis Lipoidica -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An uncommon inflammatory skin condition, often associated with diabetes mellitus, that is characterized by the formation of multiple shiny atrophic lesions thought to be due to collagen degeneration with a granulomatous response, thickening of blood vessel walls, and fat deposition. The lesions are usually found on the leg.
[C34841] Avascular Necrosis of Bone -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Necrotic changes in the bone tissue due to interruption of blood supply. Most often affecting the epiphysis of the long bones, the necrotic changes result in the collapse and the destruction of the bone structure.  **Synonyms:** - Avascular necrosis
[C34842] Alport Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A genetic syndrome usually inherited as an X-link trait. It is caused by abnormalities in the COL4A5 gene. It affects males more often than females and is characterized by hematuria, progressive renal insufficiency, hearing loss, and ocular abnormalities.  **Synonyms:** - Alport's Syndrome - Hereditary Nephritis
[C34843] Nephropathy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A term referring to any disease affecting the kidneys.
[C34844] Minimal Change Glomerulonephritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A glomerular disorder characterized by the electron microscopic finding of loss of podocyte foot processes. Light microscopic examination does not show glomerular changes. Patients present with proteinuria and nephrotic syndrome.  **Synonyms:** - Lipoid Nephrosis - Lipoid Nephrosis - Minimal Change Disease - Minimal Change Disease - Minimal Change Nephropathy - Nil Disease
[C34845] Nephrotic Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A collection of symptoms that include severe edema, proteinuria, and hypoalbuminemia; it is indicative of renal dysfunction.  **Synonyms:** - Nephrosis
[C34846] Nervous Debility -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** An episode of acute, severe anxiety or depression associated with an inability to function as normal.
[C34847] Neuronitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic inflammatory disorder of neurons.
[C34848] Neurosis -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A form of functional mental illness that manifests in distressed emotional reactions such as anxiety, obsessive thoughts, compulsive behaviors, or irrational fears.
[C34849] Newcastle Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition caused by infection by the Newcastle disease virus, which may be characterized by conjunctivitis, respiratory illness, and diarrhea.
[C3485] Atonic Epilepsy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A type of seizure that is characterized by a sudden loss of muscle tone.
[C34850] Night Blindness -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inability to see clearly in dim light.  **Synonyms:** - Night blindness - Nyctalopia - Nyctalopia
[C34851] Increased Non-Protein Nitrogen -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** A laboratory test result indicating abnormally high concentration of non-protein nitrogen in the blood.  **Synonyms:** - Increased NPN - NPN Increased - Non-Protein Nitrogen Increased - Uremia - Uremia
[C34852] Gangrenous Stomatitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Gangrene of the mucous membranes of the mouth leading to ulcers and destruction of the soft tissues of the face and bones. It usually occurs in malnourished children in areas of poor sanitation and immunocompromised patients.  **Synonyms:** - Noma
[C34853] Non-Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Hemolytic anemia that is not mediated by immune mechanisms.
[C34854] Noonan Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A genetic syndrome caused by mutations in the PTPN11 gene (over 50% of the cases) or less frequently mutations in the SOS1, RAF1, or KRAS genes. It is characterized by short stature, webbed neck, hypertelorism, low-set ears, deafness, and thrombocytopenia or abnormal platelet function.  **Synonyms:** - Noonan syndrome - Noonan's Syndrome
[C34855] Norwegian Scabies -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare, severe form of scabies that is associated with immunosuppression. It is characterized by an immense number of mites and hyperkeratotic crusted lesions, and is usually accompanied by lymphadenopathy and eosinophilia.
[C34856] Acquired Deformity of Nose -- **Semantic type:** Acquired Abnormality  **Definition:** A non-congenital malformation of the nose.
[C34857] Numbness -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A loss of the sensation of feeling in an area of the body.  **Synonyms:** - Loss of Sensation
[C34858] Morbid Obesity -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** An excess of body weight, normally defined as an individual with a body mass index greater than 35 or a body weight greater than one hundred percent of ideal body weight.
[C34859] Occupational Dermatitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Contact dermatitis associated with allergens or irritants found in the workplace.
[C3486] Epithelioid Cell Type Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumor -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant gastrointestinal stromal tumor with epithelioid morphology.  **Synonyms:** - Epithelioid Cell Type GIST - Leiomyoblastoma - Leiomyoblastoma - Stout's Leiomyoblastoma
[C34860] Oligospermia -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Decreased number of spermatozoa in the semen.  **Synonyms:** - Low Sperm Count - Low Sperm Count
[C34861] Onchocerciasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disease caused by infection with Onchocerca volvulus; symptoms may include pruritis and blindness.  **Synonyms:** - River Blindness
[C34862] Ocular Onchocerciasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Onchocerciasis affecting the eye.
[C34863] Optic Atrophy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder characterized by loss of optic nerve fibers. It may be inherited or acquired. Acquired causes include ischemia, optic nerve neuropathy, glaucoma, trauma, radiation, brain tumors, and multiple sclerosis. It leads to vision disturbances.
[C34864] Hereditary Optic Atrophy -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A family of inherited disorders characterized by progressive loss of vision secondary to death of the retinal ganglion cell axons that comprise the optic nerve.
[C34865] Optical Illusion -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** The visual perception of an image that differs from the reality of the image.
[C34866] Oral Cavity Submucous Fibrosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Chronic, progressive, debilitating and non-reversible fibrosis of the submucosal tissues of the mouth.  It is associated with chewing betel quid, a habit practiced in Southeast Asia and India.  It is a precancerous condition and results in rigidity of the jaws and inability of the affected individual to open the mouth.
[C34867] Anxiety Disorder Due to a General Medical Condition -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** An anxiety disorder in which the symptoms of anxiety have been determined to be the direct physiological consequence of a general medical condition.
[C34868] Organic Brain Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A mental disorder caused by intrinsic disease which is characterized by acute or chronic impairment of intellectual functioning, behavior, mood or judgment. It is attributable to a medical etiology and excludes a primary psychiatric cause. A chronic course usually correlates to a poorer prognosis.
[C34869] Organic Delusional Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A mental disorder caused by intrinsic disease which is characterized by persistent or recurrent irrational beliefs. Consciousness and memory are not affected. It may be broadly classified as a psychotic disorder due to a general medical condition.
[C3487] Therapy-Related Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** The development of a neoplasm in response to medical or surgical treatment, induced by the treatment itself.  **Synonyms:** - Therapy Related Neoplasm - Therapy Related Tumor
[C34870] Organic Mental Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Any decrease in mental functioning in which the cause is not attributable to psychiatric illness, which includes damage from physical trauma, anoxic injury, damage from chemicals or toxins, infection, cancer, and degenerative diseases, including Alzheimer's disease, Huntington's disease, Parkinson's disease, and Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease.
[C34871] Organic Affective Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A mental disorder caused by intrinsic disease which is characterized by persistent alterations in mood that are directly attributable to the disease itself rather than a response to knowledge of the disease or other co-morbidity. It may be broadly classified as a depressive or bipolar disorder due to a general medical condition.
[C34872] Organic Personality Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A mental disorder caused by intrinsic disease which is characterized by persistent alteration of a known behavioral pattern. Changes in emotional stability, motivation, judgment or impulse control are usually noted. It is broadly classified as a personality change due to a general medical condition.
[C34873] Ornithosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Disease caused by the Chlamydophila psittaci bacteria, usually transmitted from birds to humans.
[C34874] Osgood-Schlatter's Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Osteochondrosis of the growth plate near the tibial tuberosity.
[C34875] Osteitis Fibrosa Cystica -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder that is characterized by bone cysts and fractures, resulting from hyperparathyroidism.  **Synonyms:** - Von Recklinghausen Disease of Bone - Von Recklinghausen's Disease of Bone
[C34876] Hip Osteoarthritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Osteoarthritis of the hip joint.  **Synonyms:** - Coxarthritis
[C34877] Osteochondritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the bone and cartilage.
[C34878] Osteochondritis Dissecans -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Osteochondritis that is characterized by fragmentation of cartilage and bone.
[C34879] Osteochondrosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition that is characterized by defective bone growth that affects the growth centers of bone.
[C3488] WDHA Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare syndrome characterized by severe watery diarrhea, hypokalemia, and achlorhydria. It is caused by the oversecretion of vasoactive intestinal peptide from the pancreatic islet cells.  **Synonyms:** - Islet Cell WDHA Syndrome - Pancreatic Cholera - Pancreatic WDHA Syndrome - Verner Morrison Syndrome - WDHH - Watery Diarrhea Syndrome - Watery Diarrhea with Hypokalemic Alkalosis - Watery Diarrhea, Hypokalemia, and Achlorhydria Syndrome
[C34880] Bone Necrosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Death of bone tissue due to traumatic or nontraumatic causes.  **Synonyms:** - Osteonecrosis - Osteonecrosis
[C34881] Congenital Deformity of Hip -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Any anatomic abnormality of the hip that is present at the time of birth.
[C34882] Rheumatic Heart Disease -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An autoinflammatory condition following an infection with Group A Beta Hemolytic Streptococcus (GABHS), in which the heart is attacked by antibodies formed in reaction to a recent GABHS infection. Chief anatomic changes of the valve include leaflet thickening, commissural fusion, and shortening and thickening of the tendinous cords, all of which can result in valvular dysfunction.  **Synonyms:** - RHD
[C34883] Multiple Dislocations -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** More than one dislocation.
[C34884] Otitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome
[C34885] Otitis Media -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the anatomical structures of the middle ear, which is most often caused by an infectious process. Symptoms include erythema and edema of the tympanic membrane, pain, and possibly fever.  **Synonyms:** - Medial Otitis - Middle Ear Inflammation - Middle Ear Inflammation - Middle ear inflammation
[C34886] Secretory Otitis Media -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Otitis media associated with accumulation of fluid in the middle ear.  **Synonyms:** - OME - Otitis Media with Effusion - Serous Otitis Media
[C34887] Post-Operative Pain -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** A sensation of discomfort secondary to surgery.  **Synonyms:** - Post Surgery Pain - Postoperative Pain
[C34888] Palmoplantar Pustulosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A chronic inflammatory disorder that generally occurs in adulthood and is characterized by sterile pustules intermingled with scaly erythemas, vesicles and hyperkeratosis occurring at distinct sites on the palms and soles. It is thought to be an autoimmune disorder precipitated by several factors, including local infection, and smoking.
[C34889] Pancytopenia -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** A finding of low numbers of red and white blood cells and platelets in the peripheral blood.
[C3489] Benign Penile Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the penis and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Neoplasm of Penis - Benign Neoplasm of the Penis - Benign Penile Tumor - Benign Tumor of Penis - Benign Tumor of the Penis
[C34890] Panic Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** An anxiety disorder characterized by multiple unexpected panic attacks with persistent concern of recurring attacks. Panic disorder may or may not be accompanied by agoraphobia.
[C34891] Paracoccidioidomycosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A systemic fungal infection caused by Paracoccidioides brasiliensis that is most often seen in immunocompromised patients. It affects the mucous membranes, lymph nodes, lungs and bones.
[C34892] Parakeratosis -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Abnormal retention of nuclei, and the resulting incomplete keratinization, of epithelial cells in the stratum corneum layer of the skin.
[C34893] Paraphimosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition in which the foreskin of an uncircumcised male is retracted and cannot be pulled back over the glans penis.  It results in painful swelling of the glans penis and, if is not corrected, may lead to gangrene.
[C34894] Paratyphoid Fever A -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Paratyphoid fever that is caused by infection with Salmonella enterica, serovar Paratyphi A.
[C34895] Paratyphoid Fever B -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Paratyphoid fever that is caused by infection with Salmonella enterica, serovar Paratyphi B.
[C34896] Paratyphoid Fever C -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Paratyphoid fever that is caused by infection with Salmonella enterica, serovar Paratyphi C.
[C34897] Paratyphoid Fever -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition resembling typhoid fever that is caused by infection by Salmonella enterica serovar Parathyphi.
[C34898] Postencephalitic Parkinsonism -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Parkinsonism resulting from encephalitis.  **Synonyms:** - Postencephalitic parkinsonism
[C34899] Secondary Parkinsonism -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition with a clinical picture similar to that of Parkinson disease, but which is caused by external factors, including medication.  **Synonyms:** - Secondary parkinsonism, unspecified
[C349] Carmustine -- **Semantic type:** Hazardous or Poisonous Substance|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** An antineoplastic nitrosourea. Carmustine alkylates and cross-links DNA during all phases of the cell cycle, resulting in disruption of DNA function, cell cycle arrest, and apoptosis.  This agent also carbamoylates proteins, including DNA repair enzymes, resulting in an enhanced cytotoxic effect.  Carmustine is highly lipophilic and crosses the blood-brain barrier readily. (NCI04)  **Synonyms:** - 1,3-Bis(2-chloroethyl)-1-nitrosourea - 1,3-Bis(beta-chloroethyl)-1-nitrosourea - BCNU - BCNU - Becenum - Becenun - BiCNU - Bis(chloroethyl) Nitrosourea - Bis-Chloronitrosourea - CARMUSTINE - Carmubris - Carmubris - Carmustin - Carmustinum - FDA 0345 - N,N'-Bis(2-chloroethyl)-N-nitrosourea - N,N'-bis(2-chloroethyl)-N-nitrosourea - NSC 409962 - NSC409962 - Nitrourean - Nitrumon - Nitrumon - SK 27702 - SRI 1720 - WR 139021 - WR-139021 - WR139021 - carmustine
[C3490] Lip Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A malignant epithelial neoplasm arising from the lips.  **Synonyms:** - Carcinoma of Lip - Carcinoma of the Lip - Lip Cancer
[C34900] Paroxysmal Atrial Tachycardia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder characterized by an electrocardiographic finding of episodic atrial tachycardia with abrupt onset and termination.  **Synonyms:** - PAT - Paroxysmal atrial tachycardia - True Paroxysmal Atrial Tachycardia
[C34901] Paroxysmal Supraventricular Tachycardia by ECG Finding -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An electrocardiographic finding of episodic supraventricular tachycardia with abrupt onset and termination.  **Synonyms:** - PSVT - Paroxysmal Supraventricular Tachycardia - Paroxysmal Supraventricular Tachycardia by EKG Finding
[C34902] Paroxysmal Ventricular Tachycardia by ECG Finding -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An episodic form of ventricular tachycardia, with abrupt onset and termination.  **Synonyms:** - PAROXYSMAL VENTRICULAR TACHYCARDIA - Paroxysmal Ventricular Tachycardia - Paroxysmal Ventricular Tachycardia - Paroxysmal Ventricular Tachycardia by EKG Finding
[C34903] Pars Planitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inflammatory disorder of the cilliary body in the uvea that affects healthy, younger individuals who are often asymptomatic. It has a long clinical course with relapses and remissions. Symptoms include mildly decreased vision and floaters. It may be associated with autoimmune disorders.
[C34904] Passive-Aggressive Personality Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A disorder characterized by a pattern of indirect expression of hostility, including negativistic attitudes and passive resistance to demands for adequate performance in social and occupational situations.  **Synonyms:** - Passive-Aggressive Personality
[C34905] Human Head Louse -- **Semantic type:** Eukaryote  **Definition:** Small parasitic insects that can cause a itching red rash to the skin of the waist, shoulders, armpits, neck and pubic area. Commonly spread from person-to-person via close contact. The eggs (nits) of head lice can be mistaken for dandruff, but they are adherent to the hair shafts.  **Synonyms:** - Head Lice - Head Louse - Pediculus humanus capitis
[C34906] Human Body Louse -- **Semantic type:** Eukaryote  **Definition:** Body lice are small, parasitic insects (Pediculus humanus corporis) that feed on human blood, and lay their eggs and deposit their fecal matter on the skin and on clothing. Body lice may be 2 - 3 mm long, as opposed to head and pubic lice which are much smaller. An infestation with body lice causes intense itching, and is transmitted by contact with infested clothing and bedding, as well as by direct contact with a carrier. Risk factors include overcrowded living and/or working conditions and poor hygiene.  **Synonyms:** - Body Lice - Body Louse - Pediculus humanus corporis
[C34907] Benign Mucous Membrane Pemphigoid -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A chronic autoimmune disorder characterized by the development of blisters and ulcers in mucous membranes. It affects most often the gums, eyelids and genital mucosa sites.
[C34908] Pemphigoid -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare autoimmune blistering skin disorder that resembles pemphigus but is histologically and clinically distinguishable by lack of evidence of acantholysis and a generally benign course.
[C34909] Pemphigus -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A blistering skin disorder. Morphologically it is characterized by acantholysis and intraepidermal blister formation.
[C3491] Cecum Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A malignant epithelial neoplasm that arises from the cecum and invades through the muscularis mucosa into the submucosa.  The vast majority are adenocarcinomas.  **Synonyms:** - Carcinoma of Cecum - Carcinoma of the Cecum - Cecal Cancer - Cecal Carcinoma - Cecum Cancer
[C34910] Pemphigus Vulgaris -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An autoimmune blistering disorder. It is characterized by the presence of painful blisters and erosions in the skin and mucous membranes.
[C34911] Penicillin Allergy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An allergy to Penicillin.
[C34912] Perceptual Distortion -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** An incorrect understanding or interpretation of sensory input.
[C34913] Periapical Dental Abscess -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Localized collection of pus in the tissues that enclose the root of a tooth.  **Synonyms:** - Dentoalveolar Abscess
[C34914] Periapical Abscess with Sinus -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Localized collection of pus in the tissues that enclose the root of a tooth and is associated with the presence of a sinus tract.

[C34920] Chronic Peritoneal Effusion -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Persistent or recurring accumulation of fluid in the peritoneal cavity.
[C34921] Ostium Primum Defect -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A congenital defect in the atrial septum at the level of the atrioventricular valves, resulting in abnormalities in the mitral and/or tricuspid valve; this defect is most commonly seen in those with Trisomy 21.
[C34922] Personality Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A diverse category of psychiatric disorders characterized by behavior that deviates markedly from the expectations of the individual's culture; this pattern of deviation is pervasive and inflexible and is stable over time. The behavioral pattern negatively interferes with relationships and work.
[C34923] Stump Hallucination -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** The sensation of the continued presence of an amputated limb.
[C34924] Pharyngoconjunctival Fever -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A condition characterized by fever, conjunctivitis, and pharyngitis resulting from infection by adenovirus.
[C34925] Phlegmon -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Purulent inflammation of the cellular or areolar tissue.
[C34926] School Phobia -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** An overwhelming, irrational, and persistent fear of attending school.
[C34927] Social Anxiety Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** An anxiety disorder characterized by an intense, irrational fear of one or more social or performance situations in which the individual believes that he or she will be scrutinized by others. Exposure to social situations immediately provokes an anxiety response. In adults, the social phobia is recognized as excessive or unreasonable.  **Synonyms:** - Social Phobia
[C34928] Phocomelia -- **Semantic type:** Congenital Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A congenital malformation in which the upper portion of a limb is either shortened or absent.  **Synonyms:** - PHOCOMELIA
[C34929] Photodermatitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome
[C3493] Lung Squamous Cell Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A carcinoma arising from squamous bronchial epithelial cells. It may be keratinizing or non-keratinizing. Keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma is characterized by the presence of keratinization, pearl formation, and/or intercellular bridges. Non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma is characterized by the absence of keratinization, pearl formation, and intercellular bridges. Cigarette smoking and arsenic exposure are strongly associated with squamous cell lung carcinoma.  **Synonyms:** - Epidermoid Cell Carcinoma of Lung - Epidermoid Cell Carcinoma of the Lung - Epidermoid Cell Lung Carcinoma - LSCC - LUSC - Squamous Cell Carcinoma of Lung - Squamous Cell Carcinoma of the Lung - Squamous Cell Lung Cancer - Squamous Cell Lung Carcinoma - Squamous Cell Lung Carcinoma - Squamous cell lung carcinoma
[C34930] Abnormal Pigmentation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An abnormality in the formation or distribution of pigment in the skin, hair or nails.  **Synonyms:** - Dyschromia - Dyspigmentation
[C34931] Pilonidal Cyst -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An abnormal, fluid-filled pocket of skin that is usually located in the natal cleft, and which also often contains hair and skin debris.  **Synonyms:** - Pilonidal Sinus
[C34932] Aspiration Pneumonitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the lungs due to the inhalation of solid or liquid material.  **Synonyms:** - Aspiration Pneumonia - Aspiration Pneumonia

[C34935] Baker Cyst -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Swelling and protrusion of the gastrocnemius-semimembranosus bursa due to an accumulation of synovial fluid.  **Synonyms:** - Baker's Cyst
[C34936] Post Kala-Azar Dermal Leishmaniasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A cutaneous form of leishmaniasis which sometimes occurs after visceral leishmaniasis treatment.  It is characterized by hypo-pigmented macules, papules, plaques, nodules, or facial erythema; and is considered to be a durable infection reservoir for visceral leishmaniasis.
[C34937] Postmenopausal Hemorrhage -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Uterine bleeding occurring after menopause.
[C34938] Postoperative Hemorrhage -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Bleeding occurring after completion of a surgical procedure, which may occur immediately or may be delayed, and which may or may not be wound related.  **Synonyms:** - Postoperative Bleeding - Postoperative hemorrhage
[C34939] Potassium Deficiency Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Any disorder caused by an insufficient amount or availability of potassium, which generally manifests with myalgia, tetany, hypotension, polyuria, and polydipsia.
[C3494] Lung Papillary Adenoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign lung neoplasm characterized by the presence of a fibrovascular stroma lined by cuboidal to columnar cells. Patients are usually asymptomatic and it is incidentally discovered as a pulmonary nodule during chest X-ray examination. Surgical excision is curative.  **Synonyms:** - ADENOMA, BRONCHIAL, BENIGN - Adenoma of Bronchus - Adenoma of the Bronchus - Bronchial Adenoma - Papillary Adenoma of Type II Pneumocytes - Peripheral Papillary Tumor of Type II Pneumocytes - Type II Pneumocyte Adenoma - bronchial adenoma
[C34940] Pre-Excitation Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An electrocardiographic finding characterized by a premature activation of the whole or some part of the ventricle. The PR interval is usually shortened and delta waves are frequently present. (CDISC)  **Synonyms:** - PRE-EXCITATION - Pre-excitation - Ventricular preexcitation
[C34941] Pregnancy Complication -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The co-occurrence of pregnancy and a disease. The disease may precede or follow conception and it may or may not have a deleterious effect on the pregnant woman or fetus.  **Synonyms:** - Complication Related to Pregnancy
[C34942] Gestational Diabetes -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Carbohydrate intolerance first diagnosed during pregnancy.  **Synonyms:** - Diabetes in Pregnancy - Gestational Diabetes Mellitus - Gestational Diabetes Mellitus
[C34943] Toxemia of Pregnancy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A pregnancy induced hypertensive state that occurs after 20 weeks of gestation characterized by an increase in blood pressure, along with body swelling and proteinuria.
[C34944] Abdominal Pregnancy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome
