[C35699] Diffuse Infiltrative Lymphocytosis Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** This is usually an oligoclonal CD8+ lymphocytic infiltration of various organs.  **Synonyms:** - Diffuse infiltra. lymph. syndrome - Diffuse infiltrative lymphocytosis syndrome
[C357] Cephalosporin Antibiotic -- **Semantic type:** Chemical Viewed Functionally  **Definition:** A synthetic or semi-synthetic beta-lactam antibiotic derived from Acremonium (Cephalosporium) fungi with bactericidal activity. Cephalosporin antibiotics bind to and inactivate penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) located on the inner membrane of the bacterial cell wall. Inactivation of PBPs interferes with the cross-linkage of peptidoglycan chains necessary for bacterial cell wall strength and rigidity. This interrupts bacterial cell wall synthesis and results in the weakening of the bacterial cell wall, eventually causing cell lysis.  **Synonyms:** - Cephalosporins - cephalosporin
[C3570] Malignant Brain Stem Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the brain stem.  **Synonyms:** - Malignant Brain Stem Tumor - Malignant Brainstem Neoplasm - Malignant Brainstem Tumor - Malignant Neoplasm of Brain Stem - Malignant Neoplasm of Brainstem - Malignant Neoplasm of the Brain Stem - Malignant Neoplasm of the Brainstem - Malignant Tumor of Brain Stem - Malignant Tumor of Brainstem - Malignant Tumor of the Brain Stem - Malignant Tumor of the Brainstem - Malignant neoplasm of brain stem
[C35700] Salivary Gland Myoepithelial Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A locally aggressive carcinoma that arises from the salivary glands, predominantly the parotid gland. It is characterized by the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate exhibiting myoepithelial differentiation. Patients usually present with a painless mass.
[C35701] Salivary Gland Epithelial-Myoepithelial Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A carcinoma that arises from the salivary glands, most often the parotid gland.  It presents as a slow growing and painless mass.  It is characterized by the presence of duct-like structures lined by two layers of cells, an inner layer composed of epithelial-type cells and an outer layer composed of clear, myoepithelial-type cells.  **Synonyms:** - Salivary Gland Epithelial Myoepithelial Carcinoma
[C35702] Salivary Gland Polymorphous Adenocarcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A low grade malignant epithelial neoplasm arising from the salivary gland. It usually arises from the oral cavity, with approximately 60% of the cases involving the palate. The most common presenting sign is a painless mass in the palate. It is characterized by the presence of uniform, small to medium size malignant epithelial cells and an infiltrating pattern. There is a variety of morphologic patterns which may be present within an individual tumor, including lobular, papillary, papillary-cystic, cribriform, and trabecular pattern. It usually has a favorable clinical outcome and a low metastatic potential.  **Synonyms:** - Polymorphous adenocarcinoma, classic - Polymorphous low grade adenocarcinoma - Salivary Gland PmA - Salivary Gland Polymorphous Adenocarcinoma, Classical Variant - Salivary Gland Polymorphous Low Grade Adenocarcinoma - Salivary Gland Terminal Duct Adenocarcinoma
[C35703] Salivary Gland Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A rare neuroendocrine carcinoma that arises from the salivary gland and is composed of malignant small cells. The mitotic count is more than 10 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.

[C35709] Aagenaes Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare genetic syndrome which occurs primarily among individuals of Norwegian descent with an autosomal recessive pattern of inheritance. It is caused, in some cases, by the inheritance of a mutation of the LSC1 gene on chromosome 15. Clinical signs include lymphedema of the lower extremities and cholestasis. The clinical course includes giant-cell hepatitis and progression to cirrhosis.  **Synonyms:** - CHLS - Cholestasis-Lymphedema Syndrome - LCS - LCS1 - Lymphedema-Cholestasis Syndrome - Lymphedema-Cholestasis Syndrome 1

[C35713] Nodular Lymphoid Hyperplasia of Lung -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare, reactive lesion in the lung parenchyma. It is characterized by the formation of a single or several nodules that are composed of lymphocytic infiltrates with reactive germinal centers.
[C35714] Idiopathic Interstitial Pneumonia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Non-infectious inflammation of the interstitial lung tissue of unknown etiology. This category includes desquamative interstitial pneumonia, usual interstitial pneumonia, lymphocytic interstitial pneumonia, acute interstitial pneumonia, and nonspecific interstitial pneumonia.  **Synonyms:** - IIP - Idiopathic Interstitial Pneumonitis
[C35715] Usual Interstitial Pneumonia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Interstitial pneumonia characterized by the presence of fibrosis in the interstitial lung tissue.  The pathologic diagnosis is based on the identification of fibrotic lesions at different stages of development within a lung biopsy specimen.  Typically there are foci of normal lung parenchyma alternating with interstitial inflammation and honeycombing. The term usual interstitial pneumonia sometimes is used interchangeably with idiopathic interstitial fibrosis.  The two terms are not entirely synonymous.  Usual interstitial pneumonia may be associated with other conditions such as connective tissue disorders and asbestosis.  The diagnosis of idiopathic interstitial fibrosis requires the exclusion of such conditions.  Patients with usual interstitial pneumonia present with progressive dyspnea, cough, and restrictive pulmonary function abnormalities.  The prognosis is usually poor.  **Synonyms:** - UIP
[C35716] Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Chronic and progressive fibrosis of the lung parenchyma of unknown cause.  **Synonyms:** - IPF - idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
[C35717] Nonspecific Interstitial Pneumonia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Idiopathic interstitial pneumonia characterized by chronic inflammation and fibrosis in the interstitial lung tissue.  It includes cases that cannot be classified into one of the other types of idiopathic interstitial pneumonia.  **Synonyms:** - NSIP
[C35718] Thymus Undifferentiated Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A rare primary thymus carcinoma characterized by an undifferentiated solid tumor growth, without associated sarcomatoid features.  **Synonyms:** - Thymic Undifferentiated Carcinoma - Thymic Undifferentiated Carcinoma

[C35759] Metastasizing Pleomorphic Adenoma of the Salivary Gland -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A very rare pleomorphic adenoma of the salivary glands that metastasizes inexplicably, despite the benign histopathologic features. [C35764] Wernicke-Korsakoff Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A syndrome caused by thiamine deficiency. It usually occurs in alcoholics and is characterized by confusion, ataxia, and ophthalmoplegia.  **Synonyms:** - Wernicke Encephalopathy

[C35773] Bile Duct Stone -- **Semantic type:** Body Substance  **Synonyms:** - Bile Duct Calculus

[C35777] Pediculosis Pubis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Infestation of the pubic hair by the pthirus pubis parasite which results in mild to intense itching and macular lesions. The parasite, also known as crab lice, is transmitted through skin to skin contact with an infected person or through direct contact with infested objects.  **Synonyms:** - Phthiriasis
[C35778] Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumor of the Gastrointestinal Tract -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A stromal tumor that originates from the interstitial cells of Cajal. It may involve any part of the gastrointestinal tract. The majority of cases occur in the stomach. Morphologic variants include the epithelioid cell type, spindle cell type, and mixed cell type. Most cases contain KIT- or PDGFRA-activating mutations. Lesions with a diameter greater than 10cm and more than 5 mitotic figures per 50HPF tend to have a more aggressive clinical course. Until recently, surgery has been the only effective therapy for this tumor. However, many patients still experience recurrence. A KIT tyrosine kinase inhibitor, imatinib mesylate (also known as STI-571 or Gleevec), is now effective in the treatment of relapsed and unresectable cases.  **Synonyms:** - Digestive Stromal Neoplasm - Digestive Stromal Tumor - Digestive Tract Stromal Neoplasm - Digestive Tract Stromal Tumor - Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumor (GIST) of the Gastrointestinal Tract - Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumor of Gastrointestinal Tract - Gastrointestinal Tract Stromal Neoplasm - Gastrointestinal Tract Stromal Tumor - Stromal Neoplasm of Digestive Tract - Stromal Neoplasm of Gastrointestinal Tract - Stromal Neoplasm of the Digestive Tract - Stromal Neoplasm of the Gastrointestinal Tract - Stromal Tumor of Digestive Tract - Stromal Tumor of Gastrointestinal Tract - Stromal Tumor of the Digestive Tract - Stromal Tumor of the Gastrointestinal Tract
[C35779] Non-Neoplastic Dental Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic disorder that affects the teeth and/or the periodontal tissues.  Representative examples include odontoma, enamel hypoplasia, and periodontitis.

[C35780] Uveal Epithelioid Cell Melanoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A uveal melanoma characterized by the presence of malignant large epithelioid melanocytes.  **Synonyms:** - Epithelioid Cell Uveal Melanoma - Epithelioid Cell Uveal Melanoma
[C35781] Uveal Mixed Epithelioid and Spindle Cell Melanoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A melanoma that arises from the choroid, ciliary body, or iris and is characterized by the presence of a mixture of spindle A melanoma cells, spindle B melanoma cells, and epithelioid melanoma cells.  **Synonyms:** - Mixed Cell Uveal Melanoma - Mixed Cell Uveal Melanoma - Uveal Mixed Cell Melanoma

[C35786] Interstitial Myocarditis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the heart characterized by infiltration of the interstitial tissues by inflammatory cells, histiocytes, and the formation of granulomas. Giant cells are often present.
[C35787] Unstable Personality -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** Lability of an individual's outward presentation, which is characterized by unpredictable social behavior or uneven mood.
[C35788] Hypercellular Bone Marrow -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** An abnormal finding in a bone marrow aspirate or biopsy specimen indicating increased cellularity. [C35790] Congenital Alopecia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A congenital condition characterized by the absence of hair on the scalp or entire body. The lack of hair is rarely absolute and is usually accompanied by incompletely grown, lanugo-like hair.  It affects males twice as much as females and a familial tendency is common.
[C35791] Passive-Aggressive Behavior -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A pattern of behavior characterized by expressing one's negative attitudes and emotions in an indirect fashion.
[C35792] Xanthogranulomatous Cholecystitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Cholecystitis that is characterized by nodules containing lipid.

[C35794] Pagetoid Reticulosis -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A variant of mycosis fungoides, characterized by an exclusively intraepidermal atypical (cerebriform) lymphocytic infiltrate.  Patients present with a localized psoriasiform or hyperkeratotic patch or plaque, usually in the extremities.  Extracutaneous dissemination of the disease has never been reported.  **Synonyms:** - Pagetoid reticulosis
[C35795] Acrofacial Dysostosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A group of rare genetic syndromes characterized by craniofacial and limb defects.

[C35797] Demyelinating Encephalopathy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Encephalopathy characterized by inflammation and damage to nerve sheaths.
[C35798] Anti-Basement Membrane Glomerulonephritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the glomeruli secondary to presence of autoantibodies directed at specific antigenic targets within the glomerular basement membrane, causing hematuria, proteinuria, and impaired renal function.  **Synonyms:** - Anti-GBM Antibody Disease - Anti-Glomerular Basement Membrane Antibody Disease
[C35799] Diffuse Glomerulonephritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the glomeruli, in which all glomeruli are affected, resulting in renal failure.
[C358] Cephalothin Sodium -- **Semantic type:** Antibiotic|Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** The sodium salt form of cephalothin, a semisynthetic, beta-lactam, first-generation cephalosporin antibiotic with bactericidal activity. Cephalothin binds to and inactivates penicillin-binding proteins (PBP) located on the inner membrane of the bacterial cell wall. PBPs participate in the terminal stages of assembling the bacterial cell wall, and in reshaping the cell wall during cell division. Inactivation of PBPs interferes with the cross-linkage of peptidoglycan chains necessary for bacterial cell wall strength and rigidity. This results in the weakening of the bacterial cell wall and causes cell lysis.  **Synonyms:** - CEPHALOTHIN SODIUM - Keflin
[C3580] Metastatic Malignant Neoplasm in the Bone -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A malignant neoplasm that has spread to the bone from another anatomic site.  **Synonyms:** - Bone Metastases - Bone Metastasis - Metastases to bone, NOS - Metastatic Cancer to the Bone - Metastatic Malignant Neoplasm to the Bone - Metastatic Malignant Neoplasm to the Bone - Metastatic Neoplasm to the Bone - Metastatic Tumor to the Bone - Secondary malignant neoplasm of bone - bone metastasis
[C35800] Immune Complex Glomerulonephritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Inflammation of the glomeruli characterized by the accumulation of antibody-antigen immune complexes, resulting in glomerular damage and impaired kidney function.
[C35801] Subacute Glomerulonephritis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A term that refers to glomerular damage resulting in hematuria, proteinuria, and azotemia. The histopathologic changes include rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis and membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis.
[C35802] Postpartum Hypertension -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Maternal hypertension noted following delivery.

[C35805] Ancylostomiasis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Infection by hookworms of the genus Ancylostoma.
[C35806] Acute Interstitial Pneumonia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare, rapidly progressing interstitial lung disorder characterized by diffuse and bilateral alveolar damage.  Patients present with sudden onset of dyspnea and rapid development of respiratory failure.  Management is supportive and hospitalization and mechanical ventilation are usually required.  **Synonyms:** - AIP - Acute Interstitial Pneumonitis
[C35807] Hypercalcemic Sarcoidosis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Sarcoidosis complicated by hypercalcemia due to overproduction of 1-alpha hydroxylase.
[C35808] Abstinence Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A syndrome which occurs following withdrawal of an abused substance. Clinical signs vary depending on the substance that was abused and may include irritability, trembling and vomiting. The clinical course depends on the ability to manage the withdrawal symptoms.
[C35809] Achard Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare genetic syndrome featuring connective tissue abnormalities. Clinical signs include brachycephaly, arachnodactyly, receding mandible and joint laxity at the hands and feet.
[C3581] Ann Arbor Stage III Nodular Sclerosis Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** Ann Arbor Classification: Stage III: Involvement of lymph node regions on both sides of the diaphragm (III), which also may be accompanied by extralymphatic extension in association with adjacent lymph node involvement (IIIE) or by involvement of the spleen (IIIS) or both (IIIE,S).  **Synonyms:** - Ann Arbor Stage III Nodular Sclerosis Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma - Nodular Sclerosis Hodgkin's Disease Stage III - Nodular Sclerosis Hodgkin's Lymphoma Stage III - Stage III Nodular Sclerosis Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Nodular Sclerosis Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Nodular Sclerosis Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Nodular Sclerosis Hodgkin's Disease - Stage III Nodular Sclerosis Hodgkin's Lymphoma
[C35810] Acroparesthesia Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder which affects sensation in the hands. Clinical signs usually include tingling or numbness with onset in middle-age. There is a female predominance among those affected.
[C35811] Adherence Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A disorder which limits movement of the eye following ocular surgery. It is caused by the inadvertent scarring down of the middle portion of the inferior oblique muscle at or near the insertion of the inferior rectus muscle. Clinical signs include hypotropia and excyclotropia. Surgical release of the incarcerated segment will usually correct the deviation of the affected eye.
[C35812] Malignant Lymph Node Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A primary or metastatic malignant tumor involving the lymph node.  Lymphomas and metastatic carcinomas are representative examples.  **Synonyms:** - Lymph Node Cancer - Lymph Node Cancer
[C35813] Hematopoietic and Lymphatic System Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant (primary or metastatic) neoplasm that affects the hematopoietic and lymphatic system.  **Synonyms:** - Hematopoietic and Lymphoid System Neoplasm - Hematopoietic and Lymphoid System Neoplasm - Hematopoietic and Lymphoid System Tumor - Neoplasm of uncertain behavior of lymphoid, hematopoietic and related tissue, unspecified
[C35814] Hematopoietic and Lymphatic System Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the hematopoietic and lymphatic system.  **Synonyms:** - Hematopoietic and Lymphoid System Disorder
[C35815] Granulocytic Sarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A tumor mass composed of myeloblasts, neutrophils and neutrophil precursors.  Granulocytic sarcoma is the most common type of myeloid sarcoma.  (WHO, 2001)  **Synonyms:** - Granulocytic sarcoma - SARCOMA, GRANULOCYTIC, MALIGNANT - granulocytic sarcoma
[C35816] Monoblastic Sarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A less common form of myeloid sarcoma composed of monoblasts.  Monoblastic sarcoma may precede or occur simultaneously with acute monoblastic leukemia. (WHO 2001)
[C35817] Blastic Granulocytic Sarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A granulocytic sarcoma composed primarily of myeloblasts (WHO 2001).  **Synonyms:** - Myeloblastoma
[C35818] Immature Granulocytic Sarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A granulocytic sarcoma composed of myeloblasts and promyelocytes (WHO 2001).
[C35819] Differentiated Granulocytic Sarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A granulocytic sarcoma composed of promyelocytes and more mature neutrophils (WHO 2001).
[C3582] Ann Arbor Stage III Mixed Cellularity Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** Ann Arbor Classification: Stage III: Involvement of lymph node regions on both sides of the diaphragm (III), which also may be accompanied by extralymphatic extension in association with adjacent lymph node involvement (IIIE) or by involvement of the spleen (IIIS) or both (IIIE,S).  **Synonyms:** - Ann Arbor Stage III Mixed Cellularity Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma - Mixed Cellularity Hodgkin's Disease Stage III - Mixed Cellularity Hodgkin's Lymphoma Stage III - Stage III Mixed Cellularity Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Mixed Cellularity Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Mixed Cellularity Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Mixed Cellularity Hodgkin's Disease - Stage III Mixed Cellularity Hodgkin's Lymphoma
[C35820] Reticulin Fibrosis of the Bone Marrow -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Increased reticular fiber deposition within bone marrow.
[C35821] Collagen Fibrosis of the Bone Marrow -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating increased reticulin and collagen deposition in the bone marrow.
[C35822] Perivascular Fibrosis -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The accumulation of fibrotic tissue around blood vessels leading to impairment of blood flow and eventual damage to the end organ.
[C35823] Splenic Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder affecting the spleen. Examples include infection, hemangioma, lymphoma, leukemia and angiosarcoma.  **Synonyms:** - Spleen Disorder - Spleen Disorder - Spleen disorder
[C35824] Amputation Neuroma -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A neuroma that occurs at the site of an amputation.
[C35825] Megaloblastic Erythroid Hyperplasia -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** A laboratory test result indicating an abnormally high quantity of abnormal immature red blood cells with megaloblastic features.
[C35826] Foveolar Hyperplasia -- **Semantic type:** Pathologic Function  **Definition:** A hyperplasia of the gastric foveolar epithelium.
[C35827] Riedel Fibrosing Thyroiditis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An inflammatory disorder of unknown etiology that affects the thyroid gland. It is characterized by extensive fibrosis of the thyroid parenchyma. The fibrosis extends beyond the thyroid gland capsule to the adjacent structures.  **Synonyms:** - Riedel Thyroiditis - Riedel's Fibrosing Thyroiditis
[C35828] Subacute Granulomatous Thyroiditis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Self-limited inflammation of the thyroid gland characterized by the presence of multinucleated giant cells. Patients present with neck pain, often associated with fever and dysphagia. The clinical course includes an initial phase of hyperthyroidism, followed by a phase of hypothyroidism, and eventually a return to normal thyroid function.  **Synonyms:** - De Quervain Thyroiditis - De Quervain's Thyroiditis - DeQuervain Thyroiditis
[C35829] Subacute Lymphocytic Thyroiditis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Thyroiditis associated with painless enlargement of the thyroid gland.  It occurs more frequently in females and is characterized by alterations between hyperthyroidism and hypothyroidism and the eventual return to normal thyroid gland function.  **Synonyms:** - Silent Thyroiditis - Subacute Painless Thyroiditis
[C3583] Ann Arbor Stage III Lymphocyte-Depleted Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** Ann Arbor Classification: Stage III: Involvement of lymph node regions on both sides of the diaphragm (III), which also may be accompanied by extralymphatic extension in association with adjacent lymph node involvement (IIIE) or by involvement of the spleen (IIIS) or both (IIIE,S).  **Synonyms:** - Ann Arbor Stage III Lymphocyte-Depleted Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Hodgkin's Disease Lymphocyte Depletion Type - Stage III Hodgkin's Lymphoma Lymphocyte Depleted - Stage III Hodgkin's Lymphoma Lymphocyte Depletion Type - Stage III Lymphocyte Depleted Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Lymphocyte Depleted Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Lymphocyte Depleted Hodgkin's Lymphoma - Stage III Lymphocyte-Depleted Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Lymphocyte-Depleted Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma
[C35830] Columnar Cell Variant Thyroid Gland Papillary Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A morphologic variant of papillary carcinoma of the thyroid gland characterized by the presence of pseudostratified malignant follicular cells.  **Synonyms:** - Columnar Cell Variant Papillary Carcinoma - Columnar Cell Variant Papillary Thyroid Gland Carcinoma - Papillary Thyroid Carcinoma, Columnar Variant
[C35831] Salivary Gland Carcinosarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An aggressive, high grade malignant tumor that arises from the salivary gland. It is characterized by the presence of a malignant epithelial and a sarcomatous component.  **Synonyms:** - Carcinosarcoma of Salivary Gland - Carcinosarcoma of the Salivary Gland - True Malignant Mixed Tumor of the Salivary Gland
[C35832] Parotid Gland Carcinosarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An aggressive, high grade malignant tumor that arises from the parotid gland. It is characterized by the presence of a malignant epithelial and a sarcomatous component.
[C35833] Salivary Gland Cystadenoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An uncommon benign epithelial tumor, characterized by predominantly unicystic or multicystic growth; there is focal intraluminal papillary proliferation of the lining epithelium. Nearly half occur in the parotid gland.  In the minor salivary glands, women are affected more than men. In the major salivary glands, cystadenomas present as slowly enlarging, asymptomatic masses that may be slightly compressible. Cystadenocarcinoma is the malignant counterpart.
[C35834] Salivary Gland Intraductal Papilloma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An intraluminal papillary proliferation that causes unicystic dilatation of the duct and may have features in common with papillary cystadenoma. It is quite rare, and involves almost exclusively the excretory ducts of intraoral minor salivary glands. It presents as an asymptomatic submucosal swelling.
[C35835] Salivary Gland Inverted Ductal Papilloma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A very rare benign tumor of the minor salivary glands, characterized by a luminal papillary proliferation that arises at the junction of the salivary gland duct and the oral mucosal surface epithelium and expands as a nodular mass into the lamina propria.  It presents as an asymptomatic mucosal swelling.
[C35836] Salivary Gland Sialadenoma Papilliferum -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An uncommon exophytic papillary proliferation that involves mucosal surface epithelium and salivary duct epithelium. It is exceptional among salivary gland neoplasms because it manifests as an exophytic papillary excrescence of the mucosa, rather than as a submucosal or intraglandular mass. It most often affects the hard or soft palate.  It is generally asymptomatic.
[C35837] Sialoblastoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A rare, malignant primitive neoplasm that occurs in the salivary glands. Most tumors arise from the parotid gland, followed by the submandibular gland, and rarely the minor salivary glands. It is usually diagnosed during the neonatal period and presents with painless face swelling.  **Synonyms:** - Salivary Gland Sialoblastoma
[C35838] Adrenal Medullary Hyperplasia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Congenital or acquired hyperplasia of the cells of the adrenal medulla.
[C35839] Salivary Gland Ductal Papilloma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign papillary neoplasm that arises from the salivary glands.  This category includes intraductal papilloma, inverted ductal papilloma, and sialadenoma papilliferum.
[C3584] Ann Arbor Stage III Hodgkin Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** Ann Arbor Classification: Stage III: Involvement of lymph node regions on both sides of the diaphragm (III), which also may be accompanied by extralymphatic extension in association with adjacent lymph node involvement (IIIE) or by involvement of the spleen (IIIS) or both (IIIE,S).  **Synonyms:** - Hodgkin's Disease Stage III - Hodgkin's Lymphoma Stage III - Stage III Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage III Hodgkin's Disease - Stage III Hodgkin's Lymphoma - Stage IIIA Hodgkin Lymphoma - Stage IIIB Hodgkin Lymphoma

[C35847] Liver Dysplastic Foci -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the presence of single or multiple foci of hepatocytes that exhibit dysplastic changes.  These microscopic foci measure less than 1 mm in diameter and are usually detected in cirrhotic livers.  **Synonyms:** - Liver Dysplastic Focus
[C35848] Large Cell Dysplasia -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the presence of dysplastic changes in neoplastic large cells.

[C35850] Head and Neck Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A carcinoma that arises from the head and neck region. [C35851] Grade 1 Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A clear cell renal cell carcinoma characterized by absent or inconspicuous and basophilic nucleoli at x 400 magnification. (WHO 1982 /ISUP 2014)  **Synonyms:** - G1 Nuclei round, uniform, approximately 10 um; nucleoli inconspicuous or absent - Grade 1 Conventional (Clear Cell) Renal Cell Carcinoma
[C35852] Grade 2 Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A clear cell renal cell carcinoma characterized by nucleoli conspicuous and eosinophilic at x 400 magnification, and visible but not prominent at x 100 magnification. [C35853] Grade 3 Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A clear cell renal cell carcinoma characterized by nucleoli conspicuous and eosinophilic at x 100 magnification. (WHO 1982 /ISUP 2014)  **Synonyms:** - G3 Nuclei very irregular, approximately 20 um; nucleoli large and prominent - Grade 3 Conventional (Clear Cell) Renal Cell Carcinoma
[C35854] Grade 4 Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A clear cell renal cell carcinoma characterized by extreme nuclear pleomorphism and/or sarcomatoid and/or rhabdoid differentiation and/or tumor giant cells. (WHO, 1982 /ISUP 2014)  **Synonyms:** - G4 Nuclei bizarre and multilobated, 20 um or greater, nucleoli prominent, chromatin clumped - Grade 4 Conventional (Clear Cell) Renal Cell Carcinoma

[C35868] Maxillary Sinus Cholesteatoma -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A rare, progressive, non-neoplastic pathologic process that arises from the maxillary sinus mucosal epithelium.  It is characterized by the proliferation of keratinizing squamous epithelium and the formation of keratin sheets.  It may lead to bone erosion and infections.  Surgical removal is the appropriate treatment.
[C35869] Radiologic Finding -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Imaging results obtained through a radiology procedure.  **Synonyms:** - RADIOLOGIC FINDING
[C3587] Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia in Remission -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A finding of chronic lymphocytic leukemia that is not growing and responds to treatment.  **Synonyms:** - CLL in Remission - Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia (CLL) in Remission - Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia (in Remission) - Chronic Lymphogenous Leukemia in Remission - Chronic Lymphoid Leukemia in Remission - Chronic lymphocytic leukemia of B-cell type in remission
[C35870] Conventional Osteosarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A high grade malignant bone-forming mesenchymal neoplasm producing osteoid. The tumor arises from the medullary portion of the bone. It affects the long bones and most commonly, the distal femur, proximal tibia, and proximal humerus. Pain with or without a palpable mass is the most common clinical presentation. It usually has an aggressive growth and may metastasize through the hematogenous route. The lung is the most frequent site of metastasis.  **Synonyms:** - COS - Central - Central Osteosarcoma - Central osteosarcoma - Conventional Central Osteosarcoma - Conventional central osteosarcoma - Intracortical Osteogenic Sarcoma - Intracortical Osteosarcoma - Intracortical osteosarcoma - Medullary Osteosarcoma - Medullary osteosarcoma
[C35871] Ewing Sarcoma/Peripheral Primitive Neuroectodermal Tumor of Bone -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A spectrum of malignant tumors arising from the bone and characterized morphologically by the presence of small round cells.  Ewing sarcoma and peripheral primitive neuroectodermal tumor represent the ends of a spectrum, with Ewing sarcoma lacking evidence of neural differentiation and the markers that characterize the peripheral primitive neuroectodermal tumor.  Ewing sarcoma and peripheral primitive neuroectodermal tumor may share cytogenetic abnormalities, proto-oncogene expression, cell culture and immunohistochemical abnormalities.  Pain and the presence of a mass are the most common clinical symptoms.  **Synonyms:** - Ewing's Sarcoma/Peripheral Primitive Neuroectodermal Tumor of Bone
[C35873] Iatrogenic Kaposi Sarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A Kaposi sarcoma that develops after organ transplantation or immunosuppressive treatment.  **Synonyms:** - Iatrogenic Kaposi's Sarcoma
[C35874] Endemic African Kaposi Sarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A Kaposi sarcoma that occurs in adults and children in Equatorial Africa.  **Synonyms:** - Endemic African Kaposi's Sarcoma
[C35875] Bronchogenic Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A lung carcinoma arising from the bronchial epithelium.  **Synonyms:** - Bronchial Carcinoma - Bronchiogenic Carcinoma - Bronchogenic Lung Cancer - Bronchogenic Lung Carcinoma - Bronchus Cancer - CARCINOMA, BRONCHIAL, MALIGNANT - bronchogenic carcinoma

[C359] Ceramide -- **Semantic type:** Biologically Active Substance|Organic Chemical  **Definition:** One of a number of a class of sphingolipids, N-acyl derivatives with long chains. Ceramide is the core molecule for the synthesis of sphingomyelin, an essential lipid for myelination and neurotransmission; it may function as a second messenger to stimulate differentiation, inhibit proliferation, and induce apoptosis. (NCI04)  **Synonyms:** - Ceramide (lipids) - N-Acyl Sphingosine - ceramide

[C35957] Tumor Cell Necrosis -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding that indicates the presence of cells undergoing necrosis within a tumor.
[C35958] Comedo-Like Necrosis -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A pattern of cell death that resembles central necrosis within a cancerous ductal space.
[C35959] Mitotic Activity -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Having to do with the presence of dividing (proliferating) cells. Cancerous tissue generally has more mitotic activity than normal tissues.  **Synonyms:** - Mitosis (Y/N) - Mitotic Rate - mitotic activity
[C3596] Benign Hypopharyngeal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the hypopharynx and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Hypopharyngeal Tumor - Benign Neoplasm of Hypopharynx - Benign Neoplasm of the Hypopharynx - Benign Tumor of Hypopharynx - Benign Tumor of the Hypopharynx - Hypopharyngeal Neoplasm, Benign
[C35960] High Mitotic Activity -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The presence of a high number of mitotic figures in a malignant tumor sample. It is associated with a higher rate of tumor recurrence and distant metastases, and a shorter survival.
[C35961] Low Mitotic Activity -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Decreased frequency of cellular mitosis.
[C35962] Atypical Mitotic Figures -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the presence of atypical mitoses in malignant cells.
[C35963] Vascular Morphologic Change -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An alteration in the architecture of a blood vessel.
[C35964] Cavernous-Like Vascular Space Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The development of a structure that mimics the appearance of a cavernous vascular space on imaging.
[C35965] Cavernous Lymphatic Space Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The development of enlarged lymphatic channels lined by endothelial cells.
[C35966] Dilated Thick-Walled Vascular Space Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the development of enlarged blood vessels with thick walls.
[C35967] Dilated Thin-Walled Vascular Space Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the development of enlarged blood vessels with thin walls.
[C35968] Intravascular Thrombus Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the development of a blood clot within a blood vessel.
[C35969] Thin-Walled Branching Vascular Pattern -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding in which the blood vessel walls in a specimen appear thinned and ramified.
[C3597] Benign Pharyngeal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the pharynx and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Neoplasm of Pharynx - Benign Neoplasm of the Pharynx - Benign Pharyngeal Tumor - Benign Pharynx Neoplasm - Benign Pharynx Tumor - Benign Tumor of Pharynx - Benign Tumor of the Pharynx - Pharyngeal Neoplasm Benign
[C35970] Vascular Staghorn Configuration -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding in which the blood vessel walls in a specimen appear thinned and branched in an antler-like fashion.
[C35971] Angiectoid Space Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating abnormal, pseudovascular channels lined by cells.
[C35972] Small Capillary-Sized Vascular Proliferation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of newly formed small capillaries.
[C35973] Irregular Vascular Channel Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating abnormal development of a vascular passage.
[C35974] Sinusoidal Vascular Channel Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of newly formed, large diameter, low pressure vascular passages.
[C35975] Arborizing Vascular Channel Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating a tree-like pattern of capillary growth.
[C35976] Vascular Channel Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of newly formed vascular passages.
[C35977] Lobular Capillary Arrangement -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating clusters of endothelial cells within a fibrous stroma.
[C35978] Inflammatory Infiltrate -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A microscopic finding indicating the presence of acute, subacute or chronic inflammation in a tissue sample.
[C35979] Acute Inflammatory Infiltrate -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the presence of neutrophils and/or eosinophils in a tissue sample.
[C3598] Benign Esophageal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the esophageal wall and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Esophageal Tumor - Benign Esophagus Neoplasm - Benign Esophagus Tumor - Benign Neoplasm of Esophagus - Benign Neoplasm of the Esophagus - Benign Tumor of Esophagus - Benign Tumor of the Esophagus
[C35980] Chronic Inflammatory Infiltrate -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the presence of reactive lymphocytes, lymphoplasmacytic cells, and/or reactive plasma cells in a tissue sample.
[C35981] Eosinophilic Infiltrate -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An inflammatory infiltrate composed exclusively or predominantly of eosinophils.
[C35982] Polymorphonuclear Cell Infiltrate -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding referring to an acute inflammatory infiltrate that is composed of polymorphonuclear cells.  **Synonyms:** - Neutrophilic Infiltrate
[C35983] Lymphocytic Infiltrate -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding referring to the presence of a cellular infiltrate that is composed of non-neoplastic or neoplastic lymphocytes in a tissue sample.  **Synonyms:** - Lymphocytic infiltration
[C35984] Lymphoplasmacytic Infiltrate -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A finding referring to the presence of a cellular infiltrate that is composed of lymphocytes and plasma cells in a tissue sample.  **Synonyms:** - LPI
[C35985] Hemophagocytosis -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The term hemophagocytosis describes the pathologic finding of activated macrophages, engulfing erythrocytes, leukocytes, platelets, and their precursor cells. (from Medscape: Hemophagocytic Syndromes and Infection)
[C35986] Onion-Skinning Appearance -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding in which the cells form a pattern of concentric layers similar to that of onion skin.
[C35987] Emperipolesis -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding of an intact cell engulfed within the cytoplasm of another cell.  **Synonyms:** - EMPERIPOLESIS
[C35988] Metaplastic Bone Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of newly formed heterotopic bone.
[C35989] Nuclear Atypia -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of nuclear variations in color, size, shape, and chromatin structure and content.  **Synonyms:** - Nuclear Atypia Present
[C3599] Benign Gastric Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the stomach and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Gastric Tumor - Benign Gastric Tumors - Benign Neoplasm of Stomach - Benign Neoplasm of the Stomach - Benign Stomach Neoplasms - Benign Stomach Tumors - Benign Tumor of Stomach - Benign Tumor of the Stomach - Benign Tumors of Stomach - Benign Tumors of the Stomach - Gastric Neoplasms, Benign - Gastric Tumors, Benign - Stomach Neoplasms, Benign - Stomach Tumors, Benign
[C35990] Hemosiderin Deposition -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Accumulation of hemosiderin within tissue.
[C35991] Red Blood Cells Extravasation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of erythrocytes outside of blood vessels.
[C35992] Dystrophic Calcification -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Calcium deposits in  degenerated or necrotic tissue without abnormalities in blood calcium.
[C35993] Neoplastic Bone Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of newly formed neoplastic bone.
[C35994] Pseudoangiomatoid Space Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of newly formed stromal cells clustering together to form capillary-like spaces. This is a characteristic finding in pseudoangiomatous stromal hyperplasia (PASH).
[C35995] Ring Sideroblasts Present -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** A finding indicating that ringed sideroblasts were noted in a sample.  **Synonyms:** - Ringed Sideroblasts Present
[C35996] Stromal Change -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An alteration in the architecture of stromal cells.
[C35997] Vascular Collagenous Stroma Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of newly formed collagenous stromal cells surrounding vascular tissue.
[C35998] Myxoid Stroma Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding that indicates the presence of myxoid changes in the stroma of a tissue sample.
[C35999] Mucoid Stroma Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of newly formed hyalinized, mucoid stromal cells. This is a characteristic finding in clear cell carcinomas.
[C360] Chelating Agent -- **Semantic type:** Chemical Viewed Functionally  **Definition:** An inorganic or organic molecule which binds metal ions. Metal chelators are frequently polydentate, indicating that they form multiple bonds with the metal ion, resulting in more stable coordination complexes.  **Synonyms:** - Chelating agent - Chelators - Metal Chelant - Metal Chelating Agent - Metal Chelator - chelating agent
[C3600] Benign Small Intestinal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the small intestine and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Neoplasm of Small Intestine - Benign Neoplasm of the Small Intestine - Benign Small Intestinal Neoplasm NOS - Benign Small Intestinal Tumor - Benign Small Intestine Neoplasm - Benign Small Intestine Tumor - Benign Tumor of Small Intestine - Benign Tumor of the Small Intestine
[C36000] Hyalinized Stroma Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Replacement of tissue stromal elements by hyaline material.
[C36001] Fibromyxoid Stroma Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the development of stromal tissue characterized by a predominance of collagen fibers, myxoid matrix, and fibroblasts.
[C36002] Chondromyxoid Stroma Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the presence of cartilaginous and gel-like, mucoid stromal changes in a tissue sample.
[C36003] Desmoplastic Stroma Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the development of dense connective tissue around tumor cells.
[C36004] Fibroblastic Stroma Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the development of stromal tissue characterized by a predominance of fibroblasts.
[C36005] Edematous Stroma Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the accumulation of fluid in the supporting tissue of an organ or gland.
[C36006] Stromal Hemorrhage Present -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of extravasated blood among the stromal cells.
[C36007] Cast Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The precipitation and coagulation of Tamm-Horsfall mucoprotein in the distal convoluted tubules or collecting ducts of the kidney, with or without the inclusion of other components.
[C36008] Erythrocytic Cast Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The precipitation and coagulation of Tamm-Horsfall mucoprotein in the distal convoluted tubules or collecting ducts of the kidney, entrapping intrarenal erythrocytes.
[C36009] Leukocytic Cast Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the development of urinary casts made up of white blood cells.
[C3601] Benign Laryngeal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the larynx and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Laryngeal Tumor - Benign Larynx Neoplasm - Benign Larynx Tumor - Benign Neoplasm of Larynx - Benign Neoplasm of the Larynx - Benign Tumor of Larynx - Benign Tumor of the Larynx - Laryngeal Neoplasm, Benign
[C36010] Central Scar -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An imaging finding that may indicate a mass of unknown etiology in the kidney, liver, or pancreas.
[C36011] Decidual Reaction -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** The cellular and vascular changes occurring in the endometrium at the time of implantation.
[C36012] Nodular Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm characterized by the formation of nodules by the proliferating neoplastic cells.  **Synonyms:** - Nodular Tumor
[C36013] Bead-Like Arrangement of Elastic Fibers -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the structure of elastic fibers, which is characterized by a network of microfibrils surrounding the elastin core.
[C36014] Russell Body -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An eosinophilic, intracytoplasmic inclusion seen in the endoplasmic reticulum of mature plasma cells. They are representative of accumulations of immunoglobulins that are not secreted from the plasma cells.
[C36015] Asbestos Body -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A ferruginous body with a thin, clear fibrous core coated by  iron, protein, and mucopolysaccharide containing material that form clubbed ends.  **Synonyms:** - Asbestos bodies
[C36016] Michaelis-Gutmann Body -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A round basophilic inclusion body that contains calcium and iron. It is found in the bladder wall of patients with malakoplakia.
[C36017] Nuclear Inclusion -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A microscopic finding indicating an abnormal collection of material within a cellular nucleus.
[C36018] Ground Glass Nuclear Inclusion -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A microscopic finding indicating the presence of nuclei with ground glass appearance due to the presence of intranuclear viral particles that cause peripheral margination of the chromatin.
[C36019] Cowdry Type A Eosinophilic Intranuclear Inclusion -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding of a mass within a cell nucleus that is characterized by an eosinophilic center and marginated chromatin surrounded by clear empty space.
[C3602] Benign Tracheal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the trachea and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Neoplasm of Trachea - Benign Neoplasm of the Trachea - Benign Trachea Neoplasm - Benign Trachea Tumor - Benign Tracheal Tumor - Benign Tumor of Trachea - Benign Tumor of the Trachea
[C36020] Dutcher Body -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** Spherical cytoplasmic inclusions consisting of an abnormal accumulation of immunoglobin that invaginate into or overlie the nucleus.
[C36021] Corpora Amylacea -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding of polyglucosan aggregates accumulated in various tissues.  **Synonyms:** - Blue Body - CORPORA AMYLACEA - Concretion
[C36022] Verocay Bodies Present -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of two parallel rows of nuclear palisades separated by an anuclear zone. This is a characteristic finding in schwannomas.
[C36023] Call Exner Bodies Present -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the presence of granulosa cells arranged in rosettes around eosinophilic fluid filled spaces.
[C36024] Starry Sky Pattern -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding in which pale macrophages (stars) are found in the setting of basophilic monomorphic neoplastic cells (sky). This is a characteristic finding in hematolymphoid neoplasms, particularly Burkitt lymphoma.
[C36025] Anaplastic Malignant Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A malignant neoplasm characterized by the presence of poorly differentiated, often large neoplastic cells with bizarre morphologic characteristics, associated with high mitotic activity and necrotic changes.
[C36026] Diffuse Cellular Infiltrate -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating the presence of a cellular infiltrate with a diffuse arrangement.
[C36027] Non-Invasive Lesion -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A neoplastic process that is confined to the site of origin without morphologic evidence of cellular infiltration of the surrounding tissues.
[C36028] Microinvasive Malignant Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A malignant neoplasm that has minimal stromal invasion.
[C36029] Necrotic Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm characterized by focal or diffuse tumor cell necrosis.
[C3603] Benign Pleural Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the pleura and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Neoplasm of Pleura - Benign Neoplasm of the Pleura - Benign Pleural Tumor - Benign Tumor of Pleura - Benign Tumor of the Pleura
[C36030] Osteolytic Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that involves bone destruction.
[C36031] Papillary Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign, intermediate, or malignant neoplasm characterized by the formation of papillary structures.  **Synonyms:** - Papillary Tumor - papillary tumor
[C36032] Premalignant Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that is composed of dysplastic cells and there is no morphologic evidence of infiltration of the surrounding tissues.  **Synonyms:** - Premalignant
[C36033] Tubular Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant neoplasm characterized by the presence of a tubular architecture. This category includes tubular adenomas and tubular carcinomas.
[C36034] Villous Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process

[C36036] Non-Encapsulated Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that is non-capsulated.
[C36037] Localized Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that is confined to a specific site without evidence of spread to other anatomic sites.  **Synonyms:** - Non-Metastatic Neoplasm - Non-metastatic Disease - tumor confined to organ of origin - tumor_confined_to_organ_of_origin
[C36038] Non-Lytic Metastasis -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A radiologic finding indicating the presence of a metastatic tumor in the bone(s) without associated bone destruction.  **Synonyms:** - Non-Lytic Metastatic Lesion - Nonlytic Metastatic Lesion - Sclerotic Metastasis
[C36039] Grade 1 Malignant Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A malignant neoplasm with well differentiated morphological features.  **Synonyms:** - Low Grade Malignant Neoplasm - Well Differentiated Malignant Neoplasm

[C36048] Low Grade Malignant Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process
[C36049] Moderately Differentiated Malignant Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm whose histologic characteristics are intermediate between poorly differentiated and well differentiated.
[C3605] Benign Cardiac Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the heart and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential. [C36050] Poorly Differentiated Malignant Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm whose histologic characteristics have regressed and are more similar to stem cells.
[C36051] Undifferentiated Malignant Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that has minimal to no differentiating features.
[C36052] Well Differentiated Malignant Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A malignant neoplasm with morphological features reminiscent of the tissue from which it arose.  **Synonyms:** - Well-Differentiated Malignant Neoplasm
[C36053] Epithelial Predominant Pulmonary Blastoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A non-encapsulated, well defined pulmonary blastoma, composed of irregular tubular structures.  It affects mostly middle-aged adults and it is rare in children.  The prognosis is better compared to the biphasic pulmonary blastoma.
[C36054] Biphasic Pulmonary Blastoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A pulmonary blastoma composed of a mixture of irregular tubular structures and mesenchymal elements.  **Synonyms:** - Classic Pulmonary Blastoma
[C36055] Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia with a Variant RARA Rearrangement -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A term that refers to acute promyelocytic leukemias that show variant translocations involving the RARA gene. The variant fusion partners include NUMA1, ZBTB16, STAT5B, and NPM1.  **Synonyms:** - AML with Variant RARA Translocation - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with a Variant RARA Rearrangement - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with a Variant RARA Translocation - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with a Variant RARA Translocation - Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia with Other RARA Rearrangements

It lacks differentiation response to retinoids treatment.  **Synonyms:** - APL with t(11;17)(q23;q21); PLZF-RARA - APL with t(11;17)(q23;q21); ZBTB16-RARA - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(11;17)(q23;q21) - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(11;17)(q23;q21) - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(11;17)(q23;q21); ZBTB16-RARA - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(11;17)(q23;q21); ZBTB16-RARA Fusion - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(11;17)(q23;q21); ZBTB16::RARA - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(11;17)(q23;q21); ZBTB16::RARA Fusion - Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia with t(11;17)(q23;q21); PLZF-RARA - Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia with t(11;17)(q23;q21); PLZF-RARA - Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia with t(11;17)(q23;q21); ZBTB16::RARA
[C36057] Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia with t(5;17)(q35;q21); NPM1-RARA -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** Acute promyelocytic leukemia with a variant RARA rearrangement associated with t(5;17)(q35;q21) that results in the expression of NPM1-RARA fusion protein.  **Synonyms:** - AML with t(5;17)(q35;q21) - AML with t(5;17)(q35;q21);NPM1-RARA - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(5;17)(q35;q21) - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(5;17)(q35;q21) - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(5;17)(q35;q21); NPM1-RARA - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(5;17)(q35;q21); NPM1-RARA Fusion - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(5;17)(q35;q21); NPM1::RARA - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(5;17)(q35;q21); NPM1::RARA Fusion - Acute Myeloid Leukemia with t(5;17)(q35;q21);NPM1-RARA - Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia with t(5;17)(q35;q21); NPM1::RARA

[C36059] Granuloma-Like Growth Pattern -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding indicating an arrangement of immune cells in a cluster.
[C3606] Spermatic Cord Lipoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A lipoma that arises from the spermatic cord.  **Synonyms:** - Lipoma of Spermatic Cord - Lipoma of the Spermatic Cord
[C36060] Chronic Myelomonocytic Leukemia with Eosinophilia -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A chronic myelomonocytic leukemia characterized by a peripheral blood eosinophil count of equal or greater than 1.5x10E9/L, and absence of PDGFRA or PDGFRB gene abnormalities.  **Synonyms:** - CMML with Eosinophilia
[C36061] Chronic Myelomonocytic Leukemia-1 -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A chronic myelomonocytic leukemia characterized by the presence of less than 10 percent blasts in the bone marrow and less than 5 percent blasts in the peripheral blood.  **Synonyms:** - CMML-1
[C36062] Chronic Myelomonocytic Leukemia-2 -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A chronic myelomonocytic leukemia characterized by the presence of 10-19 percent blasts in the bone marrow and 5-19 percent blasts in the peripheral blood or by the presence of Auer rods regardless of the blasts count.  **Synonyms:** - CMML-2

[C36065] Superior Vena Cava Occlusion -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Blockage of the lumen of the superior vena cava.
[C36066] Orbivirus Infection -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An infection that is caused by Orbivirus.
[C36067] Clostridium Colitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An acute inflammatory disorder of the colon secondary to infection with any species of the bacterial genus Clostridium. Symptoms include acute diarrhea, abdominal pain, and myalgia.
[C36068] Posteroinferior Myocardial Infarction by ECG Finding -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An electrocardiographic finding of an injury in leads corresponding to the anatomic region of the posteroinferior wall of the heart.  **Synonyms:** - Posteroinferior Myocardial Infarction by EKG Finding
[C36069] Dental and Periodontal Infection -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An infectious process that affects the teeth and/or the periodontal tissues.
[C3607] Benign Cervical Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the cervix and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential. Representative examples include squamous papilloma, endocervical polyp, and rhabdomyoma.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Cervix Uteri Neoplasm - Benign Cervix Uteri Neoplasm NOS - Benign Cervix Uteri Tumor - Benign Neoplasm of Cervix Uteri - Benign Neoplasm of Uterine Cervix - Benign Neoplasm of the Cervix Uteri - Benign Neoplasm of the Uterine Cervix - Benign Tumor of Cervix Uteri - Benign Tumor of Uterine Cervix - Benign Tumor of the Cervix Uteri - Benign Tumor of the Uterine Cervix - Benign Uterine Cervix Neoplasm - Benign Uterine Cervix Tumor
[C36070] Abdominal and Gastrointestinal Infection -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An infection that affects the gastrointestinal system and the abdomen.
[C36071] Chronic Polyneuropathy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Polyneuropathy that is persistent or long-standing in nature.
[C36072] Abnormal Phenolsulfonphthalein Test Result -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** A laboratory test result indicating other than normal parameters after a phenolsulfonphthaliein test.  **Synonyms:** - Abnormal PSP Test
[C36073] Apical Myocardial Infarction by ECG Finding -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** An electrocardiographic finding of an injury in leads corresponding to the anatomic region of the apex of the heart.  **Synonyms:** - Apical Myocardial Infarction by EKG Finding
[C36074] Metastatic Peripheral Primitive Neuroectodermal Tumor of Bone -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A small round cell tumor of the bone with neural differentiation that has spread from its original site of growth to another anatomic site.  **Synonyms:** - Metastatic Peripheral Neuroepithelioma of Bone
[C36075] Rosai-Dorfman-Destombes Disease -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A rare histiocytic disorder of unknown etiology, characterized by distention of the lymph node sinuses and sinusoidal histiocytic infiltration. The histiocytes characteristically contain ingested lymphocytes and are S100 and CD68 positive, and CD1a negative. Patients present with cervical lymphadenopathy, fever, leukocytosis, and hypergammaglobulinemia. It can affect extranodal sites, including skin, bones, and the respiratory tract. It occurs in isolation or may be associated with autoimmune disorders or malignancies. Mutations in NRAS, KRAS, MAP2K1, and ARAF genes have been identified in affected tissues, suggesting a clonal origin in some cases. It is a self-limited disorder, although some patients may have a poor prognosis.  **Synonyms:** - Destombes-Rosai-Dorfman Disease - RDD - Rosai-Dorfman Disease - SHML - Sinus Histiocytosis with Massive Lymphadenopathy
[C36076] Malignant Reproductive System Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the male or female reproductive system.  Representative examples include prostate carcinoma, penile carcinoma, testicular seminoma, testicular embryonal carcinoma, endometrial carcinoma, cervical carcinoma, ovarian carcinoma, and uterine corpus leiomyosarcoma.
[C36077] Hilar Cholangiocarcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A carcinoma that arises from the junction, or adjacent to the junction, of the right and left hepatic ducts.  **Synonyms:** - Hilar CC - Hilar CCA - Klatskin Tumor - Klatskin tumor - Klatskin tumor - Perihilar CC - Perihilar CCA - Perihilar Cholangiocarcinoma - hCC - hCCA - pCC - pCCA
[C36078] Sideroblastic Anemia -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Anemia characterized by the production of ringed sideroblasts instead of healthy red blood cells.
[C36079] Subungual Glomus Tumor -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A glomus tumor that arises from the subungual space of the digits. It is usually associated with pain.
[C3608] Benign Uterine Corpus Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the uterine corpus and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential. Representative examples include leiomyoma, adenomyoma, endometrial stromal nodule, and endometrial polyp.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Corpus Uteri Neoplasm - Benign Corpus Uteri Tumor - Benign Neoplasm of Body of Uterus - Benign Neoplasm of Corpus Uteri - Benign Neoplasm of Uterine Body - Benign Neoplasm of Uterine Corpus - Benign Neoplasm of the Body of Uterus - Benign Neoplasm of the Corpus Uteri - Benign Neoplasm of the Uterine Body - Benign Neoplasm of the Uterine Corpus - Benign Tumor of Body of Uterus - Benign Tumor of Corpus Uteri - Benign Tumor of Uterine Body - Benign Tumor of Uterine Corpus - Benign Tumor of the Body of Uterus - Benign Tumor of the Corpus Uteri - Benign Tumor of the Uterine Body - Benign Tumor of the Uterine Corpus - Benign Uterine Body Neoplasm - Benign Uterine Body Tumor - Benign Uterine Corpus Tumor
[C36080] Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma Germinal Center B-Cell Type -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A biologic subset of diffuse large B-cell lymphomas with a unique molecular signature or expression profile.  It represents approximately 50% of diffuse large B-cell lymphomas, and is characterized by the expression of CD10, BCL-6, A-myb, and LMO2 genes, BCL-2 translocation, and c-REL amplification.  Morphologically, the vast majority of these lymphomas are centroblastic and a small minority are immunoblastic (ratio20:1).  Patients with this type of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma have a more favorable outcome, with a 5-year survival rate of 60% and a median survival of 10 years.  **Synonyms:** - DLBCL Germinal Center B-Cell Type - Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma with a Germinal Center B-Cell Expression Profile - GCB-DLBCL - Germinal Center B-Cell-Like Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma
[C36081] Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma Activated B-Cell Type -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A biologic subset of diffuse large B-cell lymphomas with a unique molecular signature or expression profile.  It represents approximately 30% of diffuse large B-cell lymphomas, and is characterized by the expression of CD44, PKCbeta1, Cyclin D2, BCL-2, and IRF4/MUM1 genes.  Morphologically, these lymphomas are either centroblastic or immunoblastic (ratio 2:1).  Patients with this type of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma are reported to have a less favorable outcome compared to those with a germinal center B-cell expression profile, with a 5-year survival rate of 35% and a median survival of 2 years.  **Synonyms:** - ABC DLBCL - ABC-DLBCL - Activated B-Cell-Like Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma - DLBCL Activated B-Cell Type - Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma Non-GC/ABC - Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma Non-Germinal Center/Activated B-Cell Type - Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma with an Activated B-Cell Expression Profile - Non-GC/ABC DLBCL
[C36082] Metastatic Carcinoma in the Bone -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A carcinoma that has spread to the bone from another anatomic site. Bone is one of the most frequent sites of metastatic carcinoma. Common sites of origin include lung, breast, and prostate.  **Synonyms:** - Metastatic Carcinoma to the Bone
[C36083] Breast Intraductal Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant epithelial neoplasm that arises anywhere in the ductal system of the breast.  This category includes intraductal papilloma, intraductal papillary carcinoma, ductal hyperplasia with or without atypia, and ductal carcinoma in situ.  **Synonyms:** - Intraductal Breast Neoplasm
[C36084] Invasive Breast Micropapillary Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An invasive breast carcinoma characterized by the presence of clusters of malignant cells located within artifactual clear stromal spaces that resemble vascular spaces.  **Synonyms:** - Infiltrating Micropapillary Breast Carcinoma - Invasive Micropapillary Breast Carcinoma
[C36085] Invasive Breast Papillary Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An invasive breast carcinoma characterized by the presence of papillary structures.  **Synonyms:** - Infiltrating Papillary Breast Carcinoma - Invasive Papillary Breast Carcinoma
[C36086] Breast Flat Ductal Epithelial Atypia -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A breast lesion characterized by the presence of dilated terminal ductal lobular units in which the epithelial lining has been replaced by a single layer of mildly atypical cells, or there is atypical, monotonous epithelial hyperplasia of three to five layers.  This lesion relates to columnar cell change with atypia and columnar cell hyperplasia with atypia.  **Synonyms:** - Clinging Carcinoma - Clinging Carcinoma In Situ - DIN 1A - Ductal Intraepithelial Neoplasia, Grade 1A - FEA - Flat Ductal Epithelial Atypia - Flat Ductal Epithelial Atypia of the Breast - Flat Epithelial Atypia - Flat Epithelial Atypia of the Breast
[C36087] Breast Central Papilloma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign papillary neoplasm that arises in a large duct of the breast. It is characterized by the presence of a fibrovascular core that is lined by benign epithelial and myoepithelial proliferations. Patients usually present with nipple discharge.  **Synonyms:** - Central Breast Papilloma - Large Duct Breast Papilloma - Solitary Intraductal Breast Papilloma
[C36088] Breast Peripheral Papilloma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign papillary neoplasm that arises in a terminal ductal lobular unit. It is characterized by the presence of a fibrovascular core that is lined by benign epithelial and myoepithelial proliferations. Peripheral breast papillomas are often multiple and are usually found microscopically. Patients are often asymptomatic.  **Synonyms:** - Microscopic Breast Papilloma - Peripheral Breast Papilloma
[C36089] Breast Atypical Papilloma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** An intraductal papilloma of the breast characterized by the presence of focal epithelial atypia.  **Synonyms:** - Atypical Breast Papilloma - Atypical Breast Papilloma - Intraductal Breast Papilloma with Atypia
[C3609] Benign Uterine Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplasm that arises from the uterine corpus or cervix and is characterized by the absence of atypical or malignant cytological and architectural features, and absence of invasive features or metastatic potential. Representative examples include leiomyoma, adenomyoma, and endocervical polyp.  **Synonyms:** - Benign Neoplasm of Uterus - Benign Neoplasm of the Uterus - Benign Tumor of Uterus - Benign Tumor of the Uterus - Benign Uterine Neoplasm NOS - Benign Uterine Neoplasms - Benign Uterine Tumor - Benign Uterine Tumors - Benign Uterus Neoplasm - Benign Uterus Neoplasms - Benign Uterus Tumor - Benign Uterus Tumors - Uterine Neoplasms, Benign - Uterine Tumors, Benign - Uterous Neoplasms, Benign - Uterous Tumors, Benign
[C36090] Breast Intraductal Papillary Neoplasm -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign or malignant papillary neoplasm that arises anywhere in the ductal system of the breast. It is characterized by the presence of fibrovascular structures lined by epithelial proliferations. This category includes intraductal papilloma and papillary ductal carcinoma in situ.  **Synonyms:** - Intraductal Papillary Breast Neoplasm
[C36091] Reinke Crystals Formation -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphological finding indicating the presence of newly formed crystalline inclusions in Leydig cells.
[C36092] Testicular Dermoid Cyst -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A mature teratoma that arises from the testis and is characterized by the presence of one or more cysts. [C36094] Microinvasive Cervical Squamous Cell Carcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A cervical squamous cell carcinoma with minimal stromal invasion. The risk of lymph node metastasis is low.  **Synonyms:** - Early Invasive Cervical Squamous Cell Carcinoma
[C36095] Cervical Mucinous Adenocarcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A mucinous adenocarcinoma arising from the endocervical glandular epithelium. It is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular cells that contain significant amount of intracytoplasmic mucin. Histologic variants include intestinal-type, signet ring-type, gastric-type, cervical invasive stratified mucinous adenocarcinoma, and mucinous adenocarcinoma, not otherwise specified. All histologic variants except gastric-type adenocarcinoma are associated with human papillomavirus infection.

The risk of local lymph node metastasis is insignificant and the prognosis is excellent.  **Synonyms:** - Cervical Microinvasive Adenocarcinoma

[C36098] Childhood Vulvar Botryoid-Type Embryonal Rhabdomyosarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A morphologic variant of embryonal rhabdomyosarcoma occurring in female children.  The neoplasm arises from the vulva.  It is characterized by the formation of a cambium layer in the affected tissue and polypoid nodules with an abundant myxoid stroma.  **Synonyms:** - Childhood Botryoid-Type Embryonal Rhabdomyosarcoma of the Vulva - Childhood Sarcoma Botryoides of the Vulva - Childhood Vulvar Embryonal Rhabdomyosarcoma - Vulvar Childhood Botryoid-Type Embryonal Rhabdomyosarcoma
[C36099] Adult Botryoid-Type Embryonal Rhabdomyosarcoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A morphologic variant of embryonal rhabdomyosarcoma occurring in adults.  The neoplasm arises from organs containing a mucosal epithelial surface.  It is characterized by the formation of a cambium layer in the affected tissue and polypoid nodules with an abundant myxoid stroma.  **Synonyms:** - Adult Botryoid Sarcoma
[C361] Chewing Tobacco -- **Semantic type:** Hazardous or Poisonous Substance  **Definition:** Tobacco, in the form of a plug, usually flavored, for chewing rather than smoking.  **Synonyms:** - Spit Tobacco

[C36100] BRCA1-Related Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An autosomal dominant inherited syndrome caused by deleterious mutations in the BRCA1 gene. Affected individuals are at high risk of developing breast cancer, ovarian cancer, and other cancers including cervical, endometrial, fallopian tube, and colon cancer.  **Synonyms:** - BRCA1 Syndrome - BRCA1 Syndrome - BRCA1 Syndrome - BRCA1-Associated Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome - BROVCA1 - Early Onset Breast Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
[C36101] BRCA2-Related Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An autosomal dominant inherited syndrome caused by deleterious mutations in the BRCA2 gene. [C36118] In Situ Lesion -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A morphologic finding that usually refers either to intraepithelial dysplastic and architectural changes that involve the entire thickness of the epithelium without invasion of the basement membrane (carcinoma in situ) or proliferation of atypical melanocytes in the dermal-epidermal junction without infiltration of the papillary or reticular dermis (lentigo maligna).  **Synonyms:** - In Situ Neoplasm
